note: Independent research compiled from official sources; verify dates, prices and availability with each provider. Confidence field reflects verification depth.
known_gaps: SPORTS is a near-empty category despite being in the controlled vocab: no dedicated soccer, basketball, baseball, tennis, golf, fencing, ice-skating/hockey, climbing, rowing, or gymnastics camps beyond generic rec-department listings; pro-team camps (DC United, Capitals hockey school, Wizards/Mystics, Nationals) are entirely absent.
Washington Nationals Youth Baseball Academy (free, Ward 7, one of DC's most famous youth institutions) is missing — emblematic of a broader east-of-the-river hole.

Wards 7/8 and SE/NE DC equity programs are systematically missing: Fort Dupont Ice Arena Kids on Ice (low-cost, historic), Life Pieces to Masterpieces, Latin American Youth Center, Higher Achievement, DC SCORES, Washington Tennis & Education Foundation, CityKids Wilderness Project, For Love of Children (FLOC), Camp Moss Hollow (free overnight), THEARC campus programming.
Kennedy Center ecosystem absent: WNO Opera Institute, NSO Summer Music Institute (free, prestigious), and Kennedy Center teen intensives — among the region's flagship selective arts programs.
Duke Ellington School of the Arts summer programs (famous DC arts institution) absent; National Gallery of Art teen programs also absent.
Smithsonian National Zoo has no entry — camp status post-FONZ-dissolution needs verification and an explicit 'no camp' note (the census already uses this pattern for Planet Word).

Under-6/preschool coverage is nearly nil beyond DPR Little Explorers — no co-op/church preschool summer programs (a huge DC/MoCo sector), no Waldorf (Acorn Hill), no toddler franchise camps; most listed camps start at 5-6, leaving newcomers with 2-4 year olds stranded.
Faith-based coverage is Jewish-only (JCCs, Capital Camps, Airy & Louise): no Christian/Catholic day camps, VBS landscape, CYO, Camp St. Charles, or Muslim community camps (e.g. ADAMS Center).

Regionally famous NoVA fills missing: Burgundy Farm Summer Day Camp (60+ years, one of the area's most beloved) and its Burgundy Center for Wildlife Studies overnight camp, Signature Theatre camps, Baroody Camps, Arcadia Farm Camp.
Close-in equestrian misses: Meadowbrook Stables (Chevy Chase) and Potomac Horse Center — both major, both closer than some listed options.
Montgomery Parks ice rinks (Cabin John, Wheaton) run summer skating camps — a MoCo staple missing even though both facilities' neighborhoods are in scope.
Medical/condition-specific special-needs camps absent: Camp Fantastic (NIH, childhood cancer), Brainy Camps (Children's National, chronic conditions), Lions Camp Merrick (diabetes).

Teen (15-17) practical pathways thin beyond arts CITs: lifeguard-certification pipelines (DPR/YMCA/MoCo Rec — a classic paid-teen-summer route), U.S. Senate Page summer session, NIH HiSTEP (free HS internship in Bethesda), American Legion Boys/Girls State, paid junior-counselor roles beyond Headfirst.
Newcomer-explainer gap: Montgomery County Swim League and neighborhood-pool-membership culture — the default MoCo summer institution — has no entry.
Big MoCo camp operators missing: Kids After Hours (major aftercare provider running large summer camps) and Silver Stars Gymnastics (Silver Spring).
Latino/immigrant-family programs in MoCo/DC missing: Identity Inc, CentroNia, Mary's Center, AALEAD — key for a newcomer guide's free/low-cost coverage.
Language coverage misses Alliance Francaise de Washington camps and any Japanese, Korean, or Italian options.
Rowing/crew and boathouse multi-activity camps (e.g. Thompson Boat Center) missing despite otherwise good sailing/paddling coverage.

Small distinctive misses: Jones-Haywood Dance School (historic Black ballet school) summer program, Labyrinth Games & Puzzles game camps (Capitol Hill), Trapeze School DC, Star Gazing Farm (Boyds animal-sanctuary camp), Hoop Ed (DC basketball day-camp institution).

Census hygiene: several duplicate entries need merging rather than new research (MBSYEP x2, CHAW x2, WUDL/Ornstein Debate x2, YMCA Camp Letts x2, Learn24 x2, DPR Therapeutic Recreation x2, Lessans Camp JCC x2, Girls Who Code x2).

providers.name: 826DC Summer Writing Workshops
providers.description: Free creative-writing workshops, tutoring, and bookmaking for DC students ages 6-18, run out of the whimsical 'Museum of Unnatural History' storefront in Columbia Heights. Summer offerings are episodic multi-day workshops and drop-in style sessions rather than a 9-to-5 camp — kids leave as published authors with bound books from the in-house bindery. A gem for families who can't or don't want to pay camp prices.
providers.ages: 6-18 (workshops grouped by age band)
providers.locations: 3233 14th St NW, Columbia Heights, Washington DC (Museum of Unnatural History storefront)
providers.price_detail: All programs free for DC students
providers.financial_aid: Everything is free; that is the organization's model
providers.status_2026: Summer workshop slate posts on their programs page; individual sessions fill fast because they're free — check 826dc.org for what's still open in late July/August

reg_2027.opens: Seasonally — summer workshops typically announced late spring
reg_2027.notes: Sign-ups open online per-workshop and fill quickly since everything is free. Not a full-day childcare solution — treat it as enrichment sessions, not camp coverage. Join their mailing list to catch announcements.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting real literary mentorship at zero cost; NOT a full-day camp, so it won't solve summer childcare. Priority energy goes to students from under-resourced DC schools.

providers.name: AALEAD Summer Program
providers.description: Free summer programming for low-income and underserved Asian American Pacific Islander youth in DC and Montgomery County, mixing leadership development, identity exploration, field trips, community service, and college/career prep. AALEAD is the region's main youth organization built specifically around AAPI identity, with staff who navigate language and cultural barriers for immigrant families.
providers.ages: Elementary through high school (programs grouped by grade band; MoCo and DC sites differ)
providers.price_detail: Free; focused on low-income AAPI youth
providers.locations: DC (Ward 1/4 school sites and office) and Montgomery County (Silver Spring area schools); some Fairfax VA programming
providers.hours: Varies by site; day-program hours in summer
providers.financial_aid: Program is free; no aid application needed

reg_2027.opens: Spring; interest forms via aalead.org and school-based recruitment
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment is by simple application/registration with priority for low-income AAPI youth already in AALEAD's school-year programs. Contact program staff in March-April 2027 for summer seats.

providers.sessions_2026: Multi-week summer sessions with field trips, service, and college visits, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer in progress for registered youth; inquire directly about openings
providers.fit_notes: The go-to free summer option for AAPI immigrant families in DC and Silver Spring — culturally specific mentorship you won't find at a generic camp. Youth of other backgrounds are generally referred elsewhere.

providers.name: Acorn Hill Summer Program
providers.description: A gentle, screen-free Waldorf summer program where the day is built around free, child-led play outdoors in a wooded playground beside Sligo Creek Park, plus water play, storytelling, singing, crafts, and gardening. This is the classic soft-landing camp for a preschooler who isn't ready for a big, loud rec camp — rhythmic, small (max 15 per class), and run by Waldorf-trained teachers.
providers.ages: 3.5-6.25 (as of June 1; toilet-trained required)
providers.locations: 9504 Brunett Ave, Silver Spring MD 20901 (adjacent to Sligo Creek Park)
providers.hours: Core morning program; Early Birds add-on 8:30-9:00am, Owls Nest aftercare 3:00-5:30pm for full-day registrants
providers.price_detail: Session-based tuition (multi-week sessions); payment in full at registration, non-refundable. Contact school for current rates.
providers.financial_aid: Financial aid exists for the school year (acornhill.org/admissions/tuition); summer aid not advertised — ask directly
providers.sessions_2026: Multi-week summer sessions June-July; health/permission forms were due March 14
providers.status_2026: Season underway mid-July; enrollment effectively closed for 2026 — contact school about any late openings

reg_2027.opens: winter (roughly January-February); priority typically goes to enrolled Acorn Hill families
reg_2027.notes: Small capacity (15/class) means community families should inquire in early winter; forms deadline falls in mid-March. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: Early Birds before-care and Owls Nest aftercare (to 5:30pm) as paid add-ons
providers.fit_notes: Ideal for 3-5s who thrive on rhythm, nature, and low stimulation; not for families wanting academics, screens, or specialty activities. One of the few true under-5 camps in Silver Spring.

providers.name: Action Youth Media Summer Program (formerly Gandhi Brigade)
providers.description: A free youth media nonprofit in downtown Silver Spring (founded 2008 as Gandhi Brigade) where teens learn filmmaking, podcasting, photography, and storytelling from pre- through post-production, including the DOCS summer program producing a real documentary on a community issue. Completely free, and participants can earn MCPS Student Service Learning hours.
providers.ages: teens (middle and high school)
providers.locations: 900 Wayne Ave, Level B/Media Arts, Silver Spring MD (downtown Silver Spring)
providers.price_detail: All programs free
providers.hours: Varies by program
providers.sessions_2026: Free summer media classes; DOCS documentary program for continuing participants
providers.status_2026: 2026 applications were reviewed rolling from Feb 1 with an April 30 deadline (selections notified May 10) — application window closed for this summer

reg_2027.opens: February 1 (rolling application through April 30; decisions by May 10)
reg_2027.notes: Apply early in the rolling window — it's free with limited seats, so don't wait for the April deadline. Apply via youthmediamd.org / their Linktree (linktr.ee/actionyouthmedia).

providers.fit_notes: Outstanding zero-cost option for Silver Spring-area teens interested in media, social justice storytelling, or SSL hours. It's an application-based youth program, not drop-in childcare, and it skews toward committed participants.

providers.name: ActiveMONTGOMERY Registration System (county camps master key)
providers.org: Montgomery County Recreation + Montgomery Parks (shared platform)
providers.description: Not a camp but the single registration gateway for ALL Montgomery County Recreation and Montgomery Parks camps — one account covers Summer Fun Centers, every nature center, archaeology, arts, sports and TR camps. Newcomers should create an account and add each child as a family member in early January, because on the third-Tuesday-of-January opening morning at 6:30am, seconds matter for the popular camps.
providers.ages: n/a (registration platform for camps ages 3-21)
providers.locations: Online; in-person registration help at 2425 Reedie Dr, 10th Floor, Wheaton (opens 6:30am on camp registration day); phone 240-777-6840
providers.hours: 24/7 online
providers.price_detail: Non-county residents pay +$15 per participant per activity (Recreation); full payment due at registration; VISA/MC/Amex/Discover accepted
providers.financial_aid: RecAssist ($400/person for county residents on eligible public assistance, first-come until fund depleted) applies at checkout; Parks camps take the Dream Camp scholarship

reg_2027.opens: camp registration mid-to-late January 2027 at 6:30am (2026: Jan 20; 2025: Jan 21 — third Tuesday pattern); non-camp summer programs open separately in mid-May
reg_2027.notes: The three moves that matter: (1) create/verify your account and children's profiles in early January, (2) shortlist camps from the GUIDEs (posted early January) with backups, (3) log in before 6:30am on opening day. All season long, the search filter for activities with openings shows real-time availability — the best tool for late planners and waitlist hunters.

providers.status_2026: Operating; live availability filter is the authoritative source for what's still open for late July-August 2026.
providers.fit_notes: Read this entry first if you're new to the county — every county-run camp above flows through it. City camps (Rockville, Gaithersburg, Takoma Park) use their own separate systems and calendars.

providers.name: ADAMS Sully Summer Camp
providers.org: All Dulles Area Muslim Society (ADAMS Center)
providers.description: The region's largest mosque community runs a K-6 summer day camp at its Sully branch combining Qur'an and Seerah study, character education, and fitness with weekly STEM, art, and cooking specialties — learning and play in a safe Islamic environment. It's the most established Muslim summer camp option in the DC metro, drawing families from across Northern Virginia and beyond.
providers.ages: Grades K-6
providers.locations: ADAMS Sully branch (Chantilly/Sully area); main center in Sterling VA
providers.price_detail: Per-4-day-session pricing; see adamscenter.org for current rates
providers.financial_aid: Ask center; ADAMS runs need-based assistance across programs
providers.sessions_2026: 4-day weeks: June 29-July 2, July 6-9, July 13-16, July 20-23, July 27-30
providers.status_2026: In session; the July 20-23 and July 27-30 weeks were the remaining 2026 sessions as of mid-July — register via ADAMS site

reg_2027.opens: spring (typically April-May announcement via ADAMS education channels)
reg_2027.notes: Fills from the large ADAMS community; watch the center's education/youth pages rather than camp aggregators. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For Muslim families wanting faith-integrated summer weeks; 4-day weeks suit supplementing rather than full-time care. NoVA location — a drive from DC/MoCo.

providers.name: Adventure Theatre MTC Summer Camps (Elementary)
providers.description: Two-week, full-day musical-theatre camps for rising grades 1-6 inside historic Glen Echo Park, run by one of the region's oldest children's theatres. Kids rehearse daily in singing, dancing, and acting with working DC theatre professionals and end each session with an ensemble performance. The carousel-and-arcade park setting makes it feel like classic summer camp with a professional-theatre spine.
providers.ages: Rising grades 1-6 (roughly 6-12)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD (some offsite locations in some years)
providers.price_detail: 2026 tuition not published on pages we could verify; contact registrar (301-251-5766). Two-week full-day format.
providers.financial_aid: Not verified on accessible pages; ask registrar about scholarships
providers.hours: Full day, approx 9:00am-4:00pm Monday-Friday
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions between June 22 and August 14 (Session 1: June 22-July 2), each ending in a performance
providers.status_2026: Session(s) in late July-August upcoming as of mid-July; availability not verified — call the registrar for open spots

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January; not verified)
reg_2027.notes: A regional institution that fills its early sessions fast; register in winter. Glen Echo Park location means easy pairing with siblings in other park camps.

providers.fit_notes: Great for elementary kids who love to sing and perform; two-week commitment suits kids ready for a real rehearsal process rather than drop-in fun. Official site blocked direct fetch; verified via search-indexed official content.

providers.name: Alexandria Archaeology Summer Camp
providers.org: City of Alexandria - Alexandria Archaeology Museum
providers.description: Teens excavate a real archaeological site (2026: Shuter's Hill near the George Washington Masonic Memorial) alongside the City of Alexandria's professional archaeologists, learning real excavation, recording, and artifact-processing methods. This is the rare camp where kids do actual fieldwork that contributes to the city's archaeological record - regionally famous and worth the trip from DC/Maryland.
providers.locations: Alexandria Archaeology Museum (105 N Union St, Torpedo Factory) and Shuter's Hill dig site (101 Callahan Dr), Alexandria VA
providers.price_detail: $400 for the week (2026); scholarships available
providers.hours: 9 am-3:30 pm
providers.sessions_2026: One week: June 22-26, 2026

reg_2027.opens: Early-to-mid March (2026 registration opened March 10)
reg_2027.notes: Camper application plus paid reservation, processed strictly first-come with very limited spots (it is one week, one small crew) - register the day it opens. Friends of Alexandria Archaeology also lists family dig days for younger kids.

providers.status_2026: Season over - the single 2026 week (June 22-26) has already run. Plan for March 2027 registration.
providers.fit_notes: For teens genuinely interested in history/archaeology who don't mind dirt, heat, and methodical work; ages 13-15 only, so it neatly serves kids aging out of regular day camps.

providers.name: Alliance Française de Washington Summer Camps
providers.description: Seven weeks of French-immersion day camps (ages 5-8 and 9-12) at the French cultural institute, each week a new theme — cooking and jam-making, LEGO 'French theme park' engineering — plus a weekly field trip, running 9am-3:30pm. All levels welcome including total beginners and native francophones, making it the region's most accessible French summer immersion.
providers.ages: 5-12 (grouped 5-8 and 9-12)
providers.locations: Alliance Française de Washington, Kalorama/Dupont area NW DC
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:30pm
providers.price_detail: $625/week members, $680 non-members
providers.sessions_2026: Seven themed weeks, late June-early August
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; check francedc.org for space in remaining late-July weeks

reg_2027.opens: January 15 (2026 registration opened Jan 15 — expect the same)
reg_2027.notes: Francophone expat families book early; joining AFDC as a member saves $55/week and some years unlocks earlier access. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: Great for heritage-French kids and adventurous beginners alike; monolingual kids who hate language learning may find full-immersion weeks tiring.

providers.name: American University Pre-College (Discover the World of Communication — on hiatus 2026)
providers.description: AU's beloved Discover the World of Communication summer program (filmmaking, journalism, public speaking for high schoolers, designed around commuter students) is ON HIATUS for summer 2026 with a redesigned return hoped for 2027. Meanwhile AU still runs pre-college options including Community of Scholars (3-credit international-relations courses) and High School Summer Scholars.
providers.ages: High school students (Community of Scholars: rising juniors/seniors typically)
providers.locations: American University, 4400 Massachusetts Ave NW (Metro-accessible via Tenleytown)
providers.hours: Varies by program; DWC was structured for commuting students
providers.price_detail: Credit-bearing programs billed per program; not verified for 2026
providers.financial_aid: Not verified; some AU pre-college programs have offered scholarships
providers.status_2026: DWC not running in 2026 (official hiatus, redesign underway, targeting 2027 return); other AU pre-college programs ran per their own calendars

reg_2027.opens: watch american.edu/soc/discover from fall 2026 for the redesigned program; other AU pre-college applications typically open in winter
reg_2027.notes: DWC 2027 details unannounced — join AU SOC's mailing list. Community of Scholars requires an application with academic materials; deadlines typically in spring.

providers.fit_notes: When DWC returns it is one of the best media/communication programs for teens in the country and historically commuter-friendly. For 2026, teens seeking media should look at GW's communication offerings instead.

providers.name: AoPS Academy Summer Math Camps (Gaithersburg + Virtual)
providers.description: Two-week or four-week half-day 'Math Beasts' camps and contest-prep intensives (AMC/MATHCOUNTS) from the country's leading advanced-math brand, for genuinely math-hungry students. The area campus is Gaithersburg (not Bethesda — the campus list shows Gaithersburg MD and Vienna VA), plus a full virtual campus.
providers.ages: Grades 1-12 (camps grouped by grade level; designed for advanced/motivated students)
providers.locations: AoPS Academy Gaithersburg, 9711 Washingtonian Blvd Ste 150, Gaithersburg MD; also Vienna VA campus and Virtual Campus
providers.hours: Typically half-day sessions over two-week (or four-week) camps; varies by course
providers.price_detail: $839 per summer course including all books and materials (Gaithersburg tuition page); roughly $420/week equivalent for a 2-week half-day camp
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer camps running (Math Beasts by grade, high-school contest prep); enrollment continues while seats last

reg_2027.opens: spring 2027 (summer schedule posted in spring; mailing list announces it)
reg_2027.notes: First-come enrollment once the schedule posts; new students may need a placement check for level. Advanced sections are small and fill — enroll within weeks of the schedule release.

providers.sessions_2026: Two-week and four-week camps through the summer, multiple grade levels
providers.fit_notes: For kids who love math and want to go deeper than school allows — the culture is problem-solving joy, not test drill. Not a fit for kids who need convincing about math.

providers.name: Arcadia Farm Camp
providers.org: Arcadia Center for Sustainable Food & Agriculture
providers.description: A working-farm camp at Woodlawn (Alexandria) run by the region's leading food-equity nonprofit: kids harvest vegetables, cook what they pick, care for animals, and learn where food actually comes from. Distinctive on two counts — roughly a quarter of campers attend on full or partial scholarship, and there's optional bus transportation from Old Town Alexandria ($150 round trip).
providers.ages: Elementary-age week sessions (roughly 6-12; teen/junior-farmer options some years)
providers.locations: Arcadia Farm at Woodlawn, 9000 Richmond Hwy, Alexandria VA
providers.hours: Camp day ~8/9am-3pm; bus drop-off 7:20-7:30am, pick-up 3:15-3:30pm from Old Town
providers.price_detail: $100 non-refundable deposit at registration; $15 multi-session and sibling discounts; ~25% of campers on full/partial scholarship
providers.financial_aid: Substantial — dedicated Scholarship Fund, income-qualified, arranged with community partners
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions June-August
providers.status_2026: Mid-season with an active waitlist system — waitlisted families are emailed when spots open; transportation slots claimed early

reg_2027.opens: winter (typically December-January)
reg_2027.notes: Fills fast and runs a real waitlist (email prompts to complete registration + deposit when a spot opens); grab bus transportation at registration if needed — it goes first. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: The best genuine farm-camp experience near DC and one of the most economically diverse camp communities in the region; NoVA location but bus from Old Town helps car-free families.

providers.name: Archaeology Camp (Montgomery Parks)
providers.description: A genuinely unusual public camp: kids ages 9-13 work alongside Montgomery Parks' real staff archaeologists at Needwood Mansion, learning mapping, soil identification and artifact processing, doing real excavation, and taking a field trip to a historic site. There is nothing else like it in the region at a government-camp price, and it returns each summer as a cult favorite.
providers.locations: Needwood Mansion, 6700 Needwood Rd, Derwood/Rockville, MD (Rock Creek Regional Park)
providers.hours: Weekday day camp; exact 2026 hours per ActiveMONTGOMERY listing
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on ActiveMONTGOMERY; 2026 price not independently verified
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (need-based, ages 3-18)

reg_2027.opens: late January with all Parks camps (Jan 20, 2026 pattern), 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Tiny capacity (real dig-site supervision limits group size) and a devoted following — this is a register-at-6:30am-sharp camp. Teens too old to be campers can apply to be Archaeology Camp counselors/volunteers for SSL hours.

providers.status_2026: 2026 sessions confirmed to run; assume full — verify cancellations on ActiveMONTGOMERY (not verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: For detail-oriented kids who love history, dinosaurs-and-artifacts energy, and don't mind dirt and patience; it is a working-methods camp, not a treasure hunt.

providers.name: Arts, Dance & Music Camps (Montgomery County Recreation)
providers.description: Week-long county-run camps introducing visual art (drawing, painting, printmaking, sculpture), dance styles (ballet, modern, jazz, hip hop) and instrument sampling — genuinely introductory, taught at rec centers around the county. A low-pressure, low-cost way for a kid to try an art form before committing to a private studio or conservatory program.
providers.ages: roughly 5-15 depending on camp (grade bands vary by listing)
providers.locations: Various recreation and community centers countywide; see the annual Summer Camps GUIDE for the year's sites
providers.hours: Varies by camp; many are half-day or school-day length
providers.extended_care: Not standard; varies by site — verify per listing on ActiveMONTGOMERY
providers.price_detail: Week-by-week pricing per camp on ActiveMONTGOMERY; county rec camps are generally well under private-studio rates. 2026 prices not independently verified.
providers.financial_aid: RecAssist $400/person scholarship for county residents on eligible public assistance, first-come until depleted

reg_2027.opens: mid-to-late January (third Tuesday pattern; Jan 20 in 2026), 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Browse the Summer Camps GUIDE (posted on Issuu/county site in early January) before opening day and have your ActiveMONTGOMERY account + kids' profiles set up in advance. Specialty weeks are smaller than Summer Fun Centers and the popular ones go quickly.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; individual weeks' availability varies — use ActiveMONTGOMERY's 'openings available' filter for remaining late-July/August sessions (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Great first exposure for curious kids; not a match for experienced young artists/dancers/musicians who want intensive training (look to Imagination Stage, studio programs, etc.).

providers.name: ATMTC Summer Musical Theatre Teen Program (SMTTP) + Counselors-in-Training
providers.description: Adventure Theatre's teen track: the Summer Musical Theatre Training Program, a three-week immersive program for rising grades 7-12 with daily technique classes in ballet, jazz, voice, and acting, master classes with professional musical-theatre artists, company rehearsals, a culminating showcase, and a group trip to see a DC-area or Broadway production. A separate volunteer Counselor-in-Training program places teens alongside camp staff at the Glen Echo Park elementary camps.
providers.ages: Rising grades 7-12 (SMTTP, roughly 12-18); CIT for middle/high-school volunteers (SSL-eligible)
providers.locations: SMTTP: ATMTC Academy studios at Wintergreen Plaza, 837-D Rockville Pike, Rockville, MD — NOT Glen Echo. CIT: Glen Echo Park camps (7300 MacArthur Blvd) plus some offsite Montgomery County camp locations
providers.price_detail: SMTTP: $1,650 per three-week session (about $550/week; includes trip to a DC-area or Broadway production). CIT is a volunteer (free) program
providers.financial_aid: Competitive scholarships for SMTTP with full and partial awards available for all sessions; application and audition requirements posted on the Academy site
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm Monday-Friday
providers.sessions_2026: Three-week SMTTP sessions across the summer starting June 22, each ending in a showcase of musical-theatre works on the final Friday; 2026 evening performances listed for Aug 12-13. CIT: full 9-10 day camp sessions, 63-70 volunteer hours each
providers.status_2026: Later SMTTP session upcoming as of mid-July; space not verified — contact registrar (registrar@adventuretheatre-mtc.org). 2026 CIT applications were open into spring

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January; CIT applications open in winter/spring)
reg_2027.notes: SMTTP enrolls by registration ($250-style non-refundable deposit policy applies across ATMTC camps) but scholarship awards require an application and audition materials — start that early. CIT requires an application and commits teens to full camp-day volunteer hours (SSL-eligible for MD students). Note the teen training program meets in Rockville, not Glen Echo — plan the commute accordingly.

providers.fit_notes: Suits committed teen performers who want conservatory-style daily technique training plus a showcase — more training-focused than a single big-show summer stock. CIT is one of the easiest structured SSL-hours pipelines in the arts. Official site blocks direct fetch; location, tuition, and scholarship details verified via search-indexed official pages.

providers.name: AU Future Eagles Soccer Kids Camp
providers.description: A friendly five-day soccer camp for young players of all levels run by American University's women's soccer coaches and student-athletes on the Massachusetts Avenue field next to Turtle Park in upper NW DC. Capped at 40 kids per session (half- and full-day options) with an emphasis on fundamentals through small-sided games; t-shirt, drinks, and prizes included. One of the most convenient college-run camps for NW DC families, and a rare one where girls train with college women players.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-13, all skill levels
providers.locations: Massachusetts Ave Soccer Field, 45th St & Massachusetts Ave NW (next to Turtle Park), Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Half-day and full-day weekly options; includes t-shirt, water/Gatorade, prizes (verify current rates)
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day, Mon-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 sessions per camp site; AU men's staff runs separate clinics/ID camps
providers.status_2026: Check camp site for remaining 2026 sessions; 40-kid caps mean specific weeks sell out

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring (coach-run camp sites post dates by early spring)
reg_2027.notes: First-come on the coaches' camp website (separate from AU's main site); with only 40 spots per session, AU-neighborhood families book as soon as dates post. Parents/sitters may observe anytime — unusual and reassuring.

providers.fit_notes: Sweet-spot neighborhood camp for Tenleytown/Spring Valley/AU Park kids; older competitive players should use AU's ID clinics instead.

providers.name: Bach to Rock Summer Camps (Bethesda & Gaithersburg)
providers.org: Bach to Rock - America's Music School
providers.description: Franchise music school whose weekly summer camps put kids into bands: Rock Band, Rock City (young beginners), Glee Club, Beat Making, Recording Arts, and DJ camps in a professional studio, ending each week with a live performance and a take-home recording. Explicitly no-experience-needed and offered in full-day, half-day, and partial-week formats - one of the most flexible music options in the area.
providers.locations: Bethesda (4910 Cordell Ave) and Gaithersburg locations in close-in Montgomery County; additional DMV franchises in Northern Virginia
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates vary by camp type, full vs half day, and location; not published in indexed pages - contact location (Bethesda 301-913-5757; Gaithersburg 301-330-7625). Historically roughly $250-500/week range.
providers.hours: Full-day and half-day weekly sessions, Monday-Friday
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions throughout summer 2026, June-August
providers.status_2026: Enrollment open for weekly full-day and half-day sessions as of snapshot; rolling weekly format means mid-summer sign-ups are normal.

reg_2027.opens: Typically January-February (camps published in winter)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come enrollment by the week; rarely sells out far in advance, making it a good fallback for families who missed earlier deadlines. Popular weeks (late June/July) fill first.

providers.fit_notes: Great for beginners and kids who want the 'be in a band' experience with low commitment - pick any week. Not a fit for classical training or kids wanting serious repertoire work.

providers.name: Bank of America Student Leaders (Washington DC market)
providers.description: One of the most prestigious teen summer credentials in the country: current high-school juniors and seniors win an eight-week... rather, a six-week PAID internship (35+ hrs/week) at a major local nonprofit, plus an all-expenses-paid national Leadership Summit in Washington (June 16-18 in 2026). A handful of students are selected per market, DC included. Thrives: high-achieving, service-oriented 16-18-year-olds — this is a competitive award, not an enrollment.
providers.ages: Current high school juniors and seniors (~16-18)
providers.price_detail: Paid internship; summit travel and expenses covered
providers.hours: Minimum 35 hours/week for 6 weeks, June-July
providers.locations: Placement at a major nonprofit in the DC area; summit in Washington DC
providers.sessions_2026: Six weeks June-July 2026; Leadership Summit June 16-18, 2026
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 applications ran Feb 9-Mar 16; cohort selected and interning now.

reg_2027.opens: early February (2026 app opened Feb 9, closed Mar 16 with no extensions)
reg_2027.notes: Extremely selective (roughly five students per market). Requires essays and community-service track record; juniors can apply twice. Set a January reminder — the window is barely five weeks.

providers.fit_notes: A lottery ticket, not a plan: apply, but have a real summer plan behind it. Strong fit for teens already deep in community service.

providers.name: Bar-T Mountainside Camp
providers.description: A big, polished outdoor day camp on a mountain campus in Urbana (Frederick County) with pools, zip lines, climbing, ropes courses, farm animals, and traditional camp activities, run by the long-established Bar-T organization that also operates MoCo before/after-school programs. Bus transportation from Montgomery County stops makes the distance workable. It's the 'everything camp' for families who want adventure infrastructure with strong logistics.
providers.ages: Entering K through 8th grade (sports add-ons from 2nd grade)
providers.locations: 2914 Roderick Rd, Urbana, MD 21704 (Frederick Co.); bus stops across Montgomery County; sister Bar-T Ranch campus in Gaithersburg
providers.price_detail: $565/week (2026); drops to $485/week when enrolled 5+ weeks across Bar-T camps; kick-off registration discount ($200 off 5+ weeks) during the opening weekend
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:30pm core day; before/after care and bus options extend it
providers.extended_care: Extended-day options and busing available (details on bar-t.com)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 22 - early August
providers.status_2026: Season underway; week-by-week availability unverified — the free change/cancel window closed May 15, so late openings do occur. Call 301-948-3819.

reg_2027.opens: Black Friday weekend, late November (2026 registration opened Nov 28, 2025)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online, opening Black Friday weekend with a 4-day kick-off discount ($200 off Mountainside/Ranch when booking 5+ weeks; first 500 enrollees get a gift box). Generous change policy until mid-May makes early speculative booking low-risk — locals grab weeks on Black Friday and adjust later.

providers.fit_notes: Great for kids who want variety and big-facility fun (pools + zip lines) rather than one deep skill; the Urbana location means relying on the bus for most DC/close-in-MoCo families.

providers.name: Bar-T Ranch Camp
providers.description: A working ranch camp on Olney-Laytonsville Road where kids do farm animals, fishing, zip lines, pools, and old-school outdoor play; Bar-T also runs the larger Mountainside adventure campus in Urbana. A 50+ year Montgomery County institution with unusually family-friendly policies — free schedule changes and full refunds until mid-May.
providers.ages: 5-13 (week-long day camps for ages 5 through 13 per official site; roughly entering K through 8th grade)
providers.locations: Bar-T Ranch, 6530 Olney-Laytonsville Rd, Gaithersburg MD 20882; Bar-T Mountainside, Urbana MD (Frederick County)
providers.price_detail: 2026: $565/week at Ranch Camp ($485/week when enrolled 5+ weeks across Bar-T camps); final short week priced separately
providers.hours: Full-day camp; before/after care available (verify times)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly: late June through mid-August, plus a short final week (researcher-reported June 22-Aug 14 + Aug 17-20; verify exact dates at bar-t.com/summer-camps/about/kids-camp/dates-rates)
providers.status_2026: 2026 season in progress with sessions into mid-August; historically keeps rolling availability deeper into summer than school camps — check registration portal for remaining weeks

reg_2027.opens: Winter (registration portal typically opens around January); rolling thereafter
reg_2027.notes: Low-stress registration: free changes/cancellations until mid-May with full refund, multi-week discount rewards committing early. Ranch weeks with popular specialty add-ons go first.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for animal-loving, outdoorsy kids who don't need air-conditioned polish; farther out (Gaithersburg/Urbana), so factor the drive or their transport options.

providers.name: Baroody Camps
providers.description: A NoVA multi-site operator running a huge catalog of week-long specialty camps — robotics, STEM challenges, sports, creative arts, outdoor exploration — at school campuses across Falls Church, Arlington, and Alexandria (flagship site: Alexandria Country Day School). Think of it as a menu-style camp mall: you assemble a summer week by week from dozens of themes.
providers.ages: Roughly rising K-8 depending on camp
providers.locations: School sites in Alexandria (2400 Russell Rd), Falls Church, and Arlington
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing varies by camp type; catalog at signup.baroodycamps.com
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long camps all summer across jurisdictions
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; some late-July/August weeks typically still bookable — check the signup portal

reg_2027.opens: staggered by jurisdiction in mid-to-late winter (catalog posts around January; Falls Church/Arlington/Alexandria registrations open on separate dates)
reg_2027.notes: Registration 'fills quickly' per the operator; popular STEM and sports weeks go first. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered at most sites
providers.fit_notes: NoVA-only footprint — included as a gap-filler for families west of the river; DC/MoCo equivalents are Steve & Kate's or jurisdiction rec camps.

providers.name: Barrie Camp Counselor-in-Training (CIT)
providers.description: Full-time volunteer CITs (rising 10th graders) assist counselors and activity specialists at Barrie Camp, a classic 45-acre wooded day camp in the Aspen Hill area of Silver Spring with swimming, horseback riding, and adventure activities. CITs work 8:00am-4:30pm for a minimum of four weeks and are held to paid-staff standards; younger teens can do Barrie's LIT (Leader-in-Training) camper program first. Thrives: outdoorsy teens who grew up at day camp and want the counselor track.
providers.ages: CIT: rising 10th graders (~15); LIT camper program for slightly younger teens
providers.price_detail: CIT is a volunteer staff role (no fee); the LIT camper program is paid tuition like other camp programs
providers.hours: 8:00am-4:30pm, minimum 4-week commitment
providers.locations: Barrie School campus, 13500 Layhill Rd, Silver Spring MD 20906 (Aspen Hill, near Wheaton/Glenmont)
providers.sessions_2026: Camp season roughly mid-June through mid-August 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 CIT class was selected in spring via the staff application; season underway.

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring, via the CampInTouch staff application portal
reg_2027.notes: Interview required for applicants who are not former Barrie campers or LITs — the pipeline strongly favors kids who did LIT the previous summer. Designated MCPS SSL site.

providers.financial_aid: N/A for CIT (volunteer); camp offers standard tuition inquiries for LIT
providers.fit_notes: Best for teens already in the Barrie orbit; outside applicants face an interview and fewer slots. The 4-week minimum is a real commitment but yields a big SSL-hour haul.

providers.name: Barrie Day Camp
providers.description: A time-honored traditional day camp on the Barrie School's 45 wooded acres in the Aspen Hill area of Silver Spring, with swimming, horseback riding, archery, arts, and classic camp culture for ages 4-14. One of the few close-in camps with a real equestrian program (no riding experience required), and it runs its own bus network from stops around Montgomery County and NW DC.
providers.locations: 13500 Layhill Road, Silver Spring MD 20906 (45-acre campus)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates listed at barrie.org/camp/tuition (page blocked from verification); $100 non-refundable registration fee per camper; bus and extended-day priced separately
providers.hours: Standard camp day; extended hours program available
providers.sessions_2026: Warm Up Week June 15-18, then weekly sessions across the summer; withdrawal deadline was May 1, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration ran normally for 2026; in-season availability not verified — call for open August weeks

reg_2027.opens: Typically winter (around January); not yet published
reg_2027.notes: Horseback/equestrian weeks are the scarcest slots and fill first (AM riding group is typically grades 2-4, PM group grades 4-8). Bus stops are pre-set (no door-to-door) — check the route map before committing.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want a woodsy, traditional camp with horses without leaving the close-in suburbs; less shiny-facility, more nature than the school-run camps.

providers.name: Beauvoir Summer
providers.org: Beauvoir, the National Cathedral Elementary School
providers.description: A joyful early-childhood and elementary day camp on Beauvoir's 60-acre share of the Washington National Cathedral grounds, with themed weeks of arts, swimming, games, and outdoor play for PreK through rising 5th graders, plus a Pathfinders junior-CIT track for rising 6th-10th graders. One of the best settings in the city for the youngest campers.
providers.ages: 4-10 core (PreK-rising 5th); Pathfinders/CIT rising 6th-10th
providers.locations: Beauvoir School, 3500 Woodley Road NW, Washington DC (Washington National Cathedral grounds)
providers.price_detail: Per-session pricing in CampBrain; $200 non-refundable deposit + $50 registration fee per camper; balance due April 29
providers.hours: Full camp day; extended day typically offered
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed sessions June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: First-come registration ran normally; per-week 2026 availability not verified — email for remaining sessions

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January); CampBrain
reg_2027.notes: First-come, first-served; younger age groups (Creative-age PreK/K) are the tightest. Deposit locks the spot; full balance due late April.

providers.fit_notes: The gold-standard setting for ages 4-8 in NW DC; older kids age out of the core program quickly, so it's a starter camp more than a whole-childhood camp.

providers.name: Best Delegate MUN Institute at Georgetown University
providers.org: Best Delegate (Model United Nations education company)
providers.description: The most established Model UN summer program in the country, held at Georgetown — students run simulated UN committees, give speeches, draft resolutions, and win daily awards, with day and overnight options. Draws ambitious kids nationally; being in DC means the diplomacy theme feels real. Ages 9-18, grouped by age and experience, beginners welcome.
providers.ages: 9-18 (middle school and high school divisions)
providers.locations: Georgetown University campus, Washington DC (plus other campuses nationally and virtual)
providers.price_detail: 2026: Day camp $1,750/week ($1,899 with tutoring add-on); overnight from $2,700/week. First 5 registrants per class save $350.
providers.sessions_2026: Georgetown day sessions late June-early July (e.g., Jun 22-26, Jul 6-10); overnight sessions Jun 22-28, Jun 29-Jul 3, Jul 6-12
providers.status_2026: All 2026 Georgetown sessions have started or finished as of mid-July; virtual sessions may remain; they warn camps sell out

reg_2027.opens: Early fall of the prior year (registration for the following summer goes live ~Sept-Oct)
reg_2027.notes: First-come with tiered early-bird pricing (first 5 per class save $350). Georgetown overnight fills first; register by winter break for choice of session. No audition; no experience needed.

providers.fit_notes: For globally curious, ambitious kids — great MUN on-ramp even with zero experience, but the price is steep. WUDL's free institute is the budget alternative for debate-adjacent skills.

providers.name: Bethesda Martial Arts Summer Camp (Master Lee)
providers.org: Bethesda Martial Arts / Go Master Lee
providers.description: A downtown-Bethesda taekwondo school running roughly ten weeks of summer day camp (9am-3pm) that blends daily martial-arts training — including tumbling and beginner weapons like nunchucks — with park outings, library visits, and arts & crafts. Open to complete beginners around ages 4-11, with snacks and a camp t-shirt provided. Representative of the many solid dojo day camps in close-in Montgomery County; this one is notable for its long season and walkable Bethesda Row location.
providers.ages: Approximately 4-11 (white belt and up welcome)
providers.locations: 4833 Bethesda Avenue (lower level), Bethesda, MD 20814
providers.price_detail: Weekly pricing not published in verified sources; area martial-arts camps typically run ~$125-400/week
providers.sessions_2026: Roughly 10 weekly sessions, mid-June through late August (search-indexed dates: ~Jun 16-Aug 22 pattern)
providers.status_2026: Long 10-week season means late-summer weeks are often still open in July — call to confirm

reg_2027.opens: spring
reg_2027.notes: Dojo camps like this rarely sell out months ahead; they're a dependable fallback when marquee camps are full. Enrolled students get priority in practice.

providers.name: Bethesda Soccer Club Summer Camps & Soccer Juniors
providers.description: One of the country's best-known youth soccer clubs (now with USL youth affiliation) runs summer programming at its Gaithersburg home base: Soccer Juniors morning sessions for ages 3-6, evening pre-season prep camps for its youth development (U7-U11) age groups in late August, and higher-level camp options including a residential camp. The club's brand is competitive development — even its camps skew toward kids on a club-soccer trajectory.
providers.ages: 3-6 (Soccer Juniors); U7-U11 evening camps; older youth for select/residential programs
providers.locations: Bethesda SC facility, 8717 Grovemont Circle, Gaithersburg MD (despite the club name)
providers.price_detail: Soccer Juniors $99/week (3 sessions) or $390 for 12 sessions; evening and residential camps priced separately
providers.hours: Juniors 9:00-10:30 AM; YDP evening camps 5-7 PM (boys) / 6-8 PM (girls), Aug 17-20
providers.sessions_2026: Soccer Juniors weekly across four summer weeks; YDP evening camps Aug 17-20, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open; August evening pre-season camps still bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: Spring (posted with the club's summer programming, typically March-April)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via LeagueApps. The late-August evening camps are aimed at players prepping for fall club season; casual players are fine in Soccer Juniors but the club vibe is competitive.

providers.fit_notes: For soccer-serious families and preschoolers starting the pathway; note the confusingly named club actually trains in Gaithersburg. Not a full-day childcare-style camp.

providers.name: Black Hill Discovery Center Summer Camps (incl. Nature Explorer & Nature Girls)
providers.description: Lakefront nature camps at Black Hill Regional Park in Boyds (upcounty, overlooking Little Seneca Lake) — the only county nature camp where boating and fishing are core activities. 2026 offerings include Nature Explorer Camp (ages 6-8, July 27-31, 9am-3:30pm, $350: boating, fishing, hiking, creek exploration) and Nature Girls Camp (ages 9-14, Aug 10-14), part of the Parks' girls-in-nature series.
providers.ages: 6-14 depending on week (Nature Explorer 6-8; Nature Girls 9-14)
providers.locations: Black Hill Discovery Center, Black Hill Regional Park, Boyds, MD (upcounty, ~15 min from Gaithersburg/Germantown)
providers.hours: 9am-3:30pm Mon-Fri
providers.extended_care: Aftercare for select camps — check listing
providers.price_detail: Nature Explorer Camp $350/week (2026, verified); $25 early-bird discount by April 3, 2026
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (need-based, ages 3-18; 2026 fund fully awarded)

reg_2027.opens: late January (Jan 20, 2026 pattern), 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Fewer session weeks than the downcounty nature centers, so the boating-heavy weeks disappear fast. Its upcounty location means slightly better odds for late registrants than Locust Grove/Brookside.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-season weeks were still listed for July 27-31 (Nature Explorer) and Aug 10-14 (Nature Girls) — check ActiveMONTGOMERY for live seats (availability not verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: The pick for water-loving kids — no other county nature camp puts campers in boats. Boyds location is a real drive from downcounty; no Metro access.

providers.name: Blues Alley Summer Jazz Camp
providers.description: A two-week big-band jazz camp run by the education arm of the legendary Georgetown jazz club, held at St. Augustine's Church in SW DC: improvisation coaching from working DC jazz professionals, ensemble rehearsals, jazz theory, and a new composition/arranging class. Standout campers feed into the Blues Alley Youth Orchestra, which gigs at the Kennedy Center, Smithsonian, and Blues Alley itself.
providers.ages: 11-18; requires basic understanding of major/minor scales on your instrument
providers.locations: St. Augustine's Episcopal Church, 555 Water St SW, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Tuition posted on bluesalley.org/tuition but amount not verifiable via indexed copies - do not rely on third-party figures; contact the Jazz Society
providers.hours: 9:30am-3:30pm, Monday-Friday for two weeks
providers.financial_aid: Not confirmed; the Jazz Society has historically offered scholarships - ask directly
providers.sessions_2026: July 27-August 7, 2026 (two weeks)
providers.status_2026: Session is upcoming as of snapshot (starts July 27) and registration pages were live - plausible late 2026 availability; verify directly.

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring; rolling online registration
reg_2027.notes: Register online via bluesalley.org; no audition, but campers should know basic scales. The affiliated Youth Orchestra (ages 13-18) is the selective year-round track.

providers.fit_notes: For middle/high schoolers who already play an instrument and are jazz-curious - the pro-musician faculty and venue lineage are the draw. Not for beginners who don't yet know their scales.

providers.name: Bolt Parkour & Freerunning Academy Summer Camp
providers.description: A family-owned 7,200 sq ft parkour gym in North Bethesda running weekly camps all summer: warm-up games, parkour fundamentals (vaults, jumps, balance, quadrupedal movement) in the morning, tumbling and obstacle challenges in the afternoon, coached by the gym's year-round staff rather than seasonal hires. No experience needed — everyone starts at ground level. Ideal for kids who bounce off walls literally and hated conventional sports camps.
providers.ages: 6 and up
providers.locations: 4950 Boiling Brook Pkwy, North Bethesda, MD (Randolph Rd corridor near White Flint)
providers.hours: Full day 8:30am-4:30pm; half-day option
providers.extended_care: Long full-day hours (8:30-4:30) reduce the need; no separate aftercare found
providers.price_detail: Full day $495/week, half day $345/week (early-bird discounts through March 31; premium weeks excluded); $200 non-refundable deposit, balance due June 1
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open with weeks running through August; site warns spots fill fast — late-July/August weeks were the ones to check as of mid-July 2026

reg_2027.opens: winter (early-bird pricing runs until March 31)
reg_2027.notes: Book by March 31 for the early-bird rate; balance auto-due June 1. Because it runs many weeks, mid-summer availability is more common than at one-session camps.

providers.name: Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington Summer Camps
providers.org: Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington
providers.description: Full-summer, full-day camp at BGCGW clubhouses across the region — in DC: Richard England Clubhouse 14 (Benning Rd NE), FBR Club @THEARC (Ward 8), and the Jelleff Club (Georgetown); plus Maryland (Germantown) and Virginia clubs. Long hours, structured enrichment, sports, field trips, and daily snack at a fraction of private-camp cost — one of the region's most important low-cost full-coverage options for working families.
providers.ages: roughly 5-14 (varies by clubhouse; teen programming to 18)
providers.locations: DC: Richard England Clubhouse 14 (NE), FBR Club @THEARC (SE), Jelleff Club (Georgetown NW); MD: Germantown Club; VA: multiple clubs
providers.price_detail: Historically well under $250/wk with sliding-scale membership by household income; exact 2026 fees in the enrollment packet (bgcgw.org PDF, posted Feb 2026) — could not extract fee table from indexed copy
providers.hours: Long full-day coverage (2026 caregiver handbook lists 6am-6pm for at least some clubs); free afternoon snack daily, families send breakfast + bag lunch
providers.extended_care: Effectively built in via long club hours
providers.financial_aid: Sliding-scale fees by household income; scholarships via clubs

reg_2027.opens: enrollment packet posted ~February; enrollment runs spring until capacity
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via BGCGW parent portal (bgcgreaterwashington.my.site.com), per-clubhouse capacity
reg_2027.notes: Returning club members effectively get first crack; new families should create a portal account in March and enroll the day their clubhouse opens. Capacity, not price, is the constraint.

providers.sessions_2026: Full-summer program from late June through August 2026 at participating clubhouses
providers.status_2026: Season underway; mid-summer entry sometimes possible at clubs below capacity — call your clubhouse
providers.fit_notes: The best cost-to-coverage ratio in this guide; program quality varies by clubhouse, so visit first. Not a specialty camp. Fee table not directly verifiable (site blocked fetch) — treat $ band as historical pattern plus sliding scale.

providers.name: Brainy Camps
providers.description: A consortium of condition-specific overnight camps run by Children's National Hospital, where every session — epilepsy, type 1 diabetes, heart conditions, autism (level 1), neurofibromatosis, Tourette, sickle cell, and more — has medical staff who specialize in that condition, so kids camp alongside peers who share their diagnosis. 2026 sessions ran late June through late July, including family-camp weekends for heart and T1D families.
providers.ages: Roughly 7-17 depending on condition-specific session; transitional-youth tracks for older teens
providers.locations: Residential camp facilities in Virginia (Brainy Camps' partner sites); administered from Children's National, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Session fees with scholarships and payment plans; stated goal is to never turn a child away over ability to pay
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships and payment plans — central to the mission
providers.sessions_2026: Autism L1/Transitional Youth June 27-July 1; Heart July 5-11 (family 7/9-10); T1D July 12-18 (family 7/15-17); NF & Epilepsy July 19-25
providers.status_2026: 2026 sessions essentially complete or underway as of mid-July

reg_2027.opens: winter 2027 (session calendar typically posted by January-February)
reg_2027.mechanism: application with medical forms, per condition
reg_2027.notes: Register through the specific condition camp; families connected to Children's National clinics often hear first — ask your specialist. Verified via official Brainy Camps/Children's National pages.

providers.fit_notes: The regional answer for a kid whose chronic condition makes ordinary sleepaway camp feel unsafe; being surrounded by kids with the same diagnosis is the transformative part.

providers.name: Brookside Nature Center Summer Camps
providers.description: Naturalist-led nature camp at a real nature center in Wheaton Regional Park — hikes, live animals, crafts, games and creek time, with sessions from preschool through rising 7th grade. Montgomery Parks' five nature-center camps are beloved, small and famously hard to get into on registration day.
providers.ages: preschool (age 3+) through entering grade 7, in age-banded weeks
providers.locations: Brookside Nature Center, 1400 Glenallan Ave, Wheaton, MD 20902 (Wheaton Regional Park)
providers.hours: Most camps 9am-3:30pm Mon-Fri; preschool weeks are half-day
providers.extended_care: Aftercare offered for select Montgomery Parks camps — check each listing on ActiveMONTGOMERY
providers.price_detail: Priced per week on ActiveMONTGOMERY; comparable Parks full-day nature weeks run ~$350 (Black Hill's verified 2026 price). $25 early-bird discount if registered by April 3, 2026.
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship — need-based, confidential, ages 3-18, via Montgomery Parks Foundation (2026 apps opened Jan 13 and all awards were claimed)

reg_2027.opens: late January (2026: Tue Jan 20 at 6:30am; 2025: Jan 21) on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Nature-center weeks are among the fastest-selling public camps in the county — be online at 6:30am on opening day with your account ready. Early-bird discount (~$25) for registering by early April. Dream Camp scholarship applications open the week before registration and run out.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; most weeks filled in winter/spring. Any remaining seats appear in ActiveMONTGOMERY's 'openings available' filter (live status not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Ideal for kids who love animals, creeks and being outside all day; screens-free and gently structured. Not full workday coverage unless the week offers aftercare.

providers.name: Bullis Summer Programs
providers.description: An eight-week open-enrollment camp machine on Bullis's stunning 102-acre Potomac campus, offering full- and half-day one- to two-week camps across sports, STEM, arts, and traditional day camp for ages 3.5-16. Known for variety and value among the private-school camps, with facilities (turf fields, pools, maker spaces) most camps can't match.
providers.locations: Bullis School, 10601 Falls Road, Potomac MD 20854 (102-acre campus)
providers.price_detail: Per-camp pricing; $100/session deposit at registration (applied to tuition)
providers.hours: Full- and half-day options; extended day available
providers.sessions_2026: Eight weeks of one- and two-week camps, June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: In season; large catalog means scattered availability typically persists into late July/August — check CampBrain per camp

reg_2027.opens: Early January (2026 opened Mon Jan 5 at 10:00am)
reg_2027.notes: CampBrain first-come with $100/session deposit; create your account before opening day. Popular specialty weeks fill in the first weeks of January.

providers.fit_notes: Best facilities-per-dollar in the Potomac corridor; requires driving — no bus network — so mainly for upper-MoCo families.

providers.name: Burgundy Center for Wildlife Studies (overnight)
providers.description: Burgundy's legendary tiny overnight nature camp on a 600-acre wildlife sanctuary in Capon Bridge, West Virginia — never more than 36 campers, with naturalist staff mixing serious ecology, astronomy, and conservation with hiking, art, music, and humor. For a nature-obsessed 8-15 year-old, this is arguably the best sleepaway science camp within reach of DC.
providers.ages: 8-15 (sessions for 8-10 and 11-15; adult/family programs too)
providers.locations: Capon Bridge WV (~2 hrs from DC); run by Alexandria's Burgundy Farm school
providers.price_detail: Overnight session pricing; see burgundycenter.org/programs
providers.sessions_2026: Coed overnight sessions June-August; 2026 registration opened over the winter with info sessions through Feb 19
providers.status_2026: Sessions underway; with only 36 beds per session, 2026 is effectively full

reg_2027.opens: early winter (registration historically opens Nov-Dec, with info sessions through February)
reg_2027.notes: Capacity of 36 makes this one of the fastest-filling camps in the guide — returning families rebook immediately; newcomers should join the mailing list in fall 2026. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who'd rather catch salamanders than play capture-the-flag; rustic facilities and real fieldwork. Not for kids needing lots of structure or creature comforts.

providers.name: Burgundy Farm Summer Day Camp
providers.description: One of the DC region's most famous day camps: a 26-acre farm-and-woods campus in Alexandria where ages 3-11 choose their own activities, swim daily (with instruction), and do classic camp traditions, with a CIT program for 12-15s. ACA-accredited, fully licensed, and one of the rare marquee camps that genuinely takes 3- and 4-year-olds.
providers.ages: 3-11 campers; CIT 12-15
providers.locations: 3700 Burgundy Rd, Alexandria VA (Burgundy Farm Country Day School campus)
providers.price_detail: Session-based pricing (choose one, several, or all sessions); daily swim included — see burgundysummer.org/information
providers.financial_aid: Ask camp; school operates need-based aid — summer aid availability varies
providers.sessions_2026: Multiple sessions June-August; priority registration for returning families opened Jan 27, open registration Feb 3
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; popular sessions filled in late winter — check for isolated late-summer openings

reg_2027.opens: late January (returning-family priority ~Jan 27 at 10am; open registration ~Feb 3 at 10am, based on the 2026 pattern)
reg_2027.notes: A regional institution — younger age groups and early-summer sessions go fast after open registration; set a calendar reminder for the February open date. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: Extended-day options offered — confirm hours on information page
providers.fit_notes: The go-to recommendation for a first 'real' outdoor camp, including for preschoolers; it's in Alexandria, so DC/MoCo families should weigh the commute (no DC-side transportation).

providers.name: Butler Camp
providers.description: A classic woodsy day camp on 22 acres bordering Seneca Creek State Park in Darnestown, focused on outdoor adventure, wilderness skills, creek play, and traditional camp fun for ages 4½-16. It's smaller and gentler than the big adventure operations, with a Montessori-adjacent, child-led sensibility, and runs in two-week sessions rather than single weeks. A solid mid-county choice for families near the I-270 corridor.
providers.ages: 4.5-16 (Nature Navigators 4.5-6, must be 4 by Jan 1; Wilderness Explorers 7-10; Pathfinders 10-12, must be 10 by Aug 31; site states it accepts campers up to 16 — verify teen options directly)
providers.locations: 15951 Germantown Rd, Darnestown, MD (minutes from I-270, adjacent to Seneca Creek State Park)
providers.price_detail: 2026 per two-week session: Nature Navigators $820 (~$410/wk); Wilderness Explorers $970 ($900 for Session 1; ~$450-485/wk); Pathfinders $1,070 ($990 for Session 1; ~$495-535/wk). Payment plans offered; aftercare fees refundable anytime before a session starts.
providers.hours: Standard camp day; aftercare offered
providers.financial_aid: Payment plans offered; no scholarship program found
providers.sessions_2026: Four 2-week sessions: June 22-July 3, July 6-17, July 20-31, August 3-14
providers.status_2026: Season underway (Session 2 of 4 in progress); current availability unverified — call 301-977-6600. Note their refund calendar (no refunds after April 1) implies most families commit by early spring.

reg_2027.opens: Early January (2026: priority enrollment opened Jan 7, open enrollment Jan 13)
reg_2027.notes: Priority window for returning families, then first-come open enrollment about a week later; payment plans run until April 30. Less of a stampede than Calleva/Valley Mill, but don't wait past winter for prime sessions — and note the two-week-session structure means fewer, bigger commitments.

providers.fit_notes: Good for kids who want unstructured-feeling outdoor play and wilderness skills without the intensity (or price) of an adventure-sports camp; two-week sessions suit families wanting continuity, not week-by-week flexibility; up-county location means a real drive from DC.

providers.name: Cabin John Ice Rink Summer Skating & Hockey Camps
providers.description: The county's big Bethesda/Potomac-side rink in Cabin John Regional Park runs summer camps combining a daily 30-minute group lesson with public-session practice time, grouped by age and ability with no experience needed and rental skates included. Week-long hockey camps (for kids past Hockey 2 level) cover skating, stick handling, shooting, and team play. A cool-in-every-sense escape from DC summer heat at county prices.
providers.ages: 6-12 (skating camps); hockey camps require Hockey 2 or equivalent
providers.locations: Cabin John Ice Rink, 10610 Westlake Dr, Rockville MD (Cabin John Regional Park, near Montgomery Mall)
providers.price_detail: County rates for 9 AM-1 PM camp; afternoon care until 5 PM extra; rental skates included
providers.hours: 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM; afternoon care to 5:00 PM
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (Montgomery Parks) — applications opened Jan 13, 2026
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026 (registration opened Jan 20, 2026)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; many weeks sold out since county camp registration opened in January, but cancellations and some late-summer weeks appear on ActiveMONTGOMERY

reg_2027.opens: Mid-to-late January (2026 registration opened Tue Jan 20 at 6:30 AM)
reg_2027.notes: Classic MoCo camp scramble: ActiveMONTGOMERY opens at 6:30 AM with a virtual queue — set up your account and wishlist in advance and log in at 6:30 sharp. Early-bird discount if registered by early April; scholarship applications open a week before general registration.

providers.fit_notes: Excellent value and genuinely beginner-friendly; the half-day format plus paid afternoon care requires planning for full-time working parents. Hockey camps are for kids already through basic skills levels.

providers.name: Calleva Summer Camp
providers.description: The DMV's big outdoor-adventure institution: a family-run camp based at Riley's Lock on the Potomac (Poolesville) plus Markoff's 168-acre farm in Dickerson and Northern Virginia sites (McLean, and newly Great Falls), with tracks spanning kayaking, climbing, sailing, horses, farm life, survival ('Camper vs. Wild'), and packrafting. Kids are outside all day doing genuinely skilled activities with a famously spirited staff. The farm also hosts the beloved public 'Dirty Dinners' farm-to-table family evenings — a separate event series, not a camp.
providers.ages: 4-16 (Cubs 4-5, Explorers 6-9, Sampler 8-12, Adventure 9-15, High Adventurers 12-16, Leaders in Training 13-16); ages as of Sept 1
providers.locations: Riley's Lock, 13015 Rileys Lock Rd, Poolesville, MD (hub); Markoff's Farm, Dickerson, MD; Northern Virginia campuses in McLean and (new for 2026) Great Falls; bus pick-ups around the region
providers.price_detail: 2026: $670/week Explorers-level; $785/week specialty camps (packrafting, sailing, Camper vs. Wild) including a Thursday overnight; optional farm lunches $65/week (pre-order by the Tuesday before)
providers.hours: Full camp day; bus transportation from area stops in addition to on-site drop-off
providers.extended_care: Not verified; bus options substitute for much of the need
providers.financial_aid: None found on indexed pages — ask directly
providers.sessions_2026: 9 one-week sessions, roughly mid-June through mid-August
providers.status_2026: Sells out very quickly; most weeks assumed full by mid-summer. Check CampInTouch Dates & Rates for any open weeks (calleva.campintouch.com).

reg_2027.opens: Early-to-mid January (2026: returning families Jan 6, new campers Jan 12 at noon for the VA campuses; MD follows the same returning-first pattern)
reg_2027.notes: Returning families get a priority window, then new-family registration opens and popular tracks (horses, specialty adventure weeks) go fast. Newcomers should make an account in advance and register the minute new-camper registration opens.

providers.fit_notes: Best for adventurous, physically confident kids who want real skills (whitewater, climbing, farm work); pricey, far up-county, and high-energy — not the fit for a kid who wants low-key crafts.

providers.name: Camp Accomplish
providers.description: A genuinely inclusive day and overnight camp on Melwood's 108-acre recreation center in rural Charles County (about an hour south of DC), co-run with Children's National Hospital so campers with disabilities and chronic medical conditions — and their siblings and typical peers — camp together with real medical support on site. Activities include swimming, climbing wall, ropes courses, archery, canoeing, horseback riding, and campfires. Melwood subsidizes more than half the true cost of every camper.
providers.ages: Day camp 5-18; overnight 8-18
providers.locations: Melwood Recreation Center, 9035 Ironsides Rd, Nanjemoy MD (Charles County, ~1 hr south of DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates on campaccomplish.org/experience-rates (not captured in indexed copies); every camper's fee is already subsidized >50% below true cost
providers.financial_aid: Need-based scholarships including full scholarships — request on the initial application; in 2024 they met 100% of aid requests. Nearly 1 in 3 campers receives aid.
providers.hours: Day camp full-day; overnight is residential weekly sessions
providers.extended_care: Ask camp (day campers travel from a wide radius)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly day and overnight sessions, summer 2026 (June-August)
providers.status_2026: Season underway; contact camp about remaining late-July/August day or overnight openings

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration typically opens Jan-Feb; no verified date)
reg_2027.mechanism: application (register online; scholarship request on initial application)
reg_2027.notes: One of the only overnight options in the region for kids with chronic health conditions (Children's National medical staffing). Indicate scholarship interest on the first application — do not wait. Verified via campaccomplish.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: The go-to for a child with medical complexity or a disability who wants a real camp experience (including sleepaway) with clinical backup, and for siblings who want to attend together. Located an hour+ from DC — day camp commute is significant.

providers.name: Camp Arena Stage
providers.description: A multi-arts day camp from DC's storied Arena Stage where campers ages 8-15 build their own daily schedule from classes in theatre, dance, music, visual art, filmmaking, and writing — more arts-buffet than single-show camp, held on the leafy Georgetown Visitation Prep campus. Beloved for letting kids sample widely and go deep in what they love, capped by sharing performances.
providers.locations: Georgetown Visitation Preparatory School campus, 1524 35th St NW, Washington DC (camp operated by Arena Stage, SW DC)
providers.price_detail: Per official logistics page (search-verified): $300 non-refundable deposit; full tuition listed at $1,400 with balance due June 5, 2026 (confirm which session length this covers); 20% off for military/teacher/first-responder families; returning-camper discounts ($50-$100) by Feb 14
providers.financial_aid: Need-based scholarships by application (dedicated scholarships page)
providers.hours: Full day Mon-Fri (exact hours on logistics page)
providers.extended_care: Extended day historically offered; verify with camp office
providers.sessions_2026: 3-week session July 6-24; 2-week session July 27-Aug 7
providers.status_2026: 3-week session in progress (ends July 24); 2-week session starts July 27 — call 202-600-4064 to ask about remaining spots

reg_2027.opens: December-January (registration live well before the Feb 14 returning-camper deadline; Arena also hosts a DC-area performing-arts camp fair in mid-January)
reg_2027.notes: Returning campers get an early-registration discount through mid-February — a signal that loyal families rebook fast. Book by midwinter for choice of session.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for the multi-passionate kid who can't pick one art form; the choose-your-own-schedule model is unique in the area. Less ideal for a kid who wants to rehearse and star in one big show.

providers.name: Camp Fantastic
providers.org: Special Love, Inc. (with NIH/NCI Pediatric Oncology Branch)
providers.description: A one-of-a-kind week of overnight camp for about 100 children with cancer (ages 7-17, within 5 years of treatment), held each August at the Northern Virginia 4-H Center in Front Royal with NIH National Cancer Institute physicians and nurses providing full on-site medical care since 1983. Campers do ropes course, canoeing, swimming, and cooking like any camp — chemo schedules, ports, and counts are handled quietly in the background; teens get exclusive lodging and an LIT track for 16-17s.
providers.ages: 7-17 with cancer (within 5 yrs of treatment, or 7 yrs post-BMT)
providers.locations: Northern Virginia 4-H Educational Center, Front Royal VA; run by Special Love (Winchester VA), serving the DC/MD/VA region
providers.price_detail: Special Love fundraises so cost is never a barrier; families pay little or nothing — confirm current policy with Special Love
providers.financial_aid: Core to the model — no family turned away for inability to pay
providers.sessions_2026: August 2-8, 2026 (one session, ~100 campers)
providers.status_2026: Application window closed for Aug 2026 session; contact Special Love about waitlist

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring 2027 via Special Love
reg_2027.mechanism: application with medical eligibility review (treatment-timeline criteria)
reg_2027.notes: Not competitive in the ordinary sense — eligibility and medical clearance drive admission; Special Love also runs family weekends and sibling programs year-round. Verified via official Special Love and NCI pages.

providers.fit_notes: For families navigating pediatric cancer this is the gold-standard week — full NIH medical team on site. Eligibility is strictly diagnosis-based.

providers.name: Camp Happyland / Rappahannock Camp & Retreat Center
providers.org: The Salvation Army (National Capital Area Command)
providers.description: The Salvation Army's ACA-accredited overnight camp in Richardsville, VA (about 70 miles south of DC) serves kids 8-17 from the communities its DC-area corps serve — swimming, hiking, outdoor skills, and crafts at an extraordinary price: about $75 per week plus a one-time $15 registration fee. Registration goes through your neighborhood Salvation Army unit rather than a website checkout.
providers.price_detail: $75/week + $15 one-time registration (2026); field trips extra
providers.locations: Richardsville, VA (Culpeper County); registration via Salvation Army corps in DC, MD, VA
providers.hours: Week-long residential sessions
providers.financial_aid: Fees are already donor-subsidized; corps can waive or reduce further for families in need — ask your local unit

reg_2027.opens: Spring, through local Salvation Army corps community centers
reg_2027.notes: There is no central online signup — call or visit the Salvation Army location nearest you (Sherman Ave NW, Solomon G. Brown SE, etc.) to register and arrange fees. Sessions fill through early summer; start in April.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly overnight sessions, summer 2026, ages 8-17
providers.status_2026: 2026 season running; late-session space possible via local corps
providers.fit_notes: Probably the cheapest real sleepaway camp available to DC-area kids. Faith-based but serves all families. The corps-based registration is a hurdle for newcomers — expect phone calls, not web forms.

providers.name: Camp Horizons (Virginia)
providers.description: A large coed overnight camp in the Shenandoah Valley near Harrisonburg, VA (~2.25 hours from DC) with 40+ years of history and unusually broad programming across seven departments — aquatics, equestrian, performing arts, outdoors, science/tech, sports, visual arts — plus specialty tracks (Equestrian, Adventure, Leadership, and the performing-arts intensive 'Camp Up With People'). One- and two-week sessions for ages 6-17, with a big international camper and staff contingent that gives it a broader-than-Beltway feel.
providers.ages: 6-17 (Journey 6-11, Explorer 12-16; leadership for older teens)
providers.locations: 3586 Horizons Way, Harrisonburg VA (Shenandoah Valley, ~2.25 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: From ~$1,975/week (2026); specialty programs (e.g., Equestrian) up to ~$4,000/week; $375 non-refundable registration fee per camper per session; monthly payment plans through May 29
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: One- and two-week sessions through summer 2026; registration for each session closes 10 days before it starts
providers.status_2026: Season underway with rolling registration until 10 days before each session — late-July and August sessions may still be bookable, check dates/rates page

reg_2027.opens: rolling (register any time; monthly payment plans run through late May)
reg_2027.notes: One of the few sleepaways where genuinely late registration is possible (up to 10 days before a session). Specialty tracks like Equestrian cap early. Verified via camphorizons.com indexed pages. Marked NoVA-region as the notable Virginia option; it is actually in the Shenandoah Valley.
