providers.fit_notes: Good fit for a kid who wants to sample everything or a family that decided on sleepaway camp late in the year. Horse-focused kids should compare the Equestrian track's steep price against Camp Rim Rock.

providers.name: Camp Levine
providers.description: The DC region's flagship music-and-arts day camp, run by the nonprofit Levine Music for nearly 1,000 campers per summer across four campuses. Kids of any musical background (no experience needed) rotate through singing, instrument play, musical theatre, dance, visual art, and games in two 3-week sessions; a competitive Teen Apprentice program lets rising 7th-12th graders assist classrooms and earn volunteer hours. Great for younger kids who like the arts broadly rather than one instrument intensively.
providers.ages: 4-12 (Pre-K through rising 6th grade; Pre-K campers must be 3.5 by Jan 15) for campers; rising 7th-12th grade for Teen Apprentices
providers.locations: DC NW campus (2801 Upton St NW), DC SE campus at THEARC (1901 Mississippi Ave SE, Session 1 only), MD campus at Music Center at Strathmore (5301 Tuckerman Ln, North Bethesda), VA campus at West Falls (Falls Church)
providers.price_detail: 2026: Session 1 (3 weeks) $1,512 full day (~$504/wk); Session 2 $1,599 (~$533/wk); half day Pre-K/K at Strathmore $1,056/$1,092. Before care (8-9:30am) and after care (3:45-6pm) extra.
providers.hours: 9:30am-3:45pm full day; 9:30am-1pm half day (Pre-K/K Strathmore only); before care from 8am, aftercare to 6pm
providers.extended_care: Yes - before care 8-9:30am and aftercare 3:45-6pm for additional fee
providers.financial_aid: Yes - formal tuition assistance program; the 2026-season application opened Nov 21, 2025, and families are encouraged to apply when enrolling
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: June 22-July 10 (no camp July 3); Session 2: July 13-31
providers.status_2026: Session 1 complete; Session 2 begins July 13 (tomorrow as of snapshot). Camp fills quickly and sessions sell out - join the waitlist if a class is full; contact 202-686-8000 x1080 for last-minute openings.

reg_2027.opens: Early winter - tuition assistance applications open in late November (Nov 21 for the 2026 season) with enrollment following in winter (Dec-Jan); exact 2027 open date not yet posted
reg_2027.notes: First-come online enrollment per campus; Levine's own site warns camp 'fills quickly and classes/sessions will sell out' - waitlists open by spring, so register early. Teen Apprentice is a separate competitive application (form + references + interview). Apply for tuition assistance at the same time you enroll (application opens late November).

providers.fit_notes: Best for arts-curious kids 4-12 who want variety, not conservatory rigor - genuinely beginner-friendly. Not for teens seeking instrument intensives (see Summer at Levine) or kids who want a single-discipline deep dive.

providers.name: Camp Live Out Loud (Camp LOL) - Ivymount Outreach
providers.description: A Saturday-morning summer program run by Ivymount, the region's nationally known special-education school in Rockville, serving youth and young adults ages 6-30 of all ability levels. Ivymount-trained educators lead activities in music, movement, sports, games, socialization, and the arts. Think of it as expert-led social recreation and skill-building from autism-education specialists rather than a full-day camp.
providers.locations: Ivymount School, 11614 Seven Locks Rd, Rockville MD 20854
providers.price_detail: Fees not published in search-indexed sources — contact Ivymount Outreach
providers.hours: Saturday mornings during summer (number of weeks varies by year — roughly six; confirm with Outreach)
providers.sessions_2026: Saturday-morning sessions in summer 2026 (exact dates not verified)
providers.status_2026: Likely underway; contact outreach@ivymount.org about openings

reg_2027.opens: spring (contact Outreach office; no public opening date)
reg_2027.notes: Ivymount Outreach matches participants to the right program by conversation rather than open registration. Details verified via the Ivymount Outreach program page (search-indexed) — confirm dates and fees directly.

providers.fit_notes: For autistic kids/teens/young adults (including adults up to 30, rare in this market) whose families want expert-led social skill-building. Saturday-only — does not solve weekday childcare. Ivymount's weekday extended-school-year programs are generally limited to enrolled students.

providers.name: Camp Moss Hollow
providers.description: A 90-year-legacy overnight camp in the Blue Ridge foothills (Markham, VA) serving primarily low-income and at-risk kids ages 7-14 from the DC area — rustic cabins, a pool, a two-acre fishing lake, hiking, arts, and a positive-youth-development curriculum. The long-running 'Send a Kid to Camp' campaign (famously championed by the Washington Post) underwrites fees so that cost is not a barrier for any DC family.
providers.price_detail: Heavily subsidized via Send a Kid to Camp; many DC campers attend free or near-free (income-based)
providers.locations: 12494 Moss Hollow Rd, Markham, VA (70 miles west of DC); serves DC-area children, historically majority east-of-the-river
providers.hours: One-week residential sessions
providers.financial_aid: Send a Kid to Camp donations fund camperships; families apply through Family Matters of Greater Washington and pay on an income-based scale (often nothing)

reg_2027.opens: Spring (typically March-May) through Family Matters of Greater Washington
reg_2027.notes: Register directly with Family Matters; campership funding is requested during registration. Over 1,000 kids attend each summer across sessions, but individual weeks cap — call early in spring 2027, especially for the popular mid-summer weeks.

providers.sessions_2026: One-week overnight sessions through June-August 2026, ages 7-14
providers.status_2026: 2026 sessions running; late-summer weeks sometimes have space — call Family Matters
providers.fit_notes: The classic first sleepaway camp for DC kids whose families can't pay market rates — ACA-accredited and genuinely rustic. Not for kids wanting specialized instruction (it's a traditional camp).

providers.name: Camp Rim Rock
providers.description: A classic all-girls sleepaway camp on 750 acres in Yellow Spring, WV (about 2 hours from DC) with a famous horseback riding program plus swimming, arts, sports, and campfire tradition — a multi-generation favorite of Washington families. Core sessions are two weeks (combinable to 4 or 6), with one-week Riding Camps and a one-week Mini-Camp in June for first-time rising 1st-3rd graders. Girls choose their own activity schedule.
providers.ages: 6-16 (Mini-Camp for rising 1st-3rd first-timers; general camp through age 16)
providers.locations: Yellow Spring, WV (Hampshire County, ~2 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: From ~$2,100 (1-week, per third-party listing); 2-week sessions higher. $1,000/session deposit at enrollment; balance due April 1. WV sales tax extra. Official rate table not captured in indexed copies.
providers.financial_aid: Sibling 5% and refer-a-friend ($150) discounts; need-based aid not advertised — ask camp
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: Three 2-week General sessions (combinable), 2 one-week Riding sessions, 1-week Mini-Camp (June)
providers.status_2026: Season underway; popular riding weeks fill early — contact camp about any late-session openings

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling; returning families re-enroll first); deposit due Feb 1, balance April 1
reg_2027.notes: Horse-crazy girls should book riding add-ons early — riding sessions are the first to sell out. Combining Riding + General sessions earns a $200 discount. Verified via camprimrock.com indexed pages; exact tuition unverified.

providers.fit_notes: For girls who want horses and single-gender tradition within an easy drive. Mini-Camp is a purpose-built first sleepaway. Not for kids seeking coed or one-week general sessions (minimum general session is two weeks).

providers.name: Camp Riverview (DPR Free Overnight Camp)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DC's best-kept secret: a completely FREE week-long camp on the Potomac River in Scotland, Maryland (southern MD, near where the Potomac meets the Chesapeake), exclusively for DC-resident kids 8-13, offered as both overnight and day camp with boating, fishing and crabbing, ATV trail rides, environmental science, campfires and outdoor adventure. For many DC kids it is their first night away from home, at zero cost - an extraordinary deal run by the parks department.
providers.locations: Camp Riverview, Scotland, MD (Potomac River waterfront); open to residents of all DC wards
providers.price_detail: Free for DC residents, including overnight lodging and meals
providers.hours: Week-long sessions; full-service overnight camp with a day-camp option
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions from June 22 through August 7, 2026 (season ends Aug 7 - earlier than the DPR day-camp calendar)
providers.status_2026: 2026 lottery (Apr 27-May 4) is over, but DPR launched in-person registration for remaining spots mid-summer - the season runs only through Aug 7, so late-July/early-August weeks are the last chance; call (202) 673-7647 or check the splash page

reg_2027.opens: late April (2026 lottery ran at noon Apr 27 through May 4)
reg_2027.notes: Separate from the February day-camp lottery - a second, later lottery many families miss, run with the same random-selection process. DC residency required and verified. If you miss the lottery, watch for DPR's in-person registration announcements for unfilled weeks in June/July.

providers.fit_notes: For adventurous DC kids ready for a week away (a day-camp option exists for kids not ready to sleep over); not for kids who need daily parent contact. Free overnight camp is nearly unique in the region. Dates verified against the splash page and DPR press release via search-indexed copies.

providers.name: Camp Shakespeare
providers.description: The Tony-winning Shakespeare Theatre Company's two-week summer camps where kids 6-11 and teens 12-18 spend their days on voice, movement, acting, text analysis, and real stage-combat training, building toward performing scenes from a featured play each session. Returning teens can audition for a three-week Intensive. Sessions run in Penn Quarter (downtown DC) and McLean, VA.
providers.ages: 6-18 (youth 6-11; teens 12-18; returning teens audition for 3-week Intensive)
providers.locations: STC facilities in Penn Quarter, Washington DC, and a McLean, VA site — location varies by session, check when registering
providers.price_detail: 2026 summer session tuition not verified on accessible pages (spring-break camp was $465/week as a reference point); non-refundable deposit $250 per registration ($125 for one-week camps). Sibling, multi-week, and military discounts
providers.financial_aid: Limited scholarship funds by application (Camp Shakespeare Scholarship Application on STC site)
providers.hours: Shorter than a standard camp day: Youth (6-11) 9:00am-3:00pm; Teens roughly 10:00am-4:00pm (10:00am-2:30pm in some sessions). Mon-Fri; no camp July 3
providers.extended_care: Not verified; ask CampSTC@ShakespeareTheatre.org — plan for the short day
providers.sessions_2026: Youth: Session I June 21-July 1 (A Midsummer Night's Dream), Session II July 5-15 (As You Like It), Session III July 18-29 (Macbeth); parallel teen sessions plus 3-week teen Intensive. Detailed logistics emailed to registered families by May 22, 2026
providers.status_2026: Session III (from July 18) upcoming as of mid-July — contact STC for remaining space; earlier sessions complete

reg_2027.opens: January (typical; not verified)
reg_2027.notes: Teen Intensive requires an audition and prior Camp Shakespeare experience. Scholarship application is separate and earlier. Double-check which sessions are DC vs McLean before booking, and note the shorter-than-9-to-4 camp day when planning work coverage.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for word-loving kids — text analysis and stage combat give it real substance, and no prior Shakespeare is expected. Kids who mainly want to sing and dance will be happier at a musical-theatre camp. Note the day ends at 3pm (youth) — a logistics gotcha vs 9-4 camps.

providers.name: Camp Sitar (Sitar Arts Center)
providers.description: Adams Morgan's renowned community arts center runs a multi-week, full-day summer arts camp where elementary and middle schoolers build their own schedule from studio arts, acting, music, dance, darkroom photography, and digital arts, plus field trips and performances. Tuition is on a sliding scale with 80% of seats reserved for families below 60% of area median income — the most affordable serious arts camp in DC.
providers.ages: elementary and middle school (roughly 5-14); broader Sitar programs serve 0-24
providers.locations: 1700 Kalorama Rd NW, Washington DC (Adams Morgan)
providers.price_detail: Sliding fee scale; Sitar's stated policy is that no one is turned away for inability to pay. 80% of enrollment reserved for families under 60% AMI
providers.hours: Full-day program
providers.financial_aid: Core to the model: sliding scale for all, deep subsidy for low/moderate-income families
providers.sessions_2026: Six-week full-day summer program (multiple sessions), per the Camp Sitar 2026 brochure
providers.status_2026: FULL — Camp Sitar 2026 is at capacity; a waitlist form is open and families are contacted as space frees up

reg_2027.opens: March (historic brochures say 'reserve your space in March'; spring registration announced via Sitar's channels)
reg_2027.notes: Fills to a waitlist well before summer — 2026 was full by early March. Watch sitarartscenter.org and their Instagram in February, and have income documentation ready for sliding-scale pricing.

providers.fit_notes: A genuine multi-arts immersion at a price almost any family can manage — extraordinary value and a warm, diverse community. Because affordability is the mission, middle/high-income families should expect limited seats and register the moment it opens.

providers.name: Camp St. Charles
providers.description: Maryland's flagship Catholic overnight camp, on the Wicomico River in Southern Maryland (Newburg, ~90 minutes from DC), running 1- and 2-week coed sessions since 1952 with swimming, horseback riding, archery, riflery, ropes course, boating, tubing, and daily faith life woven in. This is the traditional sleepaway-camp experience DC Catholic families have sent kids to for generations.
providers.locations: Newburg MD (Southern Maryland, Wicomico River) — draws heavily from the DC region
providers.price_detail: Overnight-camp pricing per 1- or 2-week session; $100 early-bird discount if paid in full by Jan 31; multi-session and sibling discounts
providers.financial_aid: Campership tuition-assistance program for qualifying families
providers.sessions_2026: 1- and 2-week overnight sessions June-August
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-summer session availability possible — check dates-rates page

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (registration for the following summer opens in autumn; early-bird pricing runs through Jan 31)
reg_2027.notes: Popular sessions and girls' weeks can fill by spring; registering by the January early-bird deadline is the norm for returning families. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting a faith-centered classic sleepaway camp at 1-week starter length for kids as young as 6. Explicitly Catholic — daily prayer and Mass are part of camp life.

providers.name: Camp Tall Timbers
providers.description: A coed traditional overnight camp in High View, WV — right at the 2-hour mark from DC and long marketed specifically to Washington families, with round-trip transportation from Dulles included in the registration fee. Sessions run 2, 3, 5, or 7 weeks with 40+ activities including horseback riding, sports, waterfront, and arts. It occupies the 'full-service private sleepaway, but closer and less clubby than the New England camps' niche.
providers.ages: 7-16 (coed)
providers.locations: High View, WV (~2 hr from DC); transport from Dulles included
providers.price_detail: Tuition not captured in indexed copies (expect private-sleepaway range, $1,500+/wk); deposits $250 (2-wk) / $500 (3-7 wk); $150 registration fee for 2-week session includes linens, towels, Dulles transport, t-shirt. Tuition covers riding, laundry, gratuities.
providers.financial_aid: Sibling discount $200 (2-wk session); refer-a-friend $100 each; need-based aid not advertised
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: 2, 3, 5, and 7-week sessions, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; contact camp about second-half (late July-August) openings

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling enrollment; returning campers re-enroll at camp)
reg_2027.notes: First-come; 2-week minimum makes it a bigger commitment than Camp Letts but gentler than the 3-4 week camps. Included Dulles-area transportation is unusual and genuinely convenient for DC families. Verified via camptalltimbers.com indexed pages; tuition unverified.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting a polished coed private sleepaway close to DC with horseback included in tuition. No one-week option; not a budget choice.

providers.name: Camp Washington: Summer Day Camp at Mount Vernon
providers.org: George Washington's Mount Vernon (Mount Vernon Ladies' Association)
providers.description: A week of colonial life on George Washington's actual estate: archaeology, 18th-century music and games, horticulture, costumed interpreters, and the estate's heritage-breed animals, capped by a Friday family open house. Lunch, snacks, backpack, and t-shirt are included - rare for the price point.
providers.ages: Rising 4th-6th graders (roughly 9-12)
providers.locations: George Washington's Mount Vernon estate, 3200 Mount Vernon Memorial Hwy, Mt Vernon VA (~30 min south of DC, car required)
providers.price_detail: $400/session members, $460/session non-members; includes lunches, snacks, backpack, and t-shirt
providers.hours: 9 am-3 pm (drop-off from 8:45)
providers.sessions_2026: Two one-week July sessions (indexed pages show 'July 8-12' and 'July 22-26', which do not align to 2026 weekdays - likely stale from a prior season; confirm exact 2026 dates with Mount Vernon)

reg_2027.opens: Typically spring via mountvernon.org events calendar
reg_2027.notes: Only two small sessions, member discount ($60), and no bus access - registration is straightforward but capacity is tight. Members should watch the calendar from March.

providers.status_2026: Two July sessions scheduled; current availability unverified and the exact 2026 dates on indexed pages appear stale - call or email before planning.
providers.fit_notes: Great for history-loving kids and newcomers wanting the full 'colonial Virginia' experience; requires a significant drive from DC/MoCo with no aftercare, so hard for two-commuter families.

providers.name: Camp William B. Snyder (Cub Scout Day & Resident Camp)
providers.org: Scouting America, National Capital Area Council
providers.description: NCAC's 350-acre Cub Scout camp in Haymarket, VA with a water-slide pool, fishing/boating wetland, BB and archery ranges, and an air-conditioned dining hall — the 'starter' scout camp for younger kids. Offers short 3-4 night resident sessions (including a Bear-rank-specific week) and day camp weeks, sized for first-time-away-from-home campers.
providers.ages: Cub Scout ages (~K-5), incl. parent-and-child options for youngest
providers.locations: Camp William B. Snyder, 6100 Antioch Rd, Haymarket, VA 20169 (~45 min from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026 fees behind scoutingevent.com pricing pages (not indexed); short sessions priced per 3-4 night block
providers.hours: Resident sessions 3-4 nights; day camp weeks daytime

reg_2027.opens: winter (2026 registration was live by early year; first 200 registrants got swag)
reg_2027.notes: Must be a registered Cub Scout. Short-session format is the easiest first overnight in the region; popular June weekends fill first.

providers.sessions_2026: Resident sessions June 18-21, June 26-28, July 9-12, 2026 (4 sessions incl. Bear Camp); day camp weeks per portal
providers.status_2026: All listed 2026 resident sessions have concluded as of July 12; contact camp for any remaining summer programming
providers.fit_notes: Gentlest overnight on this list (3 nights, AC dining hall, pool). Scouting membership required; sessions are early-summer only, so plan for June.

providers.name: Camp Woodend (Nature Forward)
providers.description: The region's classic naturalist day camp, run on Nature Forward's 40-acre Woodend Sanctuary in Chevy Chase, with weekly themed camps for pre-K through high school built around creek stomping, habitat hikes, and real environmental-science content. Counselors are experienced environmental educators (8:1 child-teacher ratio) and the vibe is gentle, screen-free, and genuinely educational rather than sporty. This is the go-to for a kid who loves bugs, birds, and mud more than ball games.
providers.ages: Pre-K through grade 12 (K-8 core camps; grades 9-12 via Teen Naturalist Training)
providers.locations: Woodend Sanctuary, 8940 Jones Mill Rd, Chevy Chase, MD 20815
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates in the 2026 catalog PDF (natureforward.org); member discount applies. Comparable published rates: 4-day spring camp $420 member/$485 nonmember; extended day $140/week. Exact summer weekly rates not directly verified via indexed copies.
providers.hours: 8:45am-3:45pm (drop-off window 8:45-9:15am, pick-up 3:45-4:10pm); extended camp to 5:00pm for grades 1-6
providers.extended_care: Aftercare until 5:00pm for grades 1-6 (approx. $140/week per catalog); NOT offered for kindergarten camps; no before-care
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships available — contact the Camp Director; membership lowers fees
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 22 - August 21, 2026, grouped into blocks (6/22-7/10, 7/13-7/31, 8/3-8/21)
providers.status_2026: Season underway. Popular weeks fill and carry free waitlists (48-hour claim window when a spot opens; staff say most camps see at least one cancellation) — contact the registrar at 301-652-1851 for current August openings.

reg_2027.opens: Late January (2026 registration opened January 22); catalog posts in December
reg_2027.notes: First-come online at a published date; members get discounted rates and the most popular K-2 weeks fill within days. Join Nature Forward before registration day and be online at opening time. Free waitlists are worth joining.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for curious, outdoorsy kids who don't need high-adrenaline programming; not the pick for a kid who wants sports, pools, or big-group energy. Woodend has no swimming, and kindergarten camps have no extended-day option.

providers.name: Camps Airy & Louise
providers.org: Camps Airy & Louise (Aaron and Lillie Straus Foundation)
providers.description: Historic (founded 1922) brother-sister Jewish overnight camps in the Catoctin Mountains barely an hour-plus from DC — Camp Airy for boys in Thurmont, Camp Louise for girls in Cascade, with joint activities between them. Grades 2-12 with both long sessions and short starter sessions for rising 2nd-5th graders, plus generous new-camper incentives (up to $900 off, first-come). The single-gender structure and century of tradition give it a distinct old-school warmth.
providers.ages: 7-17 (entering grades 2-11/12; short sessions for rising 2nd-5th)
providers.locations: Camp Airy: Thurmont MD; Camp Louise: Cascade MD (~1.25 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: Tuition not published in verifiable indexed copies (expect Jewish-overnight-camp range, roughly $1,000+/wk); full tuition due by Feb 1 for that summer; 2.4% credit-card fee; new campers up to $900 off (first-come, not need-based)
providers.financial_aid: Camperships (need-based scholarships) via airylouise.org/dates-registration/financial-support-camperships/, plus One Happy Camper eligibility for first-timers
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: Long sessions (grades 2-11) plus 1st/2nd short sessions (rising 2nd-5th) and a NEW 3rd short session for new rising 2nd-3rd graders
providers.status_2026: Season underway; 2026 enrollment closed (full tuition was due Feb 1)

reg_2027.opens: late summer-fall 2026 (2027 sign-ups begin around Camper-In-Training Day events in the fall)
reg_2027.notes: Register in fall for best session choice and to catch new-camper incentives (up to $900, first-come). Cancellation fees escalate monthly from Jan 1. The short sessions are a gentle sleepaway on-ramp for 7-9 year olds. Verified via airylouise.org indexed pages; tuition figures not verifiable.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting single-gender Jewish overnight camp with deep tradition and a shorter drive than most sleepaways. Baltimore-DC blended community. Kids wanting coed camp should look at Capital Camps instead.

providers.name: Capital Camps
providers.description: THE Jewish overnight camp of the Washington community — a 425-camper pluralistic camp in Waynesboro, PA (about 1.5 hours from DC) where a large share of DC/MoCo Jewish kids spend their summers together. Sessions run 2 to 7 weeks for grades 3-12 with classic camp program plus strong Jewish community life. Expect to see your kid's school friends there; the social continuity is a big part of the draw.
providers.ages: 8-17 (entering grades 3-12; 2-week starter option for rising grades 3-5)
providers.locations: 12750 Buchanan Trail East, Waynesboro PA (~1.5 hr from DC); DC-area bus transport
providers.price_detail: Full summer (7 weeks, Jun 21-Aug 9, 2026) $8,000; shorter sessions priced proportionally (~$1,100+/wk). Application fee plus deposit at registration.
providers.financial_aid: Need-based aid via Clarity platform (apply within 14 days of enrollment; early deadline Nov 16 for fall registrants); One Happy Camper grants of $500-$1,000 for first-time campers in 2+ week sessions
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: June 21 - Aug 9, 2026; Session 1 (4 wks, with 2-wk option for rising 3rd-5th), Session 2 (3 wks), full summer 7 wks
providers.status_2026: Season underway (ends Aug 9); popular sessions filled long ago — 2026 essentially closed

reg_2027.opens: early fall 2026 (returning campers first, then new; financial-aid deadlines start early November)
reg_2027.notes: Register in the fall with deposit, then file financial aid within 14 days — aid is reviewed only for deposited campers. Popular grade cohorts fill by winter. Cancellation fees escalate from January. Verified via capitalcamps.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: The default choice for DC-area Jewish families wanting their kids' school/synagogue community at camp. Pluralistic (all levels of observance). First-time-camper grants make the 2-week starter session a lower-risk entry. Not for families seeking a secular setting.

providers.name: Capital Fencing Academy Summer Camps
providers.description: A competitive fencing academy on Nebel Street in North Bethesda whose summer camps continue skill development after regular classes end in June, with games and drills for club fencers and pre-season intensive elite sabre camps for tournament-track athletes. More performance-oriented than DC Fencers Club — this is where committed young sabre fencers train.
providers.ages: Youth through teens; elite camps for competitive fencers
providers.locations: 12180 Nebel St, North Bethesda MD
providers.price_detail: Varies by camp level (recreational weeks vs. elite intensives); contact for rates
providers.hours: Varies by camp
providers.sessions_2026: Summer camps run after June classes end; pre-season elite sabre intensives in late summer
providers.status_2026: Summer camps running; contact club for remaining space

reg_2027.opens: Spring (with the club's summer schedule)
reg_2027.notes: First-come registration through the club; current students get the inside track, and the academy encourages every enrolled fencer to do at least one camp week to keep class placement — so club families book early.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids already fencing or ready to commit to sabre competitively; total beginners will find DC Fencers Club's camps a gentler entry.

providers.name: Capitol City Circus School Summer Camp
providers.description: DC's only real circus school, in Navy Yard, where campers spend a week on flying trapeze, aerial arts, juggling, and trampoline, then pick a specialty and build a routine for a Friday performance. A two-week intermediate camp serves returning campers, and an advanced camp offers focused training for dedicated young acrobats. The rare camp where a total beginner genuinely flies on a trapeze by week's end — capacity is small, and registration opens February 1.
providers.ages: 6 and up for all-levels camp weeks (most campers roughly 6-14); intermediate/advanced camps for returning or experienced campers; other youth programs span 12 months-16
providers.locations: 10 I St SE, Washington, DC 20003 (Navy Yard, near South Capitol & I St SE)
providers.hours: Week-long day camp; exact hours not verified — see site
providers.price_detail: Pricing not published in sources I could verify — contact the school
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly all-levels sessions, plus a two-week intermediate camp for returning campers and an advanced camp for experienced young acrobats
providers.status_2026: Not verified as of mid-July 2026; small program that historically fills after its February opening — call for cancellations

reg_2027.opens: February 1
reg_2027.notes: The niche appeal (flying trapeze in the city) plus tiny capacity means popular weeks go quickly after the Feb 1 opening — set a calendar reminder. Note the camp page URL still uses the 'summer-camp-2025' slug even though the page is titled SUMMER CAMP 2026 — it is the current page.

providers.name: Capitol Debate Summer Camp at American University
providers.description: A nationally known two-week debate and public-speaking intensive on the American University campus, with day (commuter) and residential (overnight) options for middle and high schoolers. Veteran coaches (many with 15-30 years experience) run practice debates with lengthy individual feedback — built for kids who want real confidence and argumentation skills, not just a taster.
providers.ages: Middle and high school (roughly 11-18)
providers.locations: American University, 4400 Massachusetts Ave NW, Washington DC (residential campers stay in Letts Hall); also virtual programs
providers.price_detail: 2026 commuter tuition $3,299 for the two-week session (weekdays plus Saturday, 9am-5pm; lunch available for purchase); residential costs more (includes Letts Hall housing and dining). Optional coaching add-on packages ~$499-$899.
providers.financial_aid: Not verified; they market early-bird and package discounts
providers.sessions_2026: July 12-23, 2026 (two weeks) at American University; multiple virtual sessions also offered
providers.status_2026: The AU session started July 12 — effectively closed for 2026; virtual sessions may still have space

reg_2027.opens: Fall of the prior year (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: Straight online enrollment (no application or audition) with early-bird pricing tiers; residential spots are more limited than day spots. Book by winter for the cheapest rate and guaranteed residential housing.

providers.fit_notes: For kids genuinely interested in speaking/argument — two weeks is a big commitment. Newcomers should compare with the free WUDL institute before paying four figures; Capitol Debate offers more polish and a residential option.

providers.name: Capitol Hill Arts Workshop (CHAW) Summer Camp
providers.description: Capitol Hill's beloved neighborhood arts center runs multi-arts summer camps (visual art, theater, music, movement) for pre-K through high school, with every week ending in a Friday demonstration for families. Full days run 9-5 with before/after care, all materials included — one of the few true full-coverage arts camps in DC, which is why Hill families race for spots.
providers.ages: Pre-K through high school (banded by age; half-day options for youngest)
providers.locations: 545 7th Street SE, Washington DC 20003
providers.price_detail: 2026 tuition not verified from official copy; tuition assistance and payment plans available
providers.hours: 9am-5pm full day; before/after care extends the day
providers.financial_aid: Tuition assistance and payment plans; CHAW's ethos is that no child is turned away for inability to pay
providers.sessions_2026: June 22-26, July 6-10, July 13-17, July 20-24, July 27-31, Aug 3-7 (no camp week of June 29)
providers.status_2026: Registration opened Feb 10 at noon and CHAW camps historically fill by spring; scattered openings possible — not verified as of July 12

reg_2027.opens: mid-February (Feb 10 at 12:00 noon in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Set a calendar alarm for the noon opening — this is one of the Hill's fastest-selling camps. Email brian@chaw.org about aid before registration so you're ready to enroll the day it opens.

providers.fit_notes: The classic choice for Capitol Hill families wanting an artsy, community-rooted camp with real 9-5 coverage. Multi-arts sampler format — kids wanting deep single-discipline training (serious ceramics, filmmaking) will outgrow it.

providers.name: Capitol Hill Cooperative Nursery School & The Hill Preschool Summer Camps
providers.org: Capitol Hill co-op preschool community (CHCNS; The Hill Preschool)
providers.description: Capitol Hill's co-op preschools run small neighborhood summer sessions: CHCNS (60+ years old, ages 2.5+) does classic Hill preschooling with walking field trips to Eastern Market, Yards Park splash pools, and the Botanic Garden, while The Hill Preschool offers summer camp for children up to age six. These are intimate, parent-involved programs — the opposite of a big commercial camp — and among the only true under-4 options on the Hill.
providers.ages: 2.5-6 (CHCNS from 2.5; Hill Preschool to 6)
providers.locations: Capitol Hill, Washington DC (CHCNS and The Hill Preschool campuses)
providers.price_detail: Session-based preschool tuition; co-op structure keeps costs below commercial camps — confirm current rates with each school
providers.financial_aid: Co-op participation reduces cost; formal aid varies by school — ask
providers.sessions_2026: Summer sessions June-July (school-specific week blocks)
providers.status_2026: Mid-July: most preschool-camp sessions underway or finished; contact schools directly about any remaining weeks

reg_2027.opens: late winter (roughly February-March), enrolled and alumni families first
reg_2027.notes: Capitol Hill preschool camps fill largely through word of mouth and school communities; newcomers should email both schools in January-February. Official sites verified; exact 2027 dates not yet posted.

providers.fit_notes: For Hill families wanting a walkable, familiar-feeling first camp for a toddler/preschooler. For ages 3-10 with full-day needs on the Hill, see Adventures on the Hill (summercampdc.com) instead.

providers.name: Casey Trees TreeWise Camps & High School Summer Crew
providers.description: DC's urban-forestry nonprofit runs two youth tracks: TreeWise spring-break and summer nature camps for kids (STEAM-themed weeks of science experiments, crafts, neighborhood hikes, and a field trip focused on trees, pollinators, and watershed care) and a paid High School Summer Crew that employs teens to water, weed, mulch, and monitor Casey Trees plantings across the city. Both are rooted in real conservation work rather than generic camp programming.
providers.ages: TreeWise camps: elementary (roughly 5-12); Summer Crew: high school
providers.locations: Casey Trees HQ, 3030 12th St NE (Brookland), with work/field sites across DC
providers.price_detail: TreeWise camp pricing not verifiable via indexed copies; High School Summer Crew is a PAID summer job
providers.hours: Camp-day hours for TreeWise; work-day schedule for crew
providers.financial_aid: Crew pays teens; camp aid not found
providers.sessions_2026: Summer camp weeks per the 2026 camps page; crew runs the summer work season
providers.status_2026: 2026 camp registration opened in January; current week availability unverified. Crew hiring for 2026 is closed.

reg_2027.opens: January (2026 spring-break + summer registration opened together in early January)
reg_2027.notes: Camps: first-come online, modest capacity. Crew: application in late winter — teens can also route via DC's SYEP window (late Jan-early Mar). Email education@caseytrees.org to get announcements.

providers.fit_notes: TreeWise suits science-y city kids who like purposeful outdoor time; the crew is one of the few paid outdoor summer jobs for DC teens. Not a full-summer childcare plan.

providers.name: Catholic University Pre-College Summer Programs
providers.description: A slate of career-taste pre-college programs on the Brookland campus: Engineering New Frontiers and the mentored Da Vinci Scholars research experience, Experiences in Architecture (3 weeks), a Summer Nursing Institute, writing workshops, and the residential High School Drama Institute (July 13-31, 2026; $4,750 all-inclusive). Smaller and more personal than the GW/Georgetown giants, on a Metro-accessible Red Line campus.
providers.ages: High school students (most programs target rising juniors and seniors)
providers.locations: Catholic University campus, 620 Michigan Ave NE (Brookland-CUA Metro)
providers.hours: Varies by program; residential and commuter options depending on program
providers.price_detail: Drama Institute $4,750 for 3 weeks incl. housing/meals (2026); engineering/architecture/nursing program fees not verified
providers.financial_aid: Not verified — ask each program office
providers.status_2026: 2026 programs running per individual calendars (Drama Institute July 13-31); most application windows closed by late spring

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring, varies by program
reg_2027.notes: Each program (engineering, architecture, nursing, drama, writing) has its own application and deadline — check the specific program page in January. Smaller cohorts than GW/Georgetown mean earlier caps but also better mentor access.

providers.sessions_2026: Program-specific: 1-week (nursing) to 3-week (architecture, drama) sessions
providers.fit_notes: Great for teens testing a specific career (nursing and architecture programs are rare finds locally). Catholic identity is present but programs draw students of all backgrounds.

providers.name: CCACC Academy Summer Camp (Chinese immersion & enrichment)
providers.org: Chinese Culture and Community Service Center (CCACC)
providers.description: Rockville's Chinese community center runs a large 9-week summer camp (71 courses across eight learning areas in 2026) for ages 5-13, anchored by Mandarin immersion and Chinese culture alongside academics, arts, and enrichment electives. The most substantial Mandarin summer option in Montgomery County, at community-center prices.
providers.locations: CCACC Academy, 9400 Key West Ave, Rockville MD 20850
providers.price_detail: A-la-carte course pricing (listings from ~$60 per course block up to ~$350); total weekly cost depends on schedule assembled. Early-bird discount for registration before March 31.
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - August 21, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season running through Aug 21; course-based enrollment means later-summer blocks often still open — check shop.ccaccacademy.org

reg_2027.opens: Early in the year, with early-bird discount deadline March 31
reg_2027.notes: Online first-come via their shop site; register before 3/31 for the early-bird discount. The a-la-carte course model takes some assembling — call them to build a full-day Mandarin-heavy schedule.

providers.fit_notes: Great for heritage speakers and beginners alike, and one of the few options running to late August. The course-menu structure suits families who want to customize; less turnkey than a standard camp.

providers.name: CentroNía Summer Camp
providers.description: Columbia Heights' bilingual (Spanish/English) early-education institution runs summer camp/care for preschool and young school-age children at its Columbia Road campus, extending its dual-language, multicultural model through the summer. CentroNía primarily serves working immigrant families and accepts DC childcare subsidy, making it one of few truly affordable bilingual summer options for the youngest kids.
providers.ages: Roughly 3-10 (early childhood through early elementary; core strength is preK)
providers.price_detail: Fees offset by DC child care subsidy vouchers for eligible families; market-rate for others — call for current rates
providers.locations: 1420 Columbia Rd NW, Washington DC (Columbia Heights); also operates DC public early-learning sites
providers.hours: Full-day child-care hours (designed for working parents)
providers.financial_aid: Accepts DC subsidy (via OSSE child care voucher); sliding arrangements for enrolled families — apply through CentroNía's enrollment office

reg_2027.opens: Spring for summer; year-round enrollment for childcare seats
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment works like childcare admissions, not camp registration — call 202-332-4200, ask for summer enrollment, and start subsidy paperwork early since OSSE voucher approval takes weeks.

providers.sessions_2026: Full-summer bilingual camp/care at Columbia Heights campus, 2026
providers.status_2026: Enrollment-based; mid-summer starts sometimes possible if seats open — call
providers.fit_notes: Best for families with preschoolers/young elementary kids who need full-day bilingual summer care, especially subsidy-eligible families. Details were only verifiable via search-indexed copies, not the live site — confirm everything by phone.

providers.name: Children's Chorus of Washington (year-round + planned summer camp)
providers.description: DC's premier treble choral program: six auditioned ensembles plus two open-entry, no-audition classes for K-3 (Prep and Training Ensemble), performing at major venues around the city. It does not run a 2026 summer camp, but has announced plans for a Northeast DC site and a summer camp beginning in FY2027 - one to watch for 2027 planners; meanwhile fall enrollment/auditions happen in spring and late summer.
providers.ages: K-grade 12 (K-2 no audition; grades 3-12 by audition)
providers.locations: Rehearsals in NW Washington DC (planned second site in NE DC)
providers.price_detail: Season tuition varies by ensemble; means-tested financial aid via Federal Financial Assistance Calculator; Choral Scholars Program covers full tuition, transportation, and uniforms for select students
providers.hours: Weekly rehearsals during school year (not a day camp)
providers.financial_aid: Yes - means-tested aid plus full-ride Choral Scholars Program
providers.sessions_2026: No summer 2026 camp; 2026-27 season enrollment via spring/summer auditions
providers.status_2026: No summer camp exists in 2026. Interest forms open for fall auditions; mid-season auditions typically in January.

reg_2027.opens: Watch childrenschorusdc.org - first-ever summer camp planned for FY2027 alongside a new NE DC site; season auditions run spring/late summer
reg_2027.mechanism: audition (grades 3+); open enrollment for K-2 classes
reg_2027.notes: K-2 Prep/Training classes are open-entry (no audition) - an easy on-ramp. The planned FY27 summer camp details were only verifiable via indexed strategic-plan summaries; confirm directly before counting on it.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who love to sing: K-2 entry is genuinely no-audition, and serious singers grade 3+ get top-tier choral training. Not a summer childcare solution in 2026 - included mainly for 2027 planning.

providers.name: Children's Science Center Lab Camps
providers.description: Weekly themed STEM camps (Cosmic Creations, Epic Engineering, Superhero Species, Museum Makers) inside Northern Virginia's interactive children's science museum at Fair Oaks Mall, with experienced STEM educators and small groups. A NoVA option worth the drive for elementary kids who love the museum format.
providers.ages: Grades 1-5
providers.locations: Children's Science Center Lab, 11948 Fair Oaks Mall, Fairfax VA
providers.hours: Full-day weekly camps (confirm exact hours with camps@childsci.org)
providers.price_detail: Not verified on official page
providers.financial_aid: Nonprofit with access programs; camp-specific aid not verified
providers.status_2026: 2026 season running (June 22 - Aug 21 themed weeks); July/August weeks were listed — email for current openings

reg_2027.opens: typically late winter
reg_2027.notes: First-come; small group sizes mean favorite weeks cap early. Members often get early or discounted access — worth joining if you plan to register.

providers.sessions_2026: Themed weeks June-August 2026 (e.g., Cosmic Creations June 22-26, Epic Engineering July 6-10, Museum Makers Aug 17-21)
providers.fit_notes: Sweet spot is grades 1-5 museum-lovers. DC/MoCo families should weigh the Fairfax commute against KID Museum, which serves a similar kid closer in.

providers.name: Circle Yoga Summer Camp
providers.description: The DC area's dedicated kids-yoga-and-mindfulness studio (founded 2003 as Budding Yogis, now a cooperative) a block from Chevy Chase Circle, running full-day camp weeks for ages 6-12 that mix yoga, mindfulness, crafts, and outdoor play — explicitly free from competition and pressure. The standout non-competitive movement option in the region: ideal for sensitive, anxious, or simply gentle kids for whom sports camps and performance camps both miss.
providers.ages: 6-12 (kids' classes from age 2 during the year)
providers.locations: 3838 Northampton St NW, Washington, DC 20015 (1 block south of Chevy Chase Circle); some camp programming listed at 4200 Davenport St NW
providers.price_detail: $495/week
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions summer 2026 (specific weeks not verified)
providers.status_2026: Not verified as of mid-July 2026 — small program; check the site for open weeks

reg_2027.opens: spring
reg_2027.notes: Small cohorts at a single studio, so favorite weeks go to studio families first; sign up in spring rather than waiting.

providers.name: City Blossoms Summer Saturdays & Open Times
providers.description: Free, kid-driven creative garden programming at City Blossoms' community green spaces — including the Girard Children's Community Garden (Columbia Heights), the Studio garden (Brightwood Park), and the Farm at Kelly Miller (NE) — with garden art, recipe-making, and open exploration. Summer Saturdays run weekly mornings in July-August alongside multi-day-a-week Open Times April-October. It's neighborhood gardening-as-play rather than a camp.
providers.ages: All ages with caregiver; programming aimed at young children through elementary
providers.locations: Girard Children's Community Garden (Columbia Heights NW), Studio garden (Brightwood Park NW), Farm at Kelly Miller (NE DC)
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.hours: Summer Saturdays ~10am-12pm; Open Times five days/week April-October (check calendar)
providers.sessions_2026: Ongoing April-October open programming; Summer Saturdays series in mid-summer
providers.status_2026: Open now, drop-in — check the calendar page for this week's garden hours.

reg_2027.opens: No registration — drop-in
reg_2027.notes: Nothing to plan; just show up during posted hours. Useful as a free supplement between camp weeks.

providers.fit_notes: Not childcare — a caregiver stays. Best for younger kids and families who want a recurring free neighborhood ritual; exact 2026 Summer Saturdays dates were only partially verifiable.

providers.name: City Kids Wilderness Project (Ranger Program + Broken Arrow Ranch summer)
providers.description: A completely free seven-year outdoor-adventure pathway for DC youth: kids join as 6th graders and progress as a cohort through backpacking, whitewater kayaking, climbing, and summers at the organization's Broken Arrow Ranch in Jackson Hole, Wyoming. Once accepted, families never pay for anything, including travel. One of the most remarkable free opportunities in the city — and correspondingly selective and commitment-heavy.
providers.ages: Entry in 6th grade; cohort continues through high school (roughly 11-18)
providers.price_detail: Free including gear, food, and travel to Wyoming
providers.locations: DC base at 2437 15th St NW (Columbia Heights); summer programming at Broken Arrow Ranch, Jackson, WY
providers.hours: Summer season June 1 - Aug 17, 2026 (multi-week overnight trips by cohort); year-round DC weekend/afterschool activities
providers.financial_aid: N/A — program is 100% free for accepted DC youth; recruitment focuses on under-resourced communities

reg_2027.opens: Applications for the next Ranger class (2026-27) open in fall/winter; join the newsletter to be notified
reg_2027.notes: Apply the year your child is in 5th/6th grade — this is the only entry point. ~130 youth total across all seven years, so seats per class are limited. Email recruitment@citykidsdc.org about school visits and eligibility.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer season June 1 - August 17, 2026 in Jackson, WY (enrolled cohorts only)
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer restricted to already-enrolled Rangers; new-family action item is the fall application for the next class
providers.fit_notes: For 5th/6th grade families ready to commit to a multi-year program and comfortable with a child traveling to Wyoming. Not a sign-up-for-a-week camp; you cannot join mid-pathway.

providers.name: City of Gaithersburg Summer Camps (Camp Discovery, Imagination, Endeavor & specialty camps)
providers.org: City of Gaithersburg Parks, Recreation & Culture
providers.description: Gaithersburg runs a full municipal camp system out of the Activity Center at Bohrer Park: traditional day camps by grade (Camp Imagination and Camp Discovery for younger kids, Camp Endeavor for ages 11-14) plus Summertime Tots/Kindergarten camp and specialty weeks in sports, dance, visual/performing arts, STEM and skateboarding. The signature perk: campers swim at the Water Park at Bohrer Park twice a week, and Camp Endeavor sessions include field trips or SSL projects.
providers.ages: 2-5 (tots/pre-K) through grades 9-12 (teen programs); core day camps roughly K-8, Camp Endeavor 11-14
providers.locations: Activity Center at Bohrer Park, 506 S. Frederick Ave, Gaithersburg, plus other city facilities; swimming at the Water Park at Bohrer Park
providers.hours: One-week sessions starting the fourth week of June; typical day-camp hours with before/after care options (verify per camp)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered for core camps — verify per listing on RecXpress
providers.price_detail: Per-week resident/nonresident pricing on RecXpress; 2026 prices not independently verified. Payment plans available for select programs before May 15.
providers.financial_aid: City residents who qualify may apply for financial assistance before registering; payment plans available

reg_2027.opens: mid-February (2026: residents Wed Feb 11, open registration Wed Feb 18)
reg_2027.notes: One-week resident head start matters for the water-park camps and skateboarding weeks. Apply for financial assistance BEFORE registration opens — it must be in place at signup. Teens can also apply for paid junior-counselor and volunteer roles with the camps.

providers.status_2026: Season underway (began fourth week of June); week-by-week availability on RecXpress (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Excellent value for upcounty families — the twice-weekly water-park swimming is the standout. Resident vs nonresident pricing gap is meaningful.

providers.name: City of Takoma Park Camps (Camp Takoma & specialty camps)
providers.org: City of Takoma Park Recreation Department
providers.description: Takoma Park's small, community-flavored recreation camps: flagship Camp Takoma (ages 6-13, 9am-4pm, weekly themes like 'Camp Around the World') plus New Ave Adventure Camp and rotating specialty weeks in basketball, dance, theater and arts. Tiny capacity and a fiercely loyal local following mean it books out almost immediately each February.
providers.ages: 6-13 (Camp Takoma); other camps vary
providers.locations: Takoma Park Community Center (7500 Maple Ave) and city recreation facilities
providers.hours: 9am-4pm Mon-Fri, weekly sessions (Camp Takoma)
providers.extended_care: Historically offered for a fee — verify per listing on the city's ActiveNet
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on Takoma Park ActiveNet; 2026 prices not independently verified (city camps are typically well below private rates)
providers.financial_aid: Recreation Department scholarships for Takoma Park residents; scholarship awardees got a dedicated early registration window (noon, Feb 11, 2026)

reg_2027.opens: February (2026: scholarship awardees at noon Wed Feb 11, general registration following)
reg_2027.notes: Traditional camp 'fills up fast' per the city itself — treat the February opening like a ticket drop. Residents should apply for scholarships in advance to use the early window. Very small capacity per week.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; at least one Camp Takoma session (July 27-31 'Camp Around the World') is marked FULL. Check ActiveNet for any remaining weeks (verified via search-indexed listing, July 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Beloved, granola, neighborhood-scale camp — ideal for Takoma Park families who want walkable and affordable. Outsiders face both late registration and long odds.

providers.name: CityDance Summer Dance Camps & Intensives
providers.description: The region's biggest nonprofit dance organization runs themed week-long summer dance camps for kids at its Strathmore (North Bethesda) and Rockville studios, plus a Friendship Heights DC option, with age bands starting at 5-7 and 8-10 and running up through the teens, mixing ballet, jazz, hip hop, tap, and crafts. A separate audition-based Conservatory track offers serious pre-professional summer intensives at Strathmore for ages 8-20. Camps suit total beginners; the intensives are for trained dancers.
providers.ages: 5-16 for day camps (bands roughly 5-7, 8-10, 11-13 plus teen weeks); Conservatory intensives 8-20 by audition
providers.locations: CityDance Center at Strathmore, 5301 Tuckerman Ln, North Bethesda; Rockville studio at 11711 Parklawn Dr; Friendship Heights DC studio
providers.hours: Camps 9:00am-3:00pm; Conservatory intensives Mon-Fri 10:00am-4:30pm
providers.price_detail: Full-day camp weeks $435 (discounted to $390 with early/promo registration); half-day options roughly $120-225/week depending on time slot (verified via search-indexed copies of official pages; confirm with studio)
providers.financial_aid: None found for studio camps; CityDance DREAM program provides free training for DC students during the school year — ask about summer aid
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly studio camps into mid-August (e.g., Camp H 'Musicals & More' Aug 3-7, Camp J 'Dance Stars' Aug 10-14, Dance Team Kick-Off Camp Aug 16-20); Conservatory intensives July 13-31 and Aug 17-21
providers.status_2026: As of mid-July 2026: video auditions still being accepted for 2026 summer intensives (Aug 17-21 week upcoming); August studio-camp weeks appear bookable — check individual camp weeks

reg_2027.opens: typically mid-winter (January-February)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come for camps; video or in-person audition for Conservatory intensives
reg_2027.notes: Weekly camps historically keep availability into summer, especially August; popular young-kid weeks at Strathmore go earlier. Intensive placement requires an audition (auditions run late fall through spring), so plan the audition video in winter. Early/promo registration knocks camp price from $435 to $390.

providers.name: Code Ninjas Summer Camps (Germantown)
providers.description: Neighborhood coding 'dojo' whose summer camps use Minecraft, Roblox, robotics, and YouTube-creator themes to teach real programming basics in a playful, video-game-belt-progression format. CAUTION: the Germantown dojo — the only close-in Montgomery County location — is listed as permanently closed on Yelp as of April 2026, and the nearest verified Maryland dojos (Frederick, Ellicott City, Arundel Mills) are well outside the DC/MoCo area.
providers.locations: Code Ninjas Germantown, 12974 Middlebrook Rd, Germantown MD — APPEARS PERMANENTLY CLOSED (Yelp listing marked closed, updated April 2026; could not verify on codeninjas.com, which blocks automated access). No Bethesda/Rockville/Silver Spring dojo exists; nearest verified MD dojos are Frederick and Ellicott City. Check codeninjas.com/locations before planning.
providers.hours: National camp format: Mon-Fri 9:00 am - 3:30 pm full day; half-day AM 9-12 or PM 12:30-3:30
providers.price_detail: National 2026 camp pricing: $250/week half-day, $225 each when booking AM+PM together for a full day (~$450/week); local pricing varies by dojo
providers.extended_care: Morning care from 8:00 am and afternoon care until 6:00 pm on request, $20/hour (per national camp info)
providers.status_2026: Germantown dojo appears permanently closed (Yelp, April 2026) — likely NOT running 2026 camps. National 2026 camp season runs June 1 - July 31 at operating dojos; nearest verified MD dojos (Frederick, Ellicott City) are outside the coverage area. Verify directly before relying on this option.

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring (rolling; franchises post schedules by early spring)
reg_2027.notes: First-come rolling enrollment per week at operating dojos; rarely sells out months ahead. For 2027, first confirm whether any Montgomery County dojo exists at codeninjas.com/locations — the Germantown franchise appears to have closed.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, June 1 - July 31, 2026 (national window; local operation unconfirmed — dojo likely closed)
providers.fit_notes: Fun-first and beginner-friendly where a dojo operates, but with Germantown apparently closed there is no close-in DC/MoCo location — treat this as a lead to verify, not a bookable option. Consider Mad Science, Play-Well, or iD Tech instead.

providers.name: CommuniKids Language Immersion Summer Camp
providers.description: Themed-week immersion camp for the youngest set (2.5-7) in Spanish, French, or Mandarin at four NW DC locations (Tenleytown, two at Cathedral Commons, Glover Park) plus Falls Church — every week a new theme blending outdoor play, art, music, and hands-on learning, all in the target language. One of very few true-immersion options for preschoolers and early elementary kids.
providers.ages: Spanish 2.5-7; French 2.5-6.5; Mandarin 3-6.5
providers.locations: Tenleytown (Spanish), Cathedral Commons x2 (Spanish/French), Glover Park (Spanish/Mandarin) — all NW DC; also Falls Church VA
providers.price_detail: $360-$550 per week depending on location and schedule; balance due May 1
providers.sessions_2026: Seven weeks of camp; register for one week or several
providers.status_2026: Season running; some late-July weeks typically bookable — check communikids.com per location

reg_2027.opens: Winter (rolling; enrolled preschool families get natural priority awareness)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online per location/language; Mandarin and French rooms are smaller and go first. Balance due May 1. This is the on-ramp if your child is too young for WISummer or Rochambeau's older groups.

providers.fit_notes: Strictly for little kids (max ~7) — no experience needed, immersion by osmosis and play. Families wanting a full-day school-age program should look at Isabella & Ferdinand or WISummer.

providers.name: Congressional Camp
providers.description: A classic full-service day camp on Congressional School's 40-acre wooded campus in Falls Church, VA — one of NoVA's institutions, with pools, day camp by age group, 'Great Adventures' travel camps, and specialty camps across 10 summer weeks. Regionally famous enough that many DC and MD families ride its extensive Northern Virginia bus network to get there.
providers.locations: Congressional School, 3229 Sleepy Hollow Road, Falls Church VA (plus satellite specialty-camp campuses)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates published on congocamp.org/dates-rates but not independently verified (page blocked); $100 registration fee plus $100 per-camp deposit; Day Camp and Great Adventures sell as two-week sessions. Older directory listings show roughly $201-350/week equivalents but may be stale.
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm core day
providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered (verify current hours with camp)
providers.sessions_2026: 10 weeks; Day Camp and Great Adventures in mandatory two-week sessions; one-week specialty camps
providers.status_2026: Season in progress; discounted single weeks appeared on deal sites (CertifiKID) in June 2026, suggesting some late-summer availability — contact camp directly

reg_2027.opens: Typically opens in winter (January); exact 2027 date not yet published
reg_2027.notes: Two-week session commitment for the core day camp is unusual for the area — plan your calendar around it. Bus cluster stops across NoVA with route maps online. Lunch available.

providers.fit_notes: Strong traditional-camp feel with swimming and acreage; two-week minimum sessions and a Virginia location make it a commitment for DC/MD families despite the buses.

providers.name: Counselor-in-Training (CIT) Program at ATMTC Academy
providers.description: Teen volunteers help professional staff run one of the region's oldest theatre day camps, managing groups of up to 24 younger campers at historic Glen Echo Park and satellite Montgomery County sites. CITs commit to full camp days (9am-4pm) for a full session, earning 63-70 MCPS SSL hours per 9-10 day session. Thrives: theatre kids aging out of camp who want real responsibility with children while staying in the drama world.
providers.ages: 13-17 (high-school-age volunteers; exact grade cutoff on application — verify)
providers.price_detail: Volunteer position — no fee; earns SSL hours (70 hrs per 10-day session, 63 per 9-day)
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm Mon-Fri for the full camp session
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park (7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD) plus offsite Montgomery County camp locations
providers.sessions_2026: Session-by-session alongside ATMTC summer camps, roughly mid-June through August
providers.status_2026: 2026 CIT applications opened in spring; mid/late-summer sessions may still have openings — contact the Academy directly (site could not be opened directly; verified via search index).

reg_2027.opens: roughly February-March, alongside camp registration
reg_2027.notes: Apply per session; MCPS SSL pre-approved organization. Popular sessions (musical theatre weeks) take CITs fastest — apply when camp registration opens. ATMTC also runs teen performance intensives if your teen wants to be on stage rather than assisting.

providers.fit_notes: Unpaid and a real full-day commitment — teens who want a paycheck should look at Headfirst or MBSYEP instead. Excellent stepping stone to paid counselor jobs at 16+.

providers.name: CREATE Arts Center Summer Art Camp
providers.description: Downtown Silver Spring's community art center (also known for its art therapy programs) runs a friendly weekly visual-arts camp for ages 6-12, with teens 13-14 attending as counselors-in-training. With before/after care, per-day registration options in early weeks, and mid-range pricing, it is one of the most practical arts camps for working Silver Spring families.
providers.ages: 6-12 campers; 13-14 as counselors-in-training
providers.locations: CREATE Arts Center, downtown Silver Spring MD
providers.price_detail: $430/week in 2026 ($345 for the 4-day June 29 week; $90/day single-day option during Session 1); CIT fee $350/week; beforecare $9/day, aftercare $28/day
providers.hours: Full-day weeks; beforecare and aftercare extend the day
providers.financial_aid: Not verified on camp pages; CREATE has community-access programming — ask the registrar
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 15 through August 21, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season running; 2026 weeks were open for registration into the summer — contact registrar@createartscenter.org (301-588-2787) for remaining late-July/August space

reg_2027.opens: winter (2026 weeks were posted and refundable until May 1, implying early-year opening)
reg_2027.notes: Fills more gently than the Bethesda/Glen Echo giants — a realistic spring registration, and the per-day option is a rare flexibility. Full refunds (minus $50) only until May 1.

providers.fit_notes: A warm, no-pressure art camp with genuine working-parent logistics (before/aftercare, day rates) in walkable downtown Silver Spring, plus a CIT path for young teens. Kids seeking advanced technique or specialty media (wheel ceramics, glass) will want VisArts or Glen Echo instead.

providers.name: Creative Youth Sewing & Fashion Camps
providers.description: A dedicated kids' sewing school on St. Elmo Ave in downtown Bethesda offering week-long summer camps in sewing, fashion design, and crafting — hand stitches, machine skills, fabric layout and cutting, and design illustration, with all skill levels welcome. The go-to close-in Montgomery County answer for a kid who wants to make actual clothes.
providers.ages: camps roughly 8-12 (CY Summer Camp); classes serve ages 5-17
providers.locations: 4805 St. Elmo Avenue, Bethesda MD 20814
providers.price_detail: 2026 weekly tuition not verified; enrollment via Sawyer platform
providers.hours: Week-long day camps; campers bring a packed lunch
providers.extended_care: None — no before or after care offered
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sewing/fashion camps through summer 2026 (booked via Sawyer)
providers.status_2026: 2026 camps listed on their Sawyer registration page; week-by-week availability not verified — check creative-youth.com/camp

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring (camps posted on their Sawyer page; no publicized single opening date)
reg_2027.notes: Small studio with machine-limited capacity — book favorite weeks by early spring. Registration via hisawyer.com links from creative-youth.com.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for craft-loving 8-12s who want to leave camp with things they actually made and wear. No extended care and a packed-lunch setup means it works best for families with flexible pickup.

providers.name: Creator Camp (Washington DC)
providers.description: A national chain of short-format (2-3 day) creative-tech camps arriving in the DC market for 2026, with themes like LEGO stop-motion animation, 2D cartoon animation, YouTube content creation, special-effects and Hollywood filmmaking, game design, and music production for ages 6-13. A Summer Pass offers unlimited camps at a steep per-week discount.
providers.ages: 6-13 (some listings say 5-13 / 8-14 by theme)
providers.locations: Washington DC area location(s) for 2026 — exact venue not verified from official copy; see creatorcamp.org/washington-dc
providers.price_detail: Marketed 'as low as $150/week' via the unlimited Summer Pass; individual 2-3 day camp pricing not verified
providers.hours: 2-day and 3-day camp formats
providers.sessions_2026: Rolling 2-3 day sessions across summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Selling for summer 2026 on their site; availability not verified

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring (rolling online sales; Summer Pass promoted early in the year)
reg_2027.notes: Short sessions and rolling sales make this the flexible filler for gap days rather than a plan-in-January camp. Verify the actual DC venue and current-year reviews before booking — newer market entrant.

providers.fit_notes: Useful for patching odd weeks with fun animation/YouTube/game-design content at a low price point, especially for younger kids. Not a full-week, full-day childcare solution, and its DC operation is new and lightly documented — do your own diligence.

providers.name: Croydon Creek Nature Center Camps (City of Rockville)
providers.org: City of Rockville Recreation and Parks
providers.description: Rockville's own city-run nature camp at a 120-acre forest preserve tucked behind the Civic Center — creek stomping, live animals, hikes and nature crafts for ages 5-14, on a separate (and slightly later) registration calendar from the county's nature centers. A strong plan-B or first choice for Rockville families, with city-resident priority registration.
providers.ages: 5-14, age-banded by week
providers.locations: Croydon Creek Nature Center, 852 Avery Rd, Rockville, MD 20851 (Rockville Civic Center Park)
providers.hours: Varies by camp; typical day-camp hours ~9am-3pm with some half-day preschool/young-child weeks
providers.extended_care: Extended care offered for many Rockville camps — verify per listing in the city camp guide
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing in Rockville's REC1 system; 2026 prices not independently verified. Nonresidents pay a surcharge.
providers.financial_aid: City of Rockville offers financial assistance/scholarships for resident campers (see city camp guide)

reg_2027.opens: early-to-mid February (2026: Tue Feb 10 at 8:30am for Rockville residents, Thu Feb 12 for everyone)
reg_2027.notes: Resident-priority window is the whole game for popular nature weeks — city residents should register Feb opening morning. Non-Rockville families can still get in on general-registration day for less-subscribed weeks. Guide PDF posts in January.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; check the REC1 catalog for live open/full status by week (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Smaller and mellower than the county nature centers; walkable from Rockville Metro-ish neighborhoods. Nonresidents pay more and register later.

providers.name: D.C. United Summer Camps (Powered by Headfirst)
providers.description: The official MLS-club soccer day camps, operated by local powerhouse Headfirst, at fields across DC, Maryland, and Northern Virginia (Catholic University, National Cathedral School, and more). All abilities welcome from rising K through rising 8th grade, with specialty goalkeeper and striker tracks; the Headfirst operation is known for polished logistics and energetic college-age coaches. In 2026 D.C. United also launched an RDS Summer Select program for more competitive players.
providers.ages: Rising K - rising 8th grade (approx. 5-13)
providers.locations: Select sites across DC (incl. Catholic University, National Cathedral School), MD, and NoVA (Mater Dei, Flint Hill School)
providers.price_detail: Headfirst-operated camps typically run roughly $500-600/week; exact 2026 pricing varies by site/week (verify at registration)
providers.hours: 8:45 AM - 2:45 PM Mon-Fri
providers.extended_care: Afternoon Extended Day until 6:00 PM available
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 15 - August 14, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; registration remains open with remaining July and August weeks bookable at many sites (popular NW DC sites sell out first)

reg_2027.opens: Late fall / early winter (Headfirst opens next-summer registration around November-December with early pricing)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online registration by week and site; the most convenient DC locations and late-June weeks fill by spring, but capacity is large — mid-summer weeks often remain available into June.

providers.fit_notes: Great, low-pressure branded soccer camp for beginners through rec players who want a well-run week; competitive club players will want MSI Classic, Bethesda SC, or the new RDS Summer Select instead.

providers.name: Dance Institute of Washington Summer Programs
providers.description: A Columbia Heights institution founded by Fabian Barnes to make serious dance training accessible to Black and brown DC youth. Summer offerings are tiered: 'Summer Sizzler' introduces ages 3-6 to creative movement and ballet readiness in short weekday sessions, while the Summer Training Experience (ages 7-13 and 14-17, full weekdays with breakfast served) challenges serious dancers across ballet, modern, and other genres on a pre-professional track. Best for families who want rigorous training in a culturally affirming, community-rooted environment.
providers.ages: 3-6 (Summer Sizzler); 7-17 for the Summer Training Experience (bands 7-13 and 14-17)
providers.locations: 3400 14th St NW, Washington, DC 20010 (Columbia Heights)
providers.hours: Sizzler: 2 weekday late-afternoon sessions/week by age band; Summer Training Experience: Mon-Fri 10:00am-3:30pm with breakfast 9:00-10:00am
providers.price_detail: Pricing not published in sources I could verify — contact the studio
providers.financial_aid: DIW's mission centers on access and it has a long scholarship tradition; specific summer-aid terms not verified — email info@danceinstitute.org
providers.sessions_2026: Summer Sizzler (ages 3-6) twice-weekly sessions; Summer Training Experience for ages 7-13 and 14-17, with level-placement auditions before the program (held June 20, 2026)
providers.status_2026: Summer Sizzler registration closed for 2026 (studio directs inquiries to fall programming); Summer Training Experience underway after June 20 placement auditions

reg_2027.opens: spring (program page posted by late spring)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come for Summer Sizzler (ages 3-6); level-placement audition required for all Summer Training Experience registrants
reg_2027.notes: Summer Sizzler closed out before the season in 2026, so register in spring. Older dancers (7-17) must attend the level-placement audition — watch for the June audition date.

providers.name: Dance Loft on 14 Youth Summer Programs
providers.description: A community dance hub on upper 14th Street NW running standalone weekly camps: a gentle creative-movement/ballet camp with crafts and games for ages 3-6 (9am-3:30pm), plus tiered Junior, Intermediate, and Advanced full-day tracks for older dancers that add pointe, modern, variations, dance history, and yoga/Pilates as levels rise. A flexible, unfussy option — enroll one week or several — with real aftercare, which is rare among DC dance camps.
providers.ages: 3-6 for the young-dancer camp; Junior/Intermediate/Advanced tracks for roughly 7-18 by level
providers.locations: 4618 14th St NW, Washington, DC (upper 14th St corridor); satellite classes at Fort Totten
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:30pm
providers.price_detail: Weekly pricing not captured in verified sources — see registration page
providers.sessions_2026: Standalone weekly sessions through summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Weekly enrollment model; late-July/August weeks likely still bookable as of mid-July 2026 — confirm on site

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring
reg_2027.notes: Each week stands alone, so families can grab single weeks late; the small ages-3-6 camp fills faster than the older tracks.

providers.name: DAR Museum Summer Camps (Colonial Camp and textile/quilt camps)
providers.org: DAR Museum / National Society Daughters of the American Revolution
providers.description: Small living-history camps at the DAR's Memorial Continental Hall near the White House: the signature Colonial Camp puts kids ~8-12 in historically accurate costume to explore colonial life through food, song, dance, stitchery, and a field trip, and the museum has also run textile-arts camps (From Fiber to Fabric; Quilt Camp for ages 10-17). A hidden-gem option for craft- and history-minded kids.
providers.ages: Colonial Camp ~8-12; quilt/textile camps ~10-17
providers.locations: DAR Museum, 1776 D St NW (near the White House/Foggy Bottom)
providers.price_detail: Current pricing not verifiable; historical fee was ~$350/week with early-pay discount - confirm current rates with the museum
providers.hours: Historically 9 am-3 pm
providers.sessions_2026: A live summer-camp page exists at dar.org/museum/education/summer-camp but 2026 session details could not be retrieved

reg_2027.opens: Check dar.org/museum/education/summer-camp in late winter/spring
reg_2027.notes: Tiny program run by museum education staff; registration is low-tech and capacity is small. Email/call the museum in February to get on their radar.

providers.status_2026: Camp program appears active (dedicated page on dar.org) but 2026 dates, price, and availability could not be verified via indexed copies - low confidence, verify directly.
providers.fit_notes: Suits kids who enjoy dress-up, crafts, and hands-on history in a small-group setting. Included because it genuinely exists, but every 2026 detail needs direct confirmation.

providers.name: Day Eight Middle School Summer Writing Camp
providers.description: A FREE five-week creative-writing day camp for rising 6th-9th graders at the Shepherd Park Library (NW DC), funded by Learn24 and the Friends of the Shepherd Park Library. Each day starts with poem recitation and study of model poems by working writers; campers' poems are published in a real book at summer's end. Curriculum by poets Robert Bettmann and Regie Cabico with guest writers-in-residence - capped at just 17 campers per week.
providers.ages: rising 6th-9th graders (roughly 11-14)
providers.locations: Shepherd Park Neighborhood Library, 7420 Georgia Ave NW, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Free for DC residents (thanks to Learn24 + Friends of Shepherd Park Library funding); enroll for 1 or 2 weeks
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:30am-4:30pm
providers.sessions_2026: June 29 - July 31, 2026, weekly enrollment (max 17 campers/week)
providers.status_2026: In session now (through Jul 31); with a 17-camper weekly cap late weeks may be full - email Day Eight to ask about remaining July weeks

reg_2027.opens: spring (2026 registration was announced open by late April)
reg_2027.notes: Free + tiny capacity = fills quickly once word spreads. Watch dayeight.org and the Learn24 listings from March; register the week it opens. DC residency required for the free rate.

providers.fit_notes: A standout for word-loving middle schoolers - real published output, professional poet instructors, zero cost. Kids who hate writing will not enjoy a full day of it. Site blocked from direct fetch; verified via search-indexed copies of dayeight.org.

providers.name: DC Boys State & Girls State
providers.org: American Legion Department of the District of Columbia
providers.description: The District runs its own American Legion Boys State (with a companion Girls State track) — the same build-a-government leadership week as the famous state programs, scaled to DC and held in the city rather than on a distant campus (2026: June 27-July 2 at the Yunus Emre Institute Turkish Cultural Center). DC high schoolers are ineligible for Maryland's program, so this is their route to the Boys/Girls Nation pipeline. Smaller and lower-profile than Maryland's, which can mean better selection odds for motivated students.
providers.ages: Rising high school seniors (approx. 17)
providers.locations: 2026 session: Yunus Emre Institute Turkish Cultural Center, Washington, DC
providers.price_detail: American Legion programs are post-sponsored and typically free to families — DC fee structure not explicitly published; confirm at registration
providers.sessions_2026: June 27-July 2, 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 session just concluded (ended July 2) — registration for 2026 is over.
