reg_2027.opens: Registration info posted at dcboysstate.com; DC Legion programs typically open applications in winter/spring for the late-June week
reg_2027.notes: Application through the DC Legion; eligibility criteria set by the DC department and may differ from other states. Small program — contact the DC Legion early in junior year.

providers.fit_notes: The DC-resident counterpart to Maryland Boys/Girls State. VERIFICATION FLAG: verified only via search-indexed copies of dclegion.com/dcboysstate.com; cost and exact 2027 timeline unconfirmed — treat details as provisional.

providers.name: DC Fencers Club Summer Camps
providers.description: A dedicated fencing center in Silver Spring's Forest Glen neighborhood running seven straight weeks of beginner-through-advanced day camps: footwork, technique, group lessons, games, and bouting alongside nationally ranked fencers, with all gear provided. It's the region's most accessible on-ramp into a niche Olympic sport — no experience or equipment needed, and before/aftercare is offered.
providers.ages: 7-16, beginner through advanced
providers.locations: 9330 Fraser Ave, Silver Spring MD (Forest Glen)
providers.price_detail: Weekly full-day camp typically ~$450-550/week incl. equipment (verify current rates); before/aftercare extra
providers.hours: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM Mon-Fri
providers.status_2026: Camps in session; multiple late-July and August weeks typically still open mid-summer

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring (schedule posted early in the year)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online; a niche-sport camp that fills steadily but rarely instantly — a realistic late-spring booking. Gear is provided, which removes the biggest barrier to trying fencing.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for curious kids (especially ones who love Star Wars/swashbuckling) and for serious youth fencers needing summer training; kids who hate structure and drilling may find the sport's discipline demanding.

providers.name: DC Fire Action / Fire & EMS youth summer program
providers.description: Part summer camp, part paid summer job: about two dozen DC youth ages 16-20 spend the summer learning firefighting and EMS skills with DC FEMS through the Marion Barry Summer Youth Employment Program. It feeds into the department's full Fire Cadet Program (ages 18-21), which pays cadets while they earn EMT and Firefighter I & II certifications — a genuine career pipeline for DC residents.
providers.ages: 16-20 (summer program); 18-21 (Fire Cadet Program)
providers.price_detail: Free and paid — participants earn MBSYEP wages
providers.locations: DC Fire and EMS training facilities, Washington DC
providers.hours: Full summer weekday schedule via MBSYEP

reg_2027.opens: MBSYEP application opens late January/February 2027 for DC residents 14-24
reg_2027.notes: Apply through summerjobs.dc.gov and seek the DC FEMS 'Fire Action' placement; only ~24 slots so express interest early and follow up with FEMS. DC residency required.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 cohort running within MBSYEP dates (late June-early August)
providers.status_2026: 2026 cohort filled via winter application; closed mid-summer
providers.fit_notes: For DC teens 16+ seriously curious about fire/EMS careers — physical, hands-on, and paid. Not open to suburban MD/VA residents.

providers.name: DC Public Library Discover Summer - Kids
providers.description: DCPL's free, all-summer reading-and-learning celebration (June 1-Aug 31): kids pick up a Bingo Board at any neighborhood library (or track in Beanstack), complete reading and activity tasks, and earn giveaways plus two Washington Nationals tickets for finishing. Branches layer on free storytimes, STEM programs and events all summer - the 2026 theme ties into America 250. Not childcare, but the best free enrichment scaffold in the city.
providers.ages: 0-12 (kids track; all-ages challenge)
providers.locations: All 26 DC Public Library locations plus online via Beanstack
providers.price_detail: Completely free; prize is a pack of two Nationals tickets plus giveaways while supplies last
providers.hours: Library open hours; events scheduled throughout the day
providers.extended_care: n/a - drop-in, parent/caregiver accompanies younger kids
providers.sessions_2026: June 1 - August 31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Running now - join anytime through August 31; no registration barrier

reg_2027.opens: June 1 (annually)
reg_2027.notes: No lottery, no signup crunch - grab a Bingo Board at any branch or register on Beanstack from June 1. Nothing to plan in advance; it never fills.

providers.fit_notes: For every family - zero cost, zero competition, works alongside any camp schedule. It is a challenge program plus event calendar, not supervised care.

providers.name: DC Public Library Discover Summer - Teens (Know Your Power)
providers.description: The teen arm of DCPL's free summer program: creative workshops in music, visual arts, writing and digital media at branches citywide, anchored by the Know Your Power Teen Art Contest (ages 13-18) where teens create poetry, art, music or multimedia on civic issues and can win laptops, cameras, guitars and graphics tablets. Free access to working artists and real prizes.
providers.ages: 13-18 (some workshops 10+)
providers.locations: DC Public Library branches citywide incl. MLK Central Library
providers.price_detail: Free, including workshops with professional artists; contest prizes include laptop, digital camera, guitar, graphics tablet
providers.hours: Scheduled workshop events, typically afternoons; see branch event calendar
providers.sessions_2026: June 1 - August 31; Know Your Power contest submissions through August 31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Active now - teens can still join workshops and enter the contest through Aug 31

reg_2027.opens: June 1 (annually)
reg_2027.notes: No advance registration needed; individual workshops may ask for event signup on the DCPL events calendar. Contest deadline is end of August - start projects early summer.

providers.fit_notes: For creative teens who would never enroll in a 'camp' - a free on-ramp to art, music production and media with legitimate prize incentives. Not supervised all-day programming.

providers.name: DC Sail Kids Set Sail
providers.description: Community-sailing summer camp on the Anacostia at Diamond Teague Park (Navy Yard/Capitol Riverfront), where beginners ages 7-15 crew 18-foot Flying Scots with an instructor aboard and intermediates (12-15) move to double-handed FJs and RS Fevas with racing and navigation. Eight one-week sessions run all summer. As the city's nonprofit sailing school it's notably mission-driven, with scholarship support.
providers.ages: 7-15 (beginner); 12-15 (intermediate/advanced, needs 3 prior sessions or equivalent)
providers.locations: Diamond Teague Park Piers, 1st & Potomac Ave SE (Capitol Riverfront); office 650 Wharf St SW
providers.price_detail: DC Sail's materials peg a camp day at ~$90 (≈$450/week); exact 2026 session pricing on each session's registration page — not fully verified
providers.hours: Roughly 9/10am-4pm; no verified extended care
providers.financial_aid: Youth scholarships are a core program — DC Sail fundraises specifically to send kids to KSS
providers.sessions_2026: 8 one-week sessions, mid-June through mid-August (Session 2 began June 29)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; late-July/August sessions (5-8) may still have space — session pages are live on dcsail.org. Unverified; call 202-547-1250.

reg_2027.opens: Winter (sessions typically posted January-February)
reg_2027.notes: First-come per session online; beginner weeks with younger kids fill first but it's less of a frenzy than the big MoCo camps. Ask early about scholarship applications.

providers.fit_notes: Genuine on-the-water instruction in the middle of the city; kids should be comfortable around water (swim test standard). Metro-accessible (Navy Yard) — one of the easiest adventure camps for car-free families.

providers.name: DCYOP Summer Intensive (Premiere Youth Ensemble)
providers.description: The storied, access-focused DC Youth Orchestra Program (founded 1960, famous for training generations of DC musicians affordably) runs small summer intensives where string, wind, brass, and percussion students work daily with DCYOP faculty and conductors on repertoire and ensemble skills. Each week is capped at just 12 students, so it's closer to a chamber intensive than a big camp; the year-round program is the real pipeline and is one of the region's best music bargains.
providers.ages: Roughly 8-18; beginner through advanced instrumentalists (summer intensive targets students who already play)
providers.locations: DCYOP program sites in DC (historically Eastern High School area / Takoma; verify current summer site with DCYOP)
providers.price_detail: $450/week; $50 non-refundable deposit at registration; early drop-off (8am) +$50/week; tuition assistance available
providers.hours: 9:30am-3:30pm; optional 8:00am early drop-off
providers.extended_care: Early drop-off from 8am for $50/week; no late aftercare found
providers.financial_aid: Yes - tuition assistance program; DCYOP also lends instruments (first-come, subject to inventory)
providers.sessions_2026: July 20-24 and July 27-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Both 2026 sessions marked PROGRAM FULL. Registration ran until April 30 with only 12 seats per session.

reg_2027.opens: Late winter/early spring; 2026 registration closed April 30
reg_2027.notes: First-come with $50 deposit and a hard April 30 deadline - with 12 seats per week it fills well before the deadline. For year-round DCYOP (the better-known program), fall registration opens in late summer.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids already playing an orchestral instrument who want serious, affordable coaching in a tiny cohort. Complete beginners should look at DCYOP's year-round beginner classes instead of the summer intensive.

providers.name: Docs In Progress Youth Filmmaking Camps
providers.description: A documentary filmmaking nonprofit a block from the Silver Spring Metro that runs one- and two-week summer camps where kids work in small crews to plan, shoot, and edit real short documentaries around downtown Silver Spring, finishing with a mini film festival for families (films posted to YouTube). Cameras, editing gear, and training are all provided — the area's best entry point into nonfiction filmmaking.
providers.ages: grades 4-6, grades 7-8, and ages 13-17 in separate sessions
providers.locations: 8560 Second Ave, Silver Spring MD (1 block from Silver Spring Metro)
providers.price_detail: Current tuition not verified from official copy (older indexed listings ranged ~$375-575/week); confirm on docsinprogress.org
providers.hours: 9:30am-4pm
providers.sessions_2026: Grades 7-8: July 13-17; Grades 4-6: July 20-24; Ages 13-17: July 27-31 and Aug 3-7 (one-week), plus a two-week teen option
providers.status_2026: Late-July and August teen sessions were still listed as of early July; earliest sessions now underway — check remaining space directly

reg_2027.opens: late winter/early spring (sessions posted and ticketed via their site/Eventbrite)
reg_2027.notes: Small cohorts but a niche audience — fills moderately, and August teen weeks often have space into early summer. Scholarship application opens in spring; apply before enrolling if needed.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for curious, story-minded kids and teens — you don't need any filmmaking experience, and the documentary (not Hollywood) angle suits interview-loving extroverts and thoughtful observers alike. Metro-accessible, which is gold for NW DC/Takoma families.

providers.name: DOEE Green Zone Environmental Program (GZEP)
providers.org: DC Department of Energy & Environment (DOEE), via MBSYEP
providers.description: The environmental track inside DC's summer-jobs program: up to 250 youth ages 14-24 get PAID to learn about climate, energy and watershed protection and spend three days a week in the field building rain gardens, marking storm drains and restoring green space with partners like Anacostia Watershed Society, Casey Trees and the Student Conservation Association. Camp-like outdoor immersion, with a paycheck.
providers.ages: 14-24 (DC residents, via MBSYEP)
providers.locations: Classroom base (incl. UDC) plus field sites across DC watersheds; 2026 sessions ran 9am-4:30pm
providers.price_detail: Free and paid - participants earn MBSYEP wages
providers.hours: Roughly 9am-4:30pm program days during the six-week SYEP window
providers.sessions_2026: Runs with MBSYEP June 29-Aug 7, 2026 (UDC calendar shows GZEP days in July)
providers.status_2026: Running now with the 2026 SYEP cohort; entry closed with the March 6 SYEP deadline

reg_2027.opens: late January (through the MBSYEP application at summerjobs.dc.gov)
reg_2027.notes: Apply to MBSYEP when it opens and indicate interest in DOEE/GZEP placement; capacity ~250 so the environmental track can fill even though SYEP overall does not. Follow @DOEE.GZEP for placement announcements.

providers.fit_notes: Best fit for outdoorsy teens 14+ who want conservation work and a paycheck instead of a camp. Verified via search-indexed doee.dc.gov copies and UDC event calendar.

providers.name: DPR Aqua Day Camp
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: A swimming-centered DPR day camp at rec centers with pools, teaching breathing technique, stroke mechanics and water-safety awareness, with each session ending in a camper swim meet. One of the cheapest ways in the region for a kid to get near-daily swim instruction all summer.
providers.ages: 6-13 (grouped by age, e.g. 6-10 and 11-13 sections)
providers.locations: DPR aquatic facilities citywide (e.g. Deanwood and other indoor-pool rec centers)
providers.price_detail: Per two-week session; DPR 2026 camp fees run $135-$325 per session for DC residents depending on camp - exact Aqua Day Camp fee not verified (fee PDF blocks direct access). Reduced rates apply for qualifying residents.
providers.hours: Standard DPR camp day (~9am-5pm)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered at DPR camp sites for $20/session where available
providers.financial_aid: 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents
providers.sessions_2026: Four two-week sessions Jun 22-Aug 14; registration was via the Feb 2-16 lottery window
providers.status_2026: Lottery over; Sessions 3-4 (Jul 20-Aug 14) remaining - leftover spots first-come on the DPR portal (activity pages on WebTrac show live availability); pool camps fill very fast

reg_2027.opens: early February (in the main DPR camp lottery, Feb 2-16 in 2026); other DPR aquatics programs (learn-to-swim, swim league) opened mid-May
reg_2027.notes: Aqua Day Camp goes through the same February lottery as Discovery/Boost. Separately, DPR summer aquatics programs (learn-to-swim, summer swim league, youth water polo) open first-come in mid-May (May 12 at noon in 2026; all other summer programs May 13 at noon) - set an alarm, they clear in hours.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want to be in the water every day and parents who want real swim-safety skills, not just pool playtime. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov pages and live WebTrac activity listings; exact fees unconfirmed.

providers.name: DPR Boost Specialty Camps
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DPR's tween track: two-week specialty enrichment camps with an academic component, with 2026 offerings including Rocket Camp, Robotics Camp, Esports Camp, Journalism Camp, Improv Camp and CSI (forensics), plus daily physical activity like swimming and group fitness. A rare chance for middle-schoolers to try STEM, media or performance camps at parks-department prices instead of private-camp prices.
providers.ages: 11-13 (middle school / rising 6th-8th)
providers.locations: Selected DPR recreation centers citywide; specialty theme varies by site
providers.price_detail: DPR 2026 camp fees run $135-$325 per two-week session for DC residents; specialty Boost themes sit toward the upper end of that range (still roughly $70-$165/week). Reduced rates 50%/75% for qualifying DC residents; non-resident rate higher.
providers.hours: Roughly 9am-5pm camp day; before/after care availability varies by site
providers.extended_care: Before-care 8-9am / after-care 5-6pm offered at $20/session at most DPR camp sites
providers.financial_aid: 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents (apply before lottery)
providers.sessions_2026: Same four sessions as other DPR camps: Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-31, Aug 3-14
providers.status_2026: Lottery over; check the DPR portal for first-come leftovers in Sessions 3-4 (Jul 20-Aug 14) - specialty themes like Robotics and Esports fill first

reg_2027.opens: early February (same window as all DPR camps: Feb 2-16 in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Part of the single DPR February lottery - rank your preferred Boost themes/sites in the application. The most popular specialties (robotics, esports) are heavily oversubscribed; list backup sites. Residency verification and reduced-rate application must be done before the window opens. Results emailed the day after the lottery closes (Feb 17 in 2026).

providers.fit_notes: Best for tweens aging out of general day camp who want a themed camp without a $500/week price tag. Not a deep-dive academy - it is still a rec-center camp with an enrichment theme. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov copies and DPR's Boost splash page.

providers.name: DPR Discovery Camp
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: The backbone of DC's public summer-camp system: theme-based day camp at recreation centers citywide with morning meetings, arts and crafts, sports, field explorations, daily reading time (DEAR), and choice activities. At $135 per two-week session for DC residents it is dramatically cheaper than private camps, which is why the February lottery is one of the most competitive tickets in DC parenting.
providers.ages: 6-10 (rising grades 1-5 roughly)
providers.locations: Dozens of DPR recreation centers across all 8 wards
providers.price_detail: $135 per ~2-week session for DC residents (under $70/week); 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified residents; non-residents pay a higher rate. Before/after care $20 per session extra. (Older sources cite $110 - $135 is the figure on DPR's current pages.)
providers.hours: Roughly 9am-5pm; before-care 8-9am, after-care 5-6pm ($20/session, guaranteed if enrolled in Discovery)
providers.extended_care: Yes - before-care 8-9am and after-care 5-6pm, $20 per session; reduced rates do NOT apply to B&A fees
providers.financial_aid: 50% or 75% reduced rate for income-qualified DC residents; apply before the lottery opens (Feb 2 deadline in 2026)
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: Jun 22-Jul 2 (one day short for July 4), Session 2: Jul 6-17, Session 3: Jul 20-31, Session 4: Aug 3-14
providers.status_2026: Lottery over; Sessions 3 and 4 (Jul 20-Aug 14) still ahead. Unfilled spots are first-come-first-served on the DPR registration portal until filled; popular sites are waitlisted and waitlist offers do move as families decline (DPR makes 3 contact attempts, payment due within 2 business days)

reg_2027.opens: early February (2026: applications Feb 2-16, results announced Feb 17)
reg_2027.notes: All applications submitted during the window are weighted equally - no first-come advantage. Do the prep in January: create/verify your DPR account, confirm DC residency, and submit reduced-rate application before the window opens (both due by day one of the lottery in 2026). Results are emailed the day after the lottery closes. If not selected you land on a waitlist; remaining spots open rolling/first-come afterward. A DC Auditor report (Aug 2025) found the lottery made access more equitable than the old first-come scramble.

providers.fit_notes: The default DC-government camp for elementary kids - social, active, neighborhood-based, unbeatable price. Not for kids who need a specialized curriculum. Discovery Inclusion track supports campers with disabilities inside regular Discovery sites. Official dc.gov pages verified via search-indexed copies only.

providers.name: DPR Extended August Camps
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DPR's answer to the dreaded late-August childcare gap: extra camp weeks after the regular Session 4 ends in mid-August, bridging toward the DCPS school start at a smaller set of recreation centers. Registration runs outside the February lottery, making this one of the easier DPR camps to actually get into - if you catch the announcement.
providers.ages: 3-13 (mirrors DPR camp age bands; varies by site)
providers.locations: Selected DPR recreation centers (fewer sites than the main summer sessions)
providers.price_detail: Standard low DPR per-session fees with reduced rates for qualifying residents; exact 2026 fee not verified
providers.hours: Standard DPR camp day (~9am-5pm) with B&A care at participating sites
providers.financial_aid: DPR 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents
providers.sessions_2026: Roughly Aug 17 through late August (after Session 4 ends Aug 14), until DCPS starts; 2026 sites/dates announced mid-summer
providers.status_2026: Not yet confirmed for 2026 as of mid-July - DPR historically announces extended-August sites in mid-summer with first-come online registration opening in early-to-mid August (a past year opened Aug 12 at noon); watch dpr.dc.gov and DPR newsletters, this is the best remaining low-cost DPR option for late August

reg_2027.opens: mid-summer announcement; first-come registration has historically opened in early-to-mid August
reg_2027.notes: NOT part of the February lottery - DPR announces which rec centers will extend into late August partway through the summer, then registration is first-come online. Fewer families track this, so it is a genuine escape valve if you lost the lottery, but sites are limited; sign up for DPR email alerts and register the day it opens.

providers.fit_notes: For working parents staring down the gap between camp season and school. Confirm which rec centers participate each year. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov copies and a historical DPR bulletin only; details sparse.

providers.name: DPR Little Explorers Camp
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DC Parks and Rec's gentle first-camp experience for preschoolers at recreation centers across the city, with a theme-based curriculum, arts and crafts, games, daily reading time and a 1:4-1:5 staff-to-child ratio led by early-childhood staff. Ideal for potty-trained 3-5 year olds who have never done camp before. One of the cheapest full-day options for this age anywhere in the region.
providers.ages: 3-5 (pre-K; must be potty-trained). Related TR inclusion program 'Let's Play Together' serves ages 3-7 with and without disabilities
providers.locations: DPR recreation centers in every ward of DC
providers.price_detail: Per two-week session; DPR 2026 camp fees run $135-$325 per session for DC residents depending on camp (Discovery is $135) - the exact Little Explorers fee is in DPR's 2026 fee PDF, which blocks direct access. Non-residents pay a higher rate. Before/after care $20 per session extra.
providers.hours: Camp day roughly 9am-5pm; before-care 8-9am and after-care 5-6pm available for $20/session (guaranteed for Little Explorers and Discovery enrollees)
providers.extended_care: Yes - before-care 8-9am, after-care 5-6pm, $20 per session (not covered by reduced rates)
providers.financial_aid: DC residents can apply for 50% or 75% reduced rates based on income; must apply by the reduced-rate deadline (Feb 2 in 2026) before the lottery
providers.sessions_2026: Four sessions: Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-31, Aug 3-14
providers.status_2026: Lottery long closed; Sessions 3 (Jul 20-31) and 4 (Aug 3-14) still ahead - remaining slots are limited and first-come on the DPR online registration portal (dprsummercamp.com) until full; most sites are waitlisted (join waitlist via the '+' button on the activity)

reg_2027.opens: early February (2026 lottery ran Feb 2-16)
reg_2027.notes: Equal-weight lottery - applying on day one gives no advantage. CRITICAL prep: verify DC residency in your DPR account AND apply for reduced rates BEFORE the lottery opens (both were due Feb 2 in 2026). Results announced by email the day after the lottery closes (Feb 17 in 2026); declined/unclaimed spots go to the waitlist, then leftovers open first-come in spring. Non-residents may register but pay more and get no reduced rate.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting affordable, low-pressure, neighborhood-based care for a preschooler - not a specialty enrichment camp. Verified via official DPR pages and press releases as indexed by search (dc.gov blocks direct fetch).

providers.name: DPR Summer Aquatics Programs (Learn-to-Swim, Summer Swim League, Free Pools)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: Beyond camps, DPR runs a full free-to-cheap summer aquatics slate: learn-to-swim lessons, a citywide youth summer swim league, youth water polo, and dozens of free outdoor pools and spray parks open to residents all summer. This is how DC kids swim all summer for essentially nothing.
providers.ages: all ages (youth swim league roughly 5-18; lessons all ages)
providers.locations: DPR indoor and outdoor pools in every ward; spray parks citywide
providers.price_detail: Outdoor pools and spray parks free for DC residents (bring ID); program fees for lessons/league are nominal; exact 2026 fees not verified
providers.hours: Pool hours vary by site; programs typically weekday mornings/evenings
providers.financial_aid: Reduced rates apply to fee-based aquatics programs; pools are free
providers.sessions_2026: Programs run late June-August; registration opened Tue May 12, 2026 at 12pm
providers.status_2026: Pools and spray parks open now, free, no registration. Structured programs (lessons, league) largely full since May - check WebTrac for openings

reg_2027.opens: mid-May (May 12 at 12pm in 2026 for aquatics; all other programs the next day)
reg_2027.notes: Swim lessons are among the fastest-selling items DPR offers - be logged into your verified DPR account before noon on opening day. Free pool access needs no registration, just proof of DC residency at the gate.

providers.fit_notes: Not a camp - listed because free pools plus cheap lessons are the free-public backbone of a DC kid summer, especially for families patching together care. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov copies.

providers.name: DPR Supreme Teens (Free Teen Summer Programming)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: Free, no-cost teen programming at 15+ DPR recreation centers - activities, trips, sports and social programs for ages 13-18 that continue through the summer. Unstructured compared with a camp, but free, drop-in-friendly, and a real answer for teens who refuse 'kid camp' but need somewhere to be.
providers.locations: 15+ DPR recreation centers across DC
providers.price_detail: No cost
providers.hours: Varies by rec center; typically afternoon/evening blocks
providers.sessions_2026: Ongoing through summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Active now - teens can join at any participating rec center; no lottery, no fee

reg_2027.opens: year-round / rolling
reg_2027.notes: No lottery and no deadline - contact your nearest participating rec center or the DPR teens page. Pairs well with SYEP for teens who want both income and recreation.

providers.fit_notes: For teens who need free, flexible summer structure rather than an enrolled camp. Programming quality varies by rec center - visit one first. Verified via DPR splash page and search-indexed dc.gov copies.

providers.name: DPR Teen Career Camps
providers.org: DC DPR in partnership with DC Department of Employment Services (DOES)
providers.description: Career-exploration summer camps for DC teens run by DPR with the Department of Employment Services, connected to the city's summer-jobs ecosystem. Themes lean toward job readiness and career fields; DPR also trains teens as lifeguards for free through its aquatics division. Good for teens too old for regular camp but not ready for a full SYEP job placement.
providers.ages: teens (roughly 13-17); note DPR's main lottery camp system officially serves ages 3-13, so confirm each career camp's age band on DPRprograms.com
providers.locations: Selected DPR recreation centers
providers.price_detail: Many teen offerings are free or nominal because of DOES funding, but DPR did not publish a clear fee table; verify per camp on DPRprograms.com
providers.hours: Standard camp-day hours; varies by program
providers.financial_aid: DPR reduced rates apply to any fee-based teen offerings; DOES-funded slots are free
providers.sessions_2026: Follows DPR summer session calendar (Jun 22-Aug 14)
providers.status_2026: Check DPRprograms.com / the DPR registration portal for open teen career camp sections; teen offerings historically have more late availability than elementary camps

reg_2027.opens: mid-May via DPR's summer-program registration (May 13 at noon in 2026 for non-aquatics programs); DOES-linked programs align with SYEP's late-January application
reg_2027.notes: DPR directs families to register for teen career camps at DPRprograms.com - the summer PROGRAMS portal whose first-come registration opened May 13 in 2026 - rather than the February camp lottery (which covers ages 3-13). Details are published late and vary year to year; call the camp office in spring. If your teen wants paid work instead, pivot to Marion Barry SYEP (separate application, opens late January).

providers.fit_notes: For DC teens who want structure and career exposure without a formal job. Details are thin on the public site - call the DPR camp office before counting on a specific theme. Verified only via search-indexed dc.gov copies; registration mechanics inferred from DPR's pointer to DPRprograms.com.

providers.name: DPR Therapeutic Recreation Camps
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation
providers.description: DC's public therapeutic recreation camps, run out of the dedicated Therapeutic Recreation Center at 3030 G St SE, serve campers with and without developmental disabilities at deeply subsidized public rates — the core offerings are Camp Adventure, an inclusion-based camp for ages 6-16, and Discovery Inclusion support for ages 6-10 within DPR's Discovery Camps. A recreation therapist assesses each child first and determines placement based on the accommodations needed. Combined with DPR's 50%/75% income-based reduced-rate program, this is the most affordable supported-camp option in the District.
providers.ages: 6-16 (Camp Adventure inclusion camp 6-16; Discovery Inclusion 6-10; general DPR camps serve 3-13; TR Center also runs teen/adult programming — placement set by assessment)
providers.locations: Therapeutic Recreation Center, 3030 G St SE, Washington DC (some TR camps at other DPR sites)
providers.price_detail: Low public rates (see DPR 2026 Summer Camp Fees PDF: resident / non-resident / 50% / 75% reduced-rate tiers); 10% non-refundable processing fee. Exact weekly dollar amounts not captured in indexed copies.
providers.financial_aid: DPR reduced rates: income-qualified DC families get 50% or 75% off all DPR programs including camps; 2026 reduced-rate application deadline for camp was Feb 2
providers.hours: Full-day day camp (typical DPR camp day ~9am-5pm; verify per camp)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 sessions aligned with DPR camp calendar (June-August)
providers.status_2026: Season underway; general DPR lottery ran Feb 2-16, but TR registration is first-come after assessment — call the TR Center about remaining space

reg_2027.opens: assessments year-round; general DPR camp lottery opens early February (2026: Feb 2-16)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come (TR camps: in-person only at the TR Center after required assessment — no online registration); lottery for general DPR camps
reg_2027.notes: Two-step process: schedule an assessment with a recreation therapist at 202-698-1794 well before spring, then register in person at the TR Center — online registration is not available for TR camps, and DPR states it cannot accept every child due to space and staff constraints. Open to non-residents at higher rates. Apply for reduced rates BEFORE camp registration opens (2026 deadline was Feb 2). Verified via search-indexed dpr.dc.gov pages; direct site fetch blocked.

providers.fit_notes: Best for DC families who need affordable supported camp and can navigate the in-person assessment process early — start in fall/winter, since DPR cannot guarantee every assessed child a slot. Not a clinical program; families outside DC pay non-resident rates.

providers.name: Duke Ellington School of the Arts Summer Intensives
providers.org: Duke Ellington School of the Arts (DCPS arts high school)
providers.description: DC's famous audition-based public arts high school (Georgetown/Burleith) runs community summer intensive classes in dance, theatre/storytelling, vocal and instrumental music, taught in its pre-professional departments — the theatre intensive has run 10am-4pm days in June. It doubles as a taste of Ellington for students considering applying to the school itself. Public information is thin and scattered across the school's site and blog, so families should email departments directly.
providers.ages: Roughly rising middle and high school students (varies by department; theatre and instrumental intensives target secondary students)
providers.locations: Duke Ellington School of the Arts, 3500 R St NW, Washington, DC (Georgetown/Burleith)
providers.price_detail: Instrumental music summer registration has been free for incoming students; other program fees not published — confirm by email
providers.financial_aid: none found (programs appear low/no cost by design)
providers.sessions_2026: June-early August sessions by department (exact 2026 dates not publicly posted)
providers.status_2026: Unclear/likely underway — 2026 session details were not findable on the open web as of July 2026; contact departments (desa.dance@ellingtonarts.org for dance, development@ellingtonarts.org general).

reg_2027.opens: Typically announced spring on ellingtonarts.org news/blog
reg_2027.notes: Rolling/first-come registration via online forms per department; no audition for community summer classes. If your child may apply to Ellington for high school (application + audition in 8th grade), the summer intensive is a smart on-ramp.

providers.fit_notes: Best for DC tweens/teens curious about serious arts training in a legendary setting. VERIFICATION FLAG: only verifiable via search-indexed copies and a school blog post; details are low-confidence — call (202) 282-0123 before planning around it.

providers.name: Dynamite Gymnastics Center Summer Camp
providers.description: A big, well-regarded recreational gymnastics gym on Boiling Brook Parkway (next door to Bolt Parkour) offering half-day and full-day summer camp weeks for all skill levels — tumbling, bars, beam, trampoline, foam pits, and games. A reliable pick for energetic kids with zero gymnastics background, and a practical one: enrollment runs through a self-serve iClassPro portal with many weeks all summer.
providers.ages: Roughly 4-14 (all skill levels)
providers.locations: 4956 Boiling Brook Parkway, North Bethesda, MD
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day sessions
providers.extended_care: Not verified — ask the front desk
providers.price_detail: Pricing shown in the iClassPro portal at enrollment; not published in sources I could verify
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026, half or full day
providers.status_2026: 2026 camp enrollment open via customer portal; multiple summer weeks listed as of July 2026

reg_2027.opens: winter (portal listings typically appear by early in the year)
reg_2027.notes: Low-stress registration; individual weeks often available well into summer. Also runs school's-out camps on MCPS days off — useful year-round.

providers.name: Echo Hill Camp
providers.description: A family-owned (three generations) sleepaway camp directly on the Chesapeake Bay in Worton, MD (~1.5 hours from DC) for about 150 campers ages 7-16 — small, salty, and famous for its free-choice program where kids design their own days: waterskiing, sailing, crabbing, swamp mucks, arts, and beach life. Three two-week sessions with all-inclusive tuition (even the camp store canteen is covered). The intimate scale is the opposite of the mega-camps.
providers.locations: 13655 Bloomingneck Rd, Worton MD (Kent County, Eastern Shore, ~1.5 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: $2,385 per two-week session (2026), all-inclusive (canteen, insurance, t-shirt, yearbook, laundry, trips, supplies) — about $1,190/wk, cheaper than most private sleepaways
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: Jun 21-Jul 3; Session 2: Jul 5-17; Session 3: Jul 19-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Session 3 (Jul 19-31) is the only remaining 2026 session — contact camp promptly about space

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling; 'Enroll for 2027' messaging typically appears at season's end)
reg_2027.notes: Small capacity (150) means returning families claim much of the space — new families should enroll in fall/early winter. All-inclusive pricing means no surprise add-ons. Verified via echohillcamp.com indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: For independent-minded kids who will thrive choosing their own schedule in a small, unfussy, water-centric community. Not for kids who need highly structured days or families wanting one-week sessions.

providers.name: Edlavitch DCJCC Summer Camps (Base Camp, Quest & Adventure)
providers.description: Urban day camp inside the 16th Street JCC near Dupont Circle: Base Camp blends arts and crafts, sports tournaments, and daily swim in the on-site pool in one- and two-week themed sessions, with one-week Quest (invention/engineering) and Adventure (ropes courses, outdoor) sessions in August, plus specialty sports/theater options and a teen Summer of Service program. Open to campers of all backgrounds and abilities.
providers.locations: Edlavitch DCJCC, 1529 16th St NW, Washington, DC 20036 (Dupont/Logan)
providers.price_detail: Per-session pricing on my.edcjcc.org portal (not search-indexed); members get discounts and/or priority registration
providers.financial_aid: Camp scholarships historically available through EDCJCC; confirm with camp office (202-518-9400)

reg_2027.opens: typically December-January, members first, then public
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via my.edcjcc.org portal with member priority
reg_2027.notes: The only full-service JCC camp in DC proper — central-DC demand means core summer sessions fill by early spring; August Quest/Adventure weeks last longer. Membership pays for itself via discount + priority.

providers.sessions_2026: One- and two-week themed sessions June-August 2026, plus one-week Quest and Adventure sessions in August
providers.status_2026: Season underway; August one-week sessions (Quest, Adventure) are the most likely to still have space — check portal
providers.fit_notes: The walkable, pool-included option for central DC families; most sessions run one to two weeks, so it works for both continuity-seekers and week-planners. Verified via indexed copies; portal has live pricing.

providers.name: Emma's Art Studio Summer Camps (Emma Teaches Art)
providers.description: A downtown-Bethesda fine-arts studio (the Deco Arts camp founder, formerly at Glen Echo Park) running small weekly camps where each day tackles a different fine-arts project — silkscreen printing, marbleized paper, fossil casting, terra cotta sculpture, tempera on real canvas — with high-quality materials. A boutique, teacher-led alternative to big institutional art camps.
providers.ages: 5-11 (most weeks 5-10; late-June and early-July weeks split into 5-8 and 8-11 sections)
providers.locations: 4931 Cordell Ave Suite 2A, Bethesda MD 20814 (downtown Bethesda)
providers.price_detail: $450/week (June 8-12, June 15-19), $475/week (June 22-July 10 weeks), $495 for the Aug 24-28 week in 2026
providers.hours: 9am-3pm
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 8 through July 10, plus an end-of-summer week Aug 24-28, 2026
providers.status_2026: Sessions listed for booking on CourseStorm into August 2026; remaining availability not individually verified

reg_2027.opens: early February (2026 registration opened Feb 3, announced on the studio blog; sessions post on CourseStorm)
reg_2027.notes: Registers online via CourseStorm (emmateachesart.coursestorm.com). Small studio capacity, so favorite weeks go early, but it is far less of a stampede than the big institutions.

providers.fit_notes: Suits elementary kids who want real art instruction in a small, personal setting near the Bethesda Metro. No teen program and no extended-day coverage found — working parents needing 8-6 care should look elsewhere.

providers.name: Encore Stage & Studio Summer Camps
providers.description: Arlington's venerable 'theatre by kids, for kids' runs a big slate of summer camps for ages 3-15, from pre-K story-and-character camps through Broadway-style musical theatre and hands-on technical-theatre/stagecraft camps, most ending in a family performance. One of the few programs anywhere that teaches elementary and middle-schoolers backstage crafts (sets, costumes, tech).
providers.locations: Multiple Arlington and Alexandria, VA sites, incl. Encore @ Cherrydale UMC, 3701 Lorcom Ln, Arlington
providers.price_detail: Per-camp pricing on encorestage.org; not verified. Early-bird registration discount ends Jan 31
providers.financial_aid: Limited full and partial scholarships for all classes and camps (application form to registrar)
providers.hours: Varies; half-day for youngest ages, full-day options for older
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed camps across summer at multiple sites; registration opened by early January (Jan 10 open house)
providers.status_2026: Registration open; with many sites and weeks, late-July/August availability is common — check the camps page

reg_2027.opens: Early January (open house ~Jan 10; early-bird pricing through Jan 31)
reg_2027.notes: Attend the January open house to sample camps and meet teaching artists before booking. NoVA entry included because its kids-run-the-show ethos and tech-theatre camps fill a genuine regional gap.

providers.fit_notes: Gentlest on-ramp for the very young (age 3+!) and the only close-in option for kids who'd rather build the set than stand on it. Sites are church halls, not a theatre facility — less glossy than Imagination Stage.

providers.name: Engineering For Kids of Northern Virginia
providers.description: Hands-on engineering camps (aerospace, mechanical, LEGO-based, junior robotics) run by an educator-led franchise at churches and community centers around Northern Virginia, with half-day and full-day weeks. A solid, lower-key alternative to the big-brand STEM camps for kids ages 4-14 who like to build.
providers.locations: Multiple community-center/church sites across Northern Virginia (varies by season); no verified DC or Montgomery County sites
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day options; exact hours vary by site
providers.price_detail: Not verified on official site
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer camps running; rolling registration, later weeks typically available — call to confirm

reg_2027.opens: late winter / early spring (schedule posted by franchise)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come by site and week; smaller sites cap quickly but the program as a whole rarely sells out far ahead.

providers.fit_notes: Good for younger builders (the 4-7 'junior' range is a real strength — most STEM camps start at 6+). DC/MoCo families face a Virginia drive.

providers.name: ESF Summer Camps at The Academy of the Holy Cross
providers.description: The Mid-Atlantic camp chain ESF runs its full model — Mini/Day Camp, Senior Camp with leadership focus, and Multi-Sports Camp with 60+ activities — on the 28-acre Academy of the Holy Cross campus in Kensington, near Rockville Pike. Professional, systems-driven camping similar in spirit to Headfirst, serving the Kensington/Bethesda/Rockville corridor.
providers.ages: Roughly 3-14 (Mini Camp through Senior Camp)
providers.locations: The Academy of the Holy Cross, 4920 Strathmore Ave, Kensington MD 20895
providers.price_detail: 2026 tuition on esfcamps.com/bethesda/rates (not independently verified); 25% deposit at enrollment, payment plans through May 15
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm standard, with extended-day options
providers.extended_care: Yes — extend-your-day options before and after core hours
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions across summer 2026
providers.status_2026: First-come enrollment continued into season; chain camps of this size typically hold some August availability — check rates page for open weeks

reg_2027.opens: Fall 'early access' sign-up, with full enrollment opening in winter
reg_2027.notes: Sign up for early access in the fall for first crack and early pricing; 25% deposit holds spots and monthly payment plans run to mid-May. No lottery.

providers.fit_notes: A polished chain-camp alternative to Headfirst for families east of Bethesda (Kensington, Wheaton, Silver Spring, Rockville); less distinctive personality than the single-campus legacy camps.

providers.name: F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre Performing Arts Camps (City of Rockville)
providers.org: City of Rockville Recreation and Parks
providers.description: Theater day camps run by the city at its 446-seat F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre in Rockville Civic Center Park: campers are cast in a play, rehearse with accomplished DC-area teaching artists, and put on a final performance for family and friends on a real proscenium stage. A rare chance to do genuine staged theater at municipal-camp prices, without auditions.
providers.ages: elementary through teen (grade bands vary by session — see city camp guide)
providers.locations: F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre, Rockville Civic Center Park, 603 Edmonston Dr, Rockville, MD 20851
providers.hours: Day-camp hours per session; see 2026 Camp Guide
providers.extended_care: Extended care available for many Rockville camps — verify per listing
providers.price_detail: Per-session pricing in Rockville's REC1 system; 2026 prices not independently verified. Nonresident surcharge applies.
providers.financial_aid: City of Rockville financial assistance for resident campers

reg_2027.opens: early-to-mid February with all Rockville camps (2026: Feb 10 residents / Feb 12 general, 8:30am)
reg_2027.notes: No audition — casting happens inside the camp — which makes these popular with first-time performers. Register during the resident window if eligible; the performance weeks with the strongest teaching-artist lineups fill first.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; session-level availability in the REC1 catalog (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026). Camp described in the city's 2026 Camp Guide, verified via search-indexed copy.
providers.fit_notes: Great confidence-builder for stage-curious kids; families seeking conservatory-level training should compare Imagination Stage or Adventure Theatre (private, pricier).

providers.name: First Tee — Greater Washington DC Summer Camps & Life Skills Classes
providers.description: The national youth-golf-plus-character-education nonprofit's DC chapter teaches at two storied public courses: Langston Golf Course (NE DC, historically significant as a Black golf landmark) and East Potomac/Hains Point (SW DC). Week-long summer camps and multi-week Life Skills Experience classes braid golf instruction with values curriculum and even STEM tie-ins, at accessible nonprofit prices with aid available. One of the best-value 'real sport' introductions in the city.
providers.ages: Roughly 6-17 (grouped by age; teen pathways available)
providers.locations: Langston Golf Course, 2600 Benning Rd NE; East Potomac Golf Links, 972 Ohio Dr SW (Hains Point)
providers.price_detail: Nonprofit pricing well below commercial golf camps; financial assistance available (playDCgolf's commercial junior camps at the same courses run $249-399/week for comparison)
providers.hours: Typically half-day morning sessions
providers.financial_aid: Yes — mission-driven nonprofit with need-based assistance
providers.sessions_2026: Summer session July 13 - August 16, 2026; week-long camps at Langston and East Potomac
providers.status_2026: Summer session starting July 13; registration open with space in many weeks as of early July

reg_2027.opens: Spring (class/camp schedule posted around March-April)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online registration; demand is steady but not frantic. Separately, playDCgolf (the courses' operator) sells first-come commercial junior camps at East Potomac ($399/wk, ages 5-15, 9-12) and Langston — a good fallback if First Tee weeks fill.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for families wanting an affordable, character-forward intro to golf at genuinely historic public courses; kids wanting all-day camp coverage will need aftercare arranged elsewhere since sessions are half-day.

providers.name: FLOC Summer Programs (tutoring + Outdoor Education Center camps)
providers.description: FLOC provides free educational services to low-income DC students: in summer that means free intervention tutoring in math and reading (2nd-12th grade, offered online) plus free residential outdoor camps at its 350-acre Outdoor Education Center in Harpers Ferry, WV — ropes courses, climbing, canoeing, and hiking with a teamwork curriculum. A rare free combination of academics and overnight outdoor adventure.
providers.ages: 2nd-12th grade (tutoring); school-age/teen cohorts for OEC residential camps
providers.price_detail: Free to students from low-income DC families
providers.locations: DC office at 1301 Pennsylvania Ave SE (Capitol Hill); camps at the Outdoor Education Center, Harpers Ferry, WV
providers.hours: Tutoring scheduled sessions; OEC camps are multi-day residential
providers.financial_aid: Services are free for low-income DC families — that is the program design

reg_2027.opens: Spring for summer tutoring and OEC camp sign-ups; students in FLOC's school-year Neighborhood Tutoring Program get priority
reg_2027.notes: Register by calling (202) 462-8686 or through floc.org. The residential OEC camps invite students from FLOC's DC programs and West Virginia partners — join the tutoring program during the school year to be in the pipeline.

providers.sessions_2026: Free summer tutoring (online) plus residential OEC camp sessions, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Summer tutoring enrollment typically remains open into summer; OEC camp rosters set by early summer
providers.fit_notes: For families who want free academic catch-up with a genuine camp experience attached. The overnight WV camps are a big deal for kids who have never left the city; requires engaging with FLOC's programs rather than one-click registration.

providers.name: Folger Shakespeare Library (no summer camp — free family programs and school-year teen fellowship)
providers.description: The world's largest Shakespeare collection, on Capitol Hill, does NOT run a summer camp — stop searching for one. Instead it offers free drop-in summer family programming (Family Takeovers every other Saturday, an all-ages Youth Poetry Workshop) and, for teens, the prestigious Lily McKee High School Fellowship, a free five-month humanities deep-dive that runs January-May with applications in the fall. Families wanting an actual Shakespeare day camp should look at Shakespeare Theatre Company's Camp Shakespeare downtown.
providers.ages: Family programs approx. 5-11 with adults; Lily McKee Fellowship for sophomores-seniors (approx. 15-18)
providers.locations: Folger Shakespeare Library, 201 East Capitol St SE
providers.price_detail: Family Takeovers and Youth Poetry Workshop are free; Lily McKee Fellowship is free to selected fellows
providers.sessions_2026: Family Takeovers every other Saturday through summer 2026; Youth Poetry Workshop drop-in dates on the events calendar
providers.status_2026: Open — the free drop-in family events require no registration and run all summer; no camp exists to book. Lily McKee Fellowship for Jan-May 2027 will open applications in fall 2026.

reg_2027.opens: n/a for camps (none exists); Lily McKee Fellowship applications each fall for the spring cohort
reg_2027.notes: For the Fellowship: competitive application, small cohort of DC-area high schoolers. For summer: just show up to free Saturday programming. The Secondary School Shakespeare Festival (March) is the other famous Folger youth entry point, organized through schools.

providers.fit_notes: Great free enrichment stop for literary families and a standout school-year credential for humanities teens; not a childcare or camp solution. Verified via search-indexed copies of folger.edu pages.

providers.name: Geneva Summer Camp for Young Children
providers.description: An award-winning preschool summer camp at a well-regarded Potomac early-childhood school, with nine weekly themes (dinosaurs, circus, rockets) delivered through science experiments, arts, gardening, water play, creek expeditions, and visiting performers and petting zoos. One of the very few close-in camps that genuinely takes 2-year-olds, and you can pick individual weeks.
providers.locations: 11931 Seven Locks Rd, Potomac MD 20854
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day schedules available
providers.price_detail: Priced per week by half/full-day schedule; contact school for current rate sheet
providers.sessions_2026: Nine themed weeks, roughly mid-June through mid-August
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; later-summer weeks may still have space for the youngest age groups — call to confirm

reg_2027.opens: mid-winter (enrollment for the coming summer typically posted by January-February)
reg_2027.notes: Current Geneva families enroll first in practice; the 2s and young-3s classrooms are the scarce commodity regionally, so newcomers with toddlers should contact the school in January. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: Ask school; full-day option covers most working-parent needs
providers.fit_notes: Best-in-class option for ages 2-4 in the Potomac/Rockville corridor. Older kids (5-6.5) may prefer a bigger camp with more range.

providers.name: Georgetown Hoyas Basketball & Soccer Camps
providers.description: Georgetown's varsity programs run summer camps on the Hilltop: the Men's Basketball Elite Camp (boys 8-18, all skill levels despite the name) at McDonough Gymnasium and the Thompson Athletic Center with commuter and overnight options, plus men's soccer camps aimed at developing and showcase-level players from one of the nation's top college soccer programs. Kids train in the actual varsity facilities with college staff and players.
providers.ages: Basketball: boys 8-18; soccer camps skew to competitive youth/HS players
providers.locations: Georgetown University campus (McDonough Gymnasium / Thompson Athletic Center), 37th & O St NW
providers.price_detail: Basketball Elite Camp: $585 commuter / $685 overnight for the five-day session (incl. lunch, dinner, t-shirt)
providers.hours: Five-day sessions; overnight option for basketball
providers.sessions_2026: Basketball Elite Camp June 23-27, 2026; soccer camp dates per guhoyas.com
providers.status_2026: June basketball session has passed; check soccer program pages for any remaining summer clinics

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring (individual coaching staffs post camps January-April)
reg_2027.notes: Each sport's coaching staff runs registration separately (a common college-camp quirk) — search '[sport] camp guhoyas' rather than expecting a central portal. First-come; elite/ID camps for older players may effectively function as recruiting showcases.

providers.fit_notes: Best for sports-mad kids who'll be thrilled by big-time college facilities, and for HS players eyeing recruiting exposure; not a general day camp and there's no aftercare.

providers.name: Georgetown Prep Sports Camps
providers.description: Sport-specific weekly camps (basketball, baseball, football, golf, lacrosse, soccer) led by Georgetown Prep's own varsity coaches on the school's 90-acre North Bethesda campus, alongside hosted programs like Jr. Wizards basketball and Nike Golf Camps. High-level instruction and facilities in a straightforward 9-3 day.
providers.ages: Roughly 6-16 (varies by sport and hosted camp)
providers.locations: Georgetown Preparatory School, 10900 Rockville Pike, North Bethesda MD
providers.price_detail: Per-camp pricing on gprep.org/camps and hosted-camp sites; not centrally published
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026 (e.g. hosted Jr. Wizards weeks June 22-26, July 6-10, July 27-31, Aug 3-7)
providers.status_2026: In season; hosted early-August weeks (Jr. Wizards Aug 3-7) were still being promoted — reasonable late-summer availability

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring, rolling by sport; hosted camps (Nike, Jr. Wizards) open on their own national schedules
reg_2027.notes: Each sport registers separately; no single opening day. Coach-led camps in marquee Prep sports (lacrosse, basketball) fill first.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want real coaching in one sport rather than a general camp; Metro-accessible (Grosvenor). Summer camps are open to boys and girls despite Prep being a boys' school — confirm per camp.

providers.name: Georgetown University Summer Academies (Pre-College)
providers.org: Georgetown University School of Continuing Studies
providers.description: One-, two-, and three-week intensive academies for high schoolers on Georgetown's campus — international relations, medicine, forensic science, business, law and more — taught through lectures, simulations, and site visits, with commuter and residential options. One application covers all academies; once approved, you register for whichever has space. Thrives: academically ambitious teens (and college-list builders) who want a taste of university life in DC; commuter option makes it practical for local families.
providers.ages: High school students (~rising 9th-12th, varies by academy)
providers.price_detail: From $3,095 for one week (commuter); residential and multi-week options cost more
providers.locations: Georgetown University main campus, 37th & O St NW
providers.sessions_2026: Rolling 1-3 week sessions across June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-July/August academy sessions sometimes retain space — registration is complete only when tuition is paid, so check the academy finder for open sections.

reg_2027.opens: roughly late fall/winter (application portal opens well before summer; academies fill on a rolling basis)
reg_2027.notes: Application (with counselor/teacher reference) unlocks registration; actual seats are first-come, first-served after approval, and marquee academies (medicine, IR) fill by early spring. Comparable local options: GW Pre-College Summer Immersion (2-week, dates TBA); American University's Discover the World of Communication is on hiatus for 2026 with a redesign expected for 2027.

providers.financial_aid: Limited; inquire with SCS — do not assume aid is available
providers.fit_notes: Expensive and academically flavored rather than academically rigorous in the credit-bearing sense (academies are non-credit enrichment). For local teens, commuter enrollment cuts cost substantially versus residential.

providers.name: Girl Scouts Nation's Capital Day & Twilight Camps
providers.description: Dozens of 100% volunteer-run day and evening camps across the region each summer — outdoor skills, crafts, songs, s'mores, hands-on science — including long-running camps in Montgomery County (one in Boyds has run 40+ years) and evening 'twilight' camps like Camp Firefly where the campfire is the finale. Any girl can join Girl Scouts to attend; these are among the cheapest organized camps anywhere in the DMV.
providers.ages: grades K-12 (program aide/teen helper roles for older girls)
providers.locations: Dozens of sites across DC, Montgomery County (incl. Boyds, and sites around Rockville/Silver Spring), NoVA and beyond; specific sites vary yearly — see annual camp guide
providers.price_detail: Volunteer-run, so fees are low (typically well under $250/wk; varies per camp). Regular Doubleknot registration requires full tuition at signup; the optional free Early Camper Placement Process instead takes a $25 deposit with the balance due by May 1.
providers.hours: Varies: day camps roughly 9am-4pm; twilight/evening camps run late afternoon-evening
providers.financial_aid: Up to 100% camp scholarships; day-camp assistance applications open early January (Jan 2 for 2026) — apply BEFORE registering, since full tuition is due at registration

reg_2027.opens: early January (2026 cycle: assistance apps Jan 2; Doubleknot registration and Early Camper Placement shortly after)
reg_2027.mechanism: varies by camp: some day/evening camps are first-come via gscnc.doubleknot.com, others run their own lottery; a free optional Early Camper Placement Process assigns spots by email, secured with a $25 deposit
reg_2027.notes: Girl must be a registered Girl Scout (GSUSA dues are $65/yr for the 2027 membership year, up from $45 in 2026) — join anytime, no troop required for camp. Check YOUR target camp's mechanism (first-come vs lottery) in the annual guide before January; beloved neighborhood camps fill within days.

providers.sessions_2026: One-week sessions June-August 2026 at volunteer-run sites region-wide
providers.status_2026: Season underway; some later-summer camps historically keep spots — check Doubleknot listings
providers.fit_notes: Unbeatable price for outdoorsy, screen-free weeks; volunteer-run means shorter hours and no aftercare — hard for two-full-time-job families without backup. Girls only. Membership cost rising to $65/yr materially changes the all-in price for one-week campers.

providers.name: Girl Scouts Nation's Capital Sleep-Away Camps (Potomac Woods, May Flather, Winona)
providers.description: GSCNC's three resident camps: Camp Potomac Woods (Leesburg, VA — closest to DC), Camp May Flather (George Washington National Forest, VA — high adventure, fully unplugged), and Camp Winona (Hughesville, MD — traditional waterfront camp), offering 2-night mini-camps up to full weeks with themed specialties (horses, water, arts, adventure). Entry is by an equal-chance lottery, not speed — a rarity among DC camps — and scholarships can cover up to 100%.
providers.ages: grades 2-12 (mini-camps for younger; CIT/counselor tracks for teens)
providers.locations: Camp Potomac Woods (Leesburg, VA), Camp May Flather (Mount Solon, VA), Camp Winona (Hughesville, MD)
providers.price_detail: Fees vary by program length/theme (nonprofit pricing, generally far below private sleepaway); $25 deposit secures placement, balance due May 1
providers.hours: Residential (2-night mini-camps through 1-week sessions)
providers.financial_aid: Up to 100% scholarships for all campers; request link arrives in confirmation email after deposit

reg_2027.opens: lottery expected mid-December 2026 - early January 2027 (2026 cycle: Dec 15-Jan 5, placements Jan 12, general registration Jan 20)
reg_2027.mechanism: lottery via Jotform with buddy/sibling matching, then placement email + $25 deposit; leftover spots go first-come at general registration
reg_2027.notes: Explicitly NOT first-come — all lottery entries have equal odds, so there is no midnight refresh race. Enter the lottery in December, and use Jan 20 general registration as the backup for unfilled programs. Must be a registered Girl Scout.

providers.sessions_2026: Themed sessions of 2 nights to 1 week across summer 2026 at all three camps
providers.status_2026: Season underway; general registration remained open after Jan 20 for unfilled programs — late-summer openings possible via Doubleknot
providers.fit_notes: The gentlest and cheapest on-ramp to sleepaway camp in the region, with mini-camps as a trial run. Lottery means you plan in December, not panic in January. Girls only.

providers.name: Girls Rock! DC Summer Camp
providers.description: A volunteer-powered, social-justice-rooted rock camp where campers form a band, learn an instrument from scratch, write an original song in one week, and perform it at a real DC venue (showcases at the Black Cat). Centers girls, trans, non-binary and gender-expansive youth - especially Black, brown, Indigenous, and native-Washingtonian young people - with radically accessible pay-what-you-can pricing from $0.
providers.locations: Camp site varies year to year (DC school/community buildings); showcase at Black Cat on 14th St NW. Open to DC, MD, VA youth.
providers.price_detail: $0-600 per week, pay-what-you-can sliding scale - no one turned away for inability to pay
providers.hours: Weekday day camp; week ends with Saturday showcase
providers.financial_aid: Built-in: entire tuition is a $0-600 sliding scale
providers.sessions_2026: July 13-18 and August 3-8, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration for 2026 summer camps is CLOSED (filled in spring). Adult volunteer applications ran through April 24.

reg_2027.opens: Spring (roughly February-April); announced via email list
reg_2027.notes: Application, not first-come checkout - priority given to the communities the org centers; demand exceeds spots, so apply as soon as the form opens and join the mailing list in winter. Volunteer applications open around the same time.

providers.fit_notes: For girls and gender-expansive kids who want to make music and find community - zero experience needed and cost is never a barrier. Not for boys (cis boys are outside the program's scope) or families needing guaranteed enrollment on a fixed schedule.

providers.name: Girls Who Code — Pathways Summer Program
providers.description: Girls Who Code's free virtual summer program for high schoolers (girls and nonbinary students): a flexible, mostly self-paced computer-science experience with live components, replacing the famous Summer Immersion Program, which ended after summer 2025. Because it is online, it works from any DC/MoCo bedroom and pairs easily with a part-time job or volunteer gig. Thrives: girls with zero-to-some coding experience who want a structured CS credential and a national peer community without cost.
providers.ages: High school students (grades 9-12)
providers.price_detail: Completely free
providers.locations: Virtual
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 Pathways cohort (multi-week, virtual)
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 application ran Jan 8-Apr 10 (early decision Feb 25); program in session. Note: the old in-person Summer Immersion Program is discontinued.

reg_2027.opens: early January (2026 cycle opened Jan 8)
reg_2027.notes: Apply by the early-decision deadline (late February) for best odds; final deadline early April. Low barrier — no experience required — but spots are finite.

providers.fit_notes: Virtual-only, so no in-person social component locally — combine with an in-person activity. Teens wanting in-person DC coding should look at university pre-college CS tracks instead.

providers.name: Girls Who Code Pathways (free, virtual)
providers.description: Girls Who Code retired its famous Summer Immersion Program after 2025 and replaced it with Pathways: a completely FREE 6-7 week virtual summer program (June 29 - Aug 14, 2026) with self-paced tracks in game design, data science, AI, cybersecurity, and web development plus a Discord community. Zero coding experience required — one of the best free STEM opportunities anywhere for girls and nonbinary students.
providers.ages: Girls and nonbinary students in grades 8-12 during the current school year (rising 9th through graduating seniors)
providers.locations: Fully virtual, self-paced with live community
providers.hours: Self-paced across the 6-7 week window
providers.price_detail: Completely free; laptop/hotspot support available for US students
providers.financial_aid: Free program; device and hotspot access support for students who need it
providers.status_2026: 2026 application window closed (opened Jan 8, closed April 10); program running June 29 - Aug 14, 2026

reg_2027.opens: early January (2026 cycle opened Jan 8)
reg_2027.notes: Short application (two short responses, written or video); early decision deadline ~late Feb, final deadline ~mid-April. Not competitive-selective like the old SIP, but apply early. Note: the in-person Summer Immersion Program no longer exists — ignore outdated listings.

providers.sessions_2026: June 29 - Aug 14, 2026
providers.fit_notes: Best zero-cost on-ramp to coding for teen girls/nonbinary students, and self-paced enough to stack with other camps. Virtual format requires some self-motivation.

providers.name: Glen Echo Park Aquarium Camps
providers.description: Marine-science day camps run by the small teaching aquarium inside Glen Echo Park, with themed weeks (e.g., Chesapeake Critters) built around live animal encounters, ecology, and hands-on science. A gentler, animal-centered alternative to sports or arts camps for younger kids who love creatures.
providers.ages: 3-12 (single-day family camps for 3-5 with a caregiver; most weeklong camps ages 6-8; two camps for 8-12. Campers must be 6 by June 1 to register for weeklong camps)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park Aquarium, Glen Echo Park, Glen Echo MD
providers.price_detail: $450/week for weeklong camps in 2026; July 4 week single-day mini-camps $75/day; ages 3-5 family camp runs as single-day sessions (~$40/day)
providers.hours: 9am-3pm daily for weeklong camps; family camp is a morning session
providers.extended_care: Park aftercare 3-6pm available for an added weekly fee
providers.sessions_2026: Themed weeks mid-June through mid-August 2026, plus single-day mini-camps the week of July 4 and single-day family camps for ages 3-5
providers.status_2026: Registration opened Feb 4, 2026 at 10am via Glen Echo's system; check ProClass for remaining July/August weeks

reg_2027.opens: early February (Feb 4 at 10am in 2026; camps listed in the January Glen Echo catalog)
reg_2027.notes: Registers through Glen Echo Park's ProClass system. Young-age weeks (6-8) are small-capacity; under-6s cannot enroll in weeklong camps — use the family day camps instead. Contact 301-955-6256 / info@gepaquarium.org with questions.

providers.fit_notes: Great for animal-obsessed 6-10s, with single-day family camps to ease preschoolers in (caregiver attends). Skews young — the oldest band is 8-12, so nothing for teens.

providers.name: Glen Echo Park Camps: Puppetry & Children's Theater (The Puppet Co.)
providers.org: Glen Echo Park Partnership for Arts and Culture / The Puppet Co.
providers.description: Glen Echo Park — the arts-filled historic amusement park that also houses Adventure Theatre — offers a summer camp catalog that includes puppetry and children's-theater offerings connected to The Puppet Co., the only year-round dedicated puppet theatre between New York and Atlanta. Offerings vary year to year (workshops for ages ~8-13 are typical); check the park's camps catalog rather than the Puppet Co. box office.
providers.ages: Varies by offering; puppetry workshops typically ~8-13
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD (Arcade Building classrooms; Puppet Co. Playhouse)
providers.price_detail: Priced per camp in the Glen Echo Park catalog; not verified for summer 2026
providers.financial_aid: Not verified; ask the Park Partnership
providers.hours: Varies by camp
providers.sessions_2026: Spring-Summer 2026 catalog includes a Camps section with registration via the park's Imperisoft system; we could NOT verify a dedicated Puppet Co. weekly summer camp for 2026
providers.status_2026: Unverified — consult the Glen Echo Park camps page (glenechopark.org/camp) or call 301-634-5380

reg_2027.opens: Late winter (when the park's Spring-Summer catalog posts)
reg_2027.notes: Registration runs through Glen Echo Park Partnership's system, not The Puppet Co. itself. Verify current puppetry camp offerings each year — they are not guaranteed annually.

providers.fit_notes: Include on your list only if your kid is specifically puppet-obsessed or you want the Glen Echo Park setting; offerings are workshop-scale, not a full camp brand. Could not verify a 2026 summer puppetry camp on any accessible page — treat as a lead, not a booking.

providers.name: Glen Echo Park Summer Camps
providers.org: Glen Echo Park Partnership for Arts and Culture
providers.description: A historic former amusement park in a national park setting that is now the region's densest cluster of working art studios, offering weeklong camps in ceramics, glassblowing, painting, drawing, dance, theater, music, magic, and more. Kids take camp inside real artist studios (kilns, glass furnace, darkroom) rather than a rec-center classroom, and the ProClass catalog lets you mix different studios week by week. One of the signature arts-camp experiences of the DC area for newcomer families on the MD side.
providers.ages: 4-17 (official range is ages 5-17 with a few camps starting at 4; most core art camps are elementary-age, teen camps to 17)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD (studios throughout the park)
providers.price_detail: Most weeklong day camps roughly $450-600 depending on studio (example: $495 fine-arts weeks; Aquarium weeks $450); aftercare about $150/week where offered. Verify per camp in the catalog.
providers.hours: Most camps 9am-3pm
providers.extended_care: After Camp extends the day until 6pm for many camps (~$150/week)
providers.financial_aid: None found on the camps pages; ask the registrar (registrar@glenechopark.org, 301-634-2255)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions mid-June through the Aug 24-28 end-of-summer week; catalog posted January, registration opened Feb 4, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open and ongoing per the park's site; many popular early-summer camps full but assorted mid/late-July and August sessions still listed in ProClass, including end-of-summer weeks

reg_2027.opens: early February (Feb 4 in 2026; digital catalog posts in January)
reg_2027.notes: Online-only via the ProClass system; you can build a wish list from the January catalog before registration opens. Glassblowing, ceramics, and the best-known art camps fill within days to weeks; late-summer weeks linger longer.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for kids who want real art-making in real studios and families who like a la carte weekly scheduling. Not a single all-summer camp community — each week can be a different program — and the park's partner-run structure means quality/format varies by studio.

providers.name: Goldfish Swim School Jump Start Clinics (Rockville)
providers.description: A learn-to-swim intensive rather than a camp: daily 30-minute lessons Monday-Thursday (or similar consecutive-day blocks) in a warm 90-degree indoor pool, designed to compress months of weekly-lesson progress into a week or two. Small ratios (4:1) and a famously gentle, confidence-first method make it the practical answer for the newcomer family whose child can't yet swim — a safety prerequisite many DC-area camps quietly assume.
providers.ages: 4 months-12 years (Jump Start clinics typically 3-12)
providers.locations: Goldfish Swim School Rockville (serves Bethesda/Potomac families); other DMV franchises exist
providers.hours: Daily short lessons (approx. 30 min) over consecutive days; families pick a time slot
providers.extended_care: Not applicable — lesson intensive, not childcare
providers.price_detail: Priced per clinic block; not published in sources I could verify — call the Rockville school
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 Jump Start Clinic blocks running through the summer
providers.status_2026: Booking open for Summer 2026 clinics as of July — consecutive-day blocks are added throughout the season

reg_2027.opens: spring (booking typically opens by April-May)
reg_2027.notes: Preferred time slots (early evening, late morning) go first; existing members book before the public. If the goal is 'swim-safe before camp season,' do a clinic block in late spring rather than mid-summer.

providers.name: Goshen Scout Reservation
providers.org: Scouting America, National Capital Area Council
providers.description: NCAC's massive 4,000-acre summer reservation on Lake Merriweather near Goshen, VA (~3.5 hrs from DC): three Scouts BSA camps (Olmsted, Marriott, Bowman), two Cub/Webelos camps (Ross, PMI), and the Lenhok'sin High Adventure backcountry program. The definitive DC-area scouting summer — merit badges, sailing and aquatics on the lake, ranges, and week-long troop camping; individual scouts without a troop trip can attend via the Provisional Camp.
providers.ages: Cub Scouts through Scouts BSA/Venturers (~7-17; Lenhok'sin 13+)
providers.locations: Goshen Scout Reservation, 340 Little India Rd, Goshen, VA 24439 (Rockbridge County, ~3.5 hrs from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026: youth $580/wk early bird (by Apr 15), $610 after; adults $310-350; $200 non-refundable unit registration fee per troop; all program fees included
providers.hours: Residential, week-long sessions

reg_2027.opens: units reserve sites starting fall 2026; individual fee milestones spring 2027 (2026 pattern: merit badge sign-ups Mar 31, early-bird ends Apr 15)
reg_2027.mechanism: unit (troop/pack) registration via gotogoshen.org; individuals without a unit trip use Provisional Camp
reg_2027.notes: Registration flows through your troop, not parents — ask the troop's Goshen coordinator in the fall. Early-bird pricing ends mid-April. Newcomers: join a troop first; provisional weeks are the solo-scout backdoor.

providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions late June through early August 2026 across all camps; 2026 Leaders Guide published Feb 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; unit slots were set months ago — provisional openings possible, contact council
providers.fit_notes: For scouting families this is the summer anchor; for non-scouts it requires joining. Rustic, phone-free, merit-badge driven — thrives on kids who like structure plus wilderness.

providers.name: Green Acres Camp
providers.description: A progressive, woodsy day camp on Green Acres School's 15-acre North Bethesda campus with its own outdoor pool, mixing swimming, sports, arts, STEM, and performing arts in weekly sessions for ages 4 through rising 8th grade. Mid-market pricing, real financial aid through SSS, and generous multi-week discounts make it one of the more accessible school camps.
providers.ages: 4-14 (age 4 through rising grade 8)
providers.locations: Green Acres School, 11701 Danville Drive, North Bethesda MD 20852
providers.price_detail: Averages ~$460/week (2026); 5% off for 4-7 weeks, 10% off full summer; $65 application fee + $250 deposit per camper
providers.hours: Core day with care from 7:30am to 6:00pm
providers.extended_care: Yes — before care from 7:30am and after care until 6pm
providers.financial_aid: Need-based tuition grants via School and Student Service (SSS) — submit Parents Financial Statement
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 22-Aug 14, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season runs to Aug 14; weekly-enrollment model means August weeks often have space — check dates-and-rates page
