reg_2027.opens: Mid-January for the public (2026: current school and returning camp families first, all others from Jan 15)
reg_2027.notes: Application-then-approval flow (not instant checkout) with priority for returning families; on-site pool weeks and youngest groups are tightest. Aid applicants should start SSS paperwork before January.

providers.fit_notes: A strong value pick with actual financial aid and a pool on campus; smaller and homier than Landon/Bullis, without their sports depth.

providers.name: GW Campus Sports Camps (Nike Tennis at GW + Craig Jones Soccer)
providers.org: George Washington University coaching staffs / US Sports Camps
providers.description: GW's Foxhall-area athletic facilities host two notable summer options: the Nike Tennis Camp at the GW Tennis Center (2100 Foxhall Rd NW) with 16 weekly coed sessions for ages 6-17 at all levels, and Craig Jones/Matt Watts soccer camps run by GW's soccer coaching staffs on the Mount Vernon campus. Convenient for Palisades/Foxhall/Georgetown families wanting college-facility camps without leaving NW.
providers.ages: Tennis: 6-17 (beginner-advanced); soccer camps by age group
providers.locations: GW Tennis Center, 2100 Foxhall Rd NW; GW Mount Vernon campus fields
providers.price_detail: Nike Tennis: $499-789 per 5-day week (full/half day); soccer camps priced separately
providers.hours: Full-day and half-day weekly sessions
providers.sessions_2026: Nike Tennis: 16 sessions June 29 - August 21, 2026; soccer camp dates per program sites
providers.status_2026: Registration open; with 16 tennis sessions through Aug 21, late-summer weeks remain bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: Late fall/winter (US Sports Camps opens next-summer Nike camps around November-January)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via ussportscamps.com for tennis; coach-run websites for soccer. Big session count means this is one of the easier quality camps to get into late — a useful backup when everything else is full.

providers.fit_notes: Reliable, professional, somewhat corporate camp experience; kids get strong instruction but less neighborhood-camp charm. Priciest weeks are the full-day options.

providers.name: GW Pre-College Summer Immersion (incl. Corcoran arts tracks)
providers.description: Two-week, full-day, non-credit intensives for rising 10th-12th graders in downtown DC — international affairs, public policy, biomedical engineering, museum studies, and arts tracks drawing on GW's Corcoran School — using the city itself as a classroom. Its Corcoran-affiliated ArtReach GW Summer Intensive is a separate free arts opportunity for eligible students.
providers.ages: Rising high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors
providers.locations: GW Foggy Bottom campus, Washington DC
providers.hours: Full weekdays for each two-week session (commuter program; no housing in Summer Immersion)
providers.price_detail: Multi-thousand-dollar per-session tuition typical of DC university pre-college programs; exact 2026 fee not verified — confirm on the program page
providers.financial_aid: Not verified for Summer Immersion; ArtReach GW Summer Intensive is free for participants
providers.status_2026: Rolling admission as space allowed; final deadlines were May 31 (Session I), June 14 (Session II), June 28 (Session III) — Session III just closed, program is now underway/complete for 2026

reg_2027.opens: applications typically open in late fall/winter; priority deadline around May 1
reg_2027.notes: Rolling admission — earlier applicants get first pick of topics; apply by the priority deadline (May 1 in 2026) since popular tracks cap. Three session windows let you fit it around other plans.

providers.sessions_2026: Three 2-week sessions across summer 2026
providers.fit_notes: Best for teens who want a resume-credible academic deep-dive plus downtown-DC field experiences. Non-credit; families wanting transcript credit should compare Georgetown Summer College or UMD Terp Young Scholars.

providers.name: Headfirst Summer Camps
providers.description: The DC area's biggest homegrown day-camp operator, running polished multi-activity, STEM ('Innovators, Inc.'), sports, and day camps on rented private-school campuses (St. Albans/National Cathedral School and St. Patrick's in NW DC, Mater Dei and Norwood in Bethesda, Flint Hill in Oakton VA). Known for heavy counselor training, slick logistics, and week-by-week flexibility — a safe default pick for newcomer families with elementary-age kids who want a high-quality general camp without knowing the local landscape.
providers.ages: 3-13 (PreK through rising 7th-8th grade, varies by camp type)
providers.locations: Six DC-metro sites: St. Albans School & National Cathedral School (Cathedral Heights NW DC), St. Patrick's Episcopal Day School (Palisades NW DC), Mater Dei School and Norwood School (Bethesda MD), Flint Hill School (Oakton VA)
providers.price_detail: $675/week verified for Bethesda (Mater Dei) K-7 multi-activity weeks in July 2026; varies by camp and site. Extended day extra.
providers.hours: Core day roughly 8:45am-2:45pm or 9:00am-3:00pm depending on program/site (e.g. Mater Dei K-7 runs 8:45-2:45, PreK 9:00-3:00); optional before/aftercare extends the day
providers.extended_care: Yes — before and aftercare sold by the week at extra cost
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June through August at all sites (Mater Dei PreK weeks run June 8-Aug 10)
providers.status_2026: Enrollment open in-season and site says camps are 'filling quickly'; individual late-July and August weeks may still have space at some sites — check the Camp Finder by week

reg_2027.opens: Not published; join their 'insider list' to be notified when registration opens (historically opens well before spring — exact month could not be verified on the official site)
reg_2027.notes: Week-by-week online enrollment, no lottery. Popular sites (Cathedral Heights, Palisades) and early-summer weeks fill first. No busing — parents drive, so pick the site near you.

providers.fit_notes: Great for K-5 kids who want variety and polish; not for families needing bus transportation, financial aid, or teen programming. One of the pricier general camps.

providers.name: Higher Achievement Summer Academy
providers.description: A no-cost six-week summer academy that is half summer school, half summer camp for academically motivated middle schoolers from under-resourced communities. Scholars take core classes aligned to the year ahead, pick electives like theater and dance, take field trips, and end the summer with day and overnight college visits. It is a multi-year commitment program (afterschool plus summer), not a drop-in camp — kids who want a serious academic boost with mentors thrive here.
providers.ages: Rising 5th-8th graders (middle school 'scholars')
providers.price_detail: Completely free, including trips and college visits
providers.locations: Achievement Centers in DC Wards 1, 4, 6, 7, 8 (e.g., Brookland, Ward 8 center), plus Alexandria VA and Prince George's County MD
providers.hours: Full weekday program during the 6-week academy; exact daily hours vary by center
providers.financial_aid: Program is entirely free; targeted at students from under-resourced communities

reg_2027.opens: Rolling recruitment, heaviest late winter-spring for the following school year and summer
reg_2027.notes: Families apply for the multi-year scholar program (not just the summer); enrollment goes through the local Achievement Center. Serves ~400 scholars region-wide, so ask early — contact the center in your ward. Verify via higherachievement.org in early 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Six weeks, roughly late June through early August 2026
providers.status_2026: Summer Academy underway for enrolled scholars; mid-summer walk-in enrollment not offered — families should inquire now for the 2026-27 school year cohort
providers.extended_care: Not a wraparound-care camp; hours are program-set
providers.fit_notes: For families who want a free, rigorous academic + enrichment summer and can commit to the year-round program. Not for families seeking flexible week-by-week childcare or a single-week camp.

providers.name: Hirshhorn Teen Studio (free Saturday art workshops)
providers.description: Free art-making workshops for teens on Saturday afternoons at the Hirshhorn: each session mixes gallery time with hands-on experimentation (painting, printmaking, fiber art, sculpture) in the museum's Art School. Weekly during the school year and roughly one Saturday a month in summer - not a day camp, but a reliable free creative outlet year-round.
providers.ages: 13-19 (summer session listings say 13-18)
providers.locations: Hirshhorn Art School, museum lower level, National Mall
providers.price_detail: Free; register once and the registration covers all sessions in the season
providers.hours: Saturdays 2:30-4 pm; weekly during the school year, about one Saturday per month in summer
providers.sessions_2026: Summer Teen Studio runs roughly one Saturday a month through summer 2026 (e.g. 'Show Your Colors!', 'Keep It Tight(-Knit)'); weekly sessions resume with the school year - check the Hirshhorn event calendar for dates

reg_2027.opens: Rolling - register once per season (online form during the school year; email HMSGEducation@si.edu for summer sessions), then attend any Saturday
reg_2027.notes: No selection, no deadline; join one session or all of them. Good on-ramp for teens who miss the Emerging Artists application window.

providers.status_2026: Active; monthly Summer Teen Studio Saturdays are running through summer 2026 - verify specific dates on the museum event calendar.
providers.fit_notes: All skill levels explicitly welcome, 'novice notebook doodler' up. Useful for newcomer teens to meet peers; not childcare and not full-day, and summer sessions are only about monthly.

providers.name: Hirshhorn Teens: Emerging Artists Program
providers.description: A free, selective 8-week summer intensive for high-school artists at the Smithsonian's modern art museum: two days a week of studio work, gallery time, and behind-the-scenes access with museum professionals, ending in a public showcase of the teens' work. One of the best free arts opportunities in the city for a committed teen.
providers.ages: High school students ages 15-19 in the DC metro area
providers.locations: Hirshhorn Museum, Independence Ave & 7th St SW, National Mall
providers.price_detail: Free; must commit to all 16 sessions plus showcase
providers.hours: Mondays and Tuesdays, 11 am-3 pm
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - August 11, 2026 (Mon/Tue), showcase Saturday August 15

reg_2027.opens: Applications open late winter; 2026 applications were due Monday April 6 with decisions by April 24
reg_2027.notes: Open to any DC-metro high schooler 15-19; request application materials by email via the program page. Requires full attendance commitment - treat the early-April deadline as firm.

providers.status_2026: 2026 cohort selected and program in session (June 22-Aug 11); applications closed.
providers.fit_notes: For teens serious about making art and meeting other teen artists - not a drop-in craft camp. Two days/week means it pairs well with a job or another program.

providers.name: Holton-Arms Creative Summer
providers.description: A beloved arts-forward day camp on the Holton-Arms campus on River Road, where campers rotate through visual arts, theater, dance, swimming, and creative play in three-week sessions. One of the area's most demanded camps — core programs were full with closed waitlists by early June 2026 — so it rewards families who plan early.
providers.ages: 4-13 (Creative Fours; Creative Fives/rising K; Junior Campers 6-7; Creative Campers 7-13)
providers.locations: Holton-Arms School, 7303 River Road, Bethesda MD 20817
providers.price_detail: 2026: $1,655 per 3-week day-camp session (~$550/week), $150 deposit; refundable only until May 1
providers.hours: Full camp day; extended day available
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: June 22-July 10; Session 2: July 13-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: FULL — Creative Campers, Junior Campers, and Creative Fives full with waitlists closed as of June 5, 2026; do not count on 2026 space

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January); CampBrain
reg_2027.notes: Among the fastest-filling camps in the guide — 2026 was fully waitlisted before the season started. Register the day it opens; note the three-week session commitment.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for arts-loving elementary kids who thrive in a warm, tradition-rich camp; wrong fit if you need week-by-week flexibility or waited past January.

providers.name: HoopEd Summer Camps
providers.description: A long-running local operator of deliberately small basketball camps (plus multi-sport 'Day Camp' options) at school gyms across DC and close-in Montgomery County, including Grace Episcopal Day School in Kensington. Camps are intentionally capped so every kid gets coaching attention — a good pick for kids who'd get lost at a giant camp. Full-day schedule with cheap, flexible before/aftercare makes it working-parent-friendly.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-13
providers.locations: School-gym sites in NW/NE DC and Montgomery County (e.g., Grace Episcopal Day School, Kensington)
providers.price_detail: $365-375/week; aftercare $15/day (until 5:30 PM); before-care 7:30-9:00 AM available
providers.hours: 9:00 AM - 3:30 PM; before-care from 7:30 AM, aftercare to 5:30 PM
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions mid-June through August 2026 across sites (e.g., Kensington weeks June 29-July 3 and July 20-24)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; because camps are small, popular weeks fill, but multiple late-July/August weeks across sites usually still open in July

reg_2027.opens: Winter (schedule typically posted January-February)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online per site/week. Small caps mean specific convenient sites fill by spring even though the overall operation is large — book your home-site weeks early.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for kids who want real basketball reps with individual attention; not a spectacle-scale camp, and multi-sport day camp options exist for siblings who don't love hoops.

providers.name: Horizons Greater Washington Summer Academy
providers.description: A completely tuition-free multi-week (roughly five-to-six-week) summer academic-and-enrichment program for K-8 students from underserved DC and Maryland families, hosted on three independent-school campuses: Maret School and St. Patrick's Episcopal Day School in NW DC and Norwood School in Bethesda. Mornings blend literacy and STEAM academics with swimming — every child learns to swim — plus arts, field trips, and confidence-building, at least six hours a day, with meals and daily transportation provided free. Students typically enter after kindergarten via teacher recommendation at partner schools and return every summer through 8th grade.
providers.ages: 5-14 (K-8; students enter after kindergarten and re-enroll each summer through 8th grade)
providers.locations: Maret School (NW DC), St. Patrick's Episcopal Day School (NW DC), Norwood School (Bethesda)
providers.price_detail: Tuition-free (privately funded); breakfast, hot lunch, snacks, supplies, and daily transportation included
providers.financial_aid: Program is free — that is its purpose; eligibility requires free/reduced-price lunch qualification at admission
providers.hours: At least 6 hours/day for 25+ program days
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026: June 26 - July 31 (Maret site, 140 K-8 students; St. Patrick's and Norwood sites run similar calendars)
providers.status_2026: Enrollment closed for 2026 (new-student enrollment event was March 21); program in session now

reg_2027.opens: winter-early spring (2026 new-student event was Saturday March 21); Salesforce application portal
reg_2027.notes: Not open-enrollment: students must qualify for free/reduced-price lunch and are recommended by teachers at partner schools (Bancroft ES, HD Cooke, LAMB PCS, Simon ES, Bishop Walker, Rock Creek Forest ES, Rolling Terrace ES), typically for the summer after kindergarten; siblings of enrolled students get preference. Once in, children keep their seat every summer through 8th grade. Verified via horizonsgreaterwashington.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: Life-changing free option — with meals and transportation included — for eligible families at partner schools, but only for them. Newcomers not at a recommending school cannot simply sign up; check whether your assigned school is on the list.

providers.name: iD Tech Camps at American University
providers.org: iD Tech (national provider; DC host campus: American University)
providers.description: The biggest national tech-camp brand, hosted on AU's leafy NW DC campus: weeklong courses in Python, Roblox/Minecraft game dev, AI, 3D printing, and robotics with small groups (roughly 8 students per instructor) of exact-age peers. Best for kids who want a real tech curriculum and a taste of a college campus; note the premium price.
providers.ages: 7-17 (grouped by age; separate teen tracks)
providers.locations: American University, 4400 Massachusetts Ave NW, Washington DC. NOTE: the Georgetown University site is NOT running in summer 2026 — AU is the only DC host campus.
providers.hours: Day camps roughly 9 am - 5 pm Mon-Fri; day and overnight options advertised
providers.price_detail: Day camps from $1,149/week at the AU campus (2026 published starting price; iD Tech's cheapest campuses start at $949 but AU is not one of them); overnight room-and-board upgrade additional (approx. $679/week, not re-verified); payment plans from $375 down
providers.extended_care: Not verified; iD Tech typically offers extended-day add-ons — confirm at checkout
providers.status_2026: Season runs June 23 - Aug 8, 2026 at AU; registration open, late-season weeks may still have seats. Georgetown campus confirmed not operating in 2026 (iD Tech's own page says so).

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (iD Tech opens next-summer registration in Oct-Nov with early-bird pricing)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come by course/week; popular courses (Roblox, Minecraft, AI) at popular weeks sell out by spring but the season rarely sells out entirely. Book by winter for best course choice and early-bird discount.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, June 23 - Aug 8, 2026
providers.fit_notes: Great for kids already excited about games/coding who want structured skill-building. Expensive relative to local options; beginners are welcome but a motivated kid gets the most value.

providers.name: Identity Youth Summer Programming
providers.description: Montgomery County's main organization for Latino and other historically underserved youth runs free summer activities out of its Youth Opportunity Centers (Gaithersburg, Wheaton area) and school-based sites: soccer and outdoor sports nights, skill-building and academic-gap workshops, family events, and wraparound services — all bilingual and trauma-informed. Aimed squarely at teens who fall through the cracks of paid camp culture.
providers.ages: Mostly middle school through young adult (roughly 11-24)
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.locations: Youth Opportunity Centers and offices in Gaithersburg (620 E Diamond Ave); Wellness Centers at Gaithersburg, Kennedy, Northwood, Seneca Valley, Watkins Mill, and Wheaton high schools
providers.hours: Varies by site; evening and daytime summer activities

reg_2027.opens: Rolling; summer activity schedules posted late spring
reg_2027.notes: No formal camp registration — youth connect through the Youth Opportunity Centers, school Wellness Centers, or by calling. Spanish-language intake available. Just show up or call in June.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 slate of sports, outdoor events, and skill-building workshops across MoCo sites
providers.status_2026: Open and rolling through summer 2026
providers.fit_notes: The right door for Latino/immigrant teens in mid-Montgomery County who need free, welcoming summer structure — not a childcare-hours day camp for younger kids, and not activity-registration in the suburban-camp sense.

providers.name: Imagination Stage Summer Camps
providers.description: The region's flagship children's theatre-arts organization, with a purpose-built Bethesda facility and satellite camp sites in Rockville and NW DC. Offers 1- to 4-week camps in acting, musical theatre, dance, and filmmaking for ages 4-18, from playful early-childhood drama to production-level teen programs. The default first choice for many MoCo families; polished, well-staffed, and strong on inclusion supports for kids with disabilities.
providers.ages: 4-18 (pre-K through rising grade 12; programs banded by age)
providers.locations: Main facility 4908 Auburn Ave, Bethesda; satellite camp sites in Rockville and Northwest DC (check each camp's listed site)
providers.price_detail: 2026 per-week tuition not verified on an open page; ranges widely by length (1-4 weeks) and half vs full day. Payment plans available (set up by Apr 1, paid in full by May 1). Late pickup fee $10 + $1/min after 15 min.
providers.financial_aid: Tuition assistance awarded on financial need, rolling basis (contact Pam Freedy via imaginationstage.org); payment plans for all camps
providers.hours: Varies by camp; half-day options for youngest ages, full-day for most
providers.extended_care: Before and after care offered at main sites (details/prices on registration pages)
providers.sessions_2026: 1-, 2-, 3-, and 4-week sessions across the summer at Bethesda, Rockville, and NW DC sites; summer preview grid published Dec 10, 2025
providers.status_2026: Registration open as of mid-July; a multi-site weekly structure means later-summer sessions often still have seats, but popular musical-theatre weeks fill by spring

reg_2027.opens: December (2027 preview grid typically posts ~Dec 10; registration opens mid-to-late December)
reg_2027.notes: Popular sessions and best-known productions fill by early spring; register in December-January for full choice. Payment-plan setup closes April 1. Tuition assistance is rolling — apply early. Flag accessibility needs at registration; their Access team supports inclusion.

providers.fit_notes: Best all-around starter theatre camp — genuinely beginner-friendly at every age, with real craft for kids who get serious. Not the pick for a teen wanting a gritty conservatory vibe; teen programs skew polished/musical. Official site blocked our direct fetch; details verified via search-indexed copies of official pages.

providers.name: International School of Music Summer Music Camps
providers.description: Bethesda/Potomac's large private music academy runs summer camps and group classes - keyboard exploration, singing, creative music/improvisation, and 'Instrument Explorers' (first touch of wind, string, and percussion instruments) - alongside its core private-lesson program. A polished, lesson-school environment suited to families who want summer to feed into year-round study.
providers.ages: Roughly 3-12 for camps/classes; lessons all ages
providers.locations: ISM campuses in Bethesda and Potomac, MD
providers.price_detail: Pricing not published in indexed pages; contact 301-365-5888
providers.hours: Varies by program; largely half-day/class-length blocks
providers.sessions_2026: Summer camps scheduled July 12-August 8, 2026 per school calendar
providers.status_2026: 2026 camp window begins July 12 (today as of snapshot); enrollment handled directly - contact school for openings.

reg_2027.opens: Spring; rolling
reg_2027.notes: Rolling enrollment by phone/online; free trial offered. Rarely a high-pressure deadline - this is a low-stress option for late planners in Bethesda/Potomac.

providers.fit_notes: Best for preschool-elementary kids starting music in a structured lesson-school setting, or ISM students keeping momentum in summer. Not a full-day camp solution for working parents; verify schedule blocks.

providers.name: International Spy Museum — Spy Camp Teen Volunteer Program
providers.description: High-energy teens (15-18) staff the Spy Museum's popular Spy Camp for kids: preparing 'mission' materials, running carpool check-in/out, and supporting counselors for both camp weeks, earning about 80 volunteer hours plus behind-the-scenes museum experience. Requires an evening training session and full availability 8am-4pm for both weeks. Thrives: reliable, outgoing teens who want a short, intense, resume-worthy volunteer gig with a cool-factor museum.
providers.price_detail: Volunteer position; earns ~80 service hours
providers.hours: 8:00am-4:00pm daily for both camp weeks, plus one 4-hour evening training
providers.locations: International Spy Museum, 700 L'Enfant Plaza SW
providers.sessions_2026: Camp weeks July 13-17 and July 20-24, 2026; training July 9
providers.status_2026: Full — 2026 recruitment is complete; camp weeks start July 13.

reg_2027.opens: January 2027 (museum states summer 2027 applications post in January)
reg_2027.notes: Small corps, fills well before summer — apply in January. Must commit to BOTH weeks plus training. Listed with the Montgomery County Volunteer Center, so hours generally count for MCPS SSL (confirm with your school).

providers.fit_notes: Only a two-week commitment — great as one building block of a patchwork teen summer, useless as full-summer coverage.

providers.name: Investigating Where We Live (teen summer program)
providers.description: A free five-week summer program (running since 1996) where about 30 DC-area teens document a Washington neighborhood through photography, creative writing, and interviews, then design and install a real exhibition in the National Building Museum. Winner of the National Arts and Humanities Youth Program Award - a genuine portfolio-and-resume builder.
providers.ages: Teens from the Washington DC metro area (high school age)
providers.locations: National Building Museum, 401 F St NW, plus fieldwork in DC neighborhoods
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.hours: Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays for five weeks in summer
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 cycle not independently verified

reg_2027.opens: Applications typically in spring; email teenprograms@nbm.org to get on the list
reg_2027.notes: Small cohort (~30 students); the museum also runs a school-year Design Apprenticeship Program for teens who want more. Reach out by email early in the year - the application is not always prominently posted.

providers.status_2026: Program exists per official pages, but 2026-session dates and application status could not be verified on indexed copies - confirm by email.
providers.fit_notes: For teens interested in cities, photography, design, or exhibitions; the neighborhood-documentary format suits observant, curious kids more than pure artists. Three-days-a-week schedule leaves room for a job or another program.

providers.name: Isabella & Ferdinand Outdoor Spanish Summer Camp
providers.description: A 100% Spanish-immersion outdoor day camp for ages 5-13 across partner sites and outdoor venues in DC — sports (2026 theme: World Cup), art, adventures, and outdoor learning, entirely in Spanish, with a flexible pick-your-weeks model across nine weeks. The strongest full-day Spanish immersion option for elementary and middle-school kids in the District proper.
providers.locations: Partner sites and outdoor venues in Washington DC (varies by session)
providers.price_detail: $560/week; before-care $80/wk (7:30-8:30am); aftercare $120/wk (3:00-5:30pm)
providers.hours: Core day to 3:00pm; before-care from 7:30am, aftercare to 5:30pm
providers.sessions_2026: Nine weeks, June 22 - August 21, 2026 (Weeks 1-9); pick any combination
providers.status_2026: Season running through Aug 21 — remaining July weeks (Jul 13, Jul 20, Jul 27) and all August weeks were bookable per the 2026 camp page; one of the better late-summer options

reg_2027.opens: Around December-January (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: First-come pick-your-weeks online; runs deep into August (rare!), which makes its late-summer weeks valuable — but also means early weeks fill first. Groups are leveled, so true beginners are placed appropriately.

providers.fit_notes: Best for active kids — it's genuinely outdoor and physical, in Spanish all day. Full-coverage hours with before/aftercare suit working parents. Covers the awkward mid-August gap most camps ignore.

providers.name: JAM Camp (Jazz Academy of Music)
providers.description: A two-week afternoon jazz camp (1-5pm) led by the Jazz Academy of Music (saxophonist Paul Carr's long-running Montgomery County jazz-education nonprofit) at North Chevy Chase Christian Church. Instrumentalists and vocalists ages 8-18 focus squarely on jazz improvisation - learning to solo - with combos, theory, and a final performance; the half-day format pairs well with a morning camp.
providers.ages: 8-18, instrumental and vocal
providers.locations: North Chevy Chase Christian Church, Chevy Chase MD (Jazz Academy also runs a Southern Maryland camp)
providers.price_detail: $995 for the full two-week camp incl. music and t-shirt (~$500/week but half-day); $200 deposit starts a payment plan; full payment due June 1
providers.hours: 1:00-5:00pm, Monday-Friday, two weeks
providers.financial_aid: Payment plans offered; scholarships not confirmed
providers.sessions_2026: June 29-July 10, 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 session has ended (concluded July 10). Season over for this provider.

reg_2027.opens: Winter/early spring; payment plans begin April 1
reg_2027.notes: Rolling registration with payment due June 1; doesn't have the frenzy of the big day camps, but jazz-serious families register by spring. Some playing experience helps a lot.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who play an instrument (or sing) and specifically want to learn to improvise - it's the most improvisation-focused option in Montgomery County. Afternoon-only schedule is a plus for camp-stackers, a minus for full-day childcare.

providers.name: Johns Hopkins CTY Day Program at The Woods Academy (Bethesda)
providers.org: Johns Hopkins University Center for Talented Youth (CTY)
providers.description: New for 2026: the storied Johns Hopkins gifted-education program opened a Bethesda day site at The Woods Academy, running three one-week-style courses (June 28 - July 17) like The Physics of Engineering, Numbers: Zero to Infinity, and Shaping Our World: Architecture for advanced learners in grades 2-6. Admission requires joining CTY and qualifying test scores — this is a plan-ahead program, not a sign-up camp.
providers.ages: Grades 2-6, advanced learners with CTY-qualifying test scores
providers.locations: The Woods Academy, 6801 Greentree Rd, Bethesda MD (also Gilman School and JHU Homewood sites in Baltimore)
providers.hours: 9:00 am - 4:00 pm Mon-Fri
providers.price_detail: Tuition not verified for the Woods Academy site; CTY day programs are premium-priced and CTY offers need-based financial aid
providers.financial_aid: CTY has an established need-based financial aid program — apply alongside enrollment
providers.status_2026: Running June 28 - July 17, 2026; enrollment opened January and courses were 'filling fast' by March per local coverage

reg_2027.opens: January (2026 enrollment opened in January; first-come after eligibility established)
reg_2027.notes: Two-step: (1) join CTY and submit/sit for qualifying test scores — do this in fall; (2) enroll first-come when courses open in January. New MoCo site means local demand is high; do not wait on testing.

providers.sessions_2026: Three weeks, June 28 - July 17, 2026 (weekly courses)
providers.fit_notes: For genuinely advanced, school-loving kids who want intellectual peers — the CTY credential also unlocks their famous online and residential programs later. Testing requirement makes it impossible to book last-minute.

providers.name: Jones-Haywood Dance School Summer Program
providers.description: Founded in 1941 by Doris Jones and Claire Haywood to train Black ballet dancers when other schools would not, Jones-Haywood is a DC cultural landmark (alumni include Chita Rivera and Sylvester Campbell) still training dancers in NW DC. Its summer program offers all-day training in ballet, tap, jazz, and modern plus fitness, creative writing/poetry, and a student-choreography movement lab; select advanced students access travel intensives (Cannes, Martha's Vineyard).
providers.ages: Roughly 4-18 (grouped by level; conservatory track for serious students)
providers.price_detail: Tuition not published online; historically kept affordable with scholarships as part of the school's access mission — call for current rates
providers.locations: 1200 Delafield Pl NW, Washington DC (Petworth/16th Street corridor)
providers.hours: All-day summer programming
providers.financial_aid: Scholarship support consistent with the school's founding mission; inquire directly

reg_2027.opens: Spring; enrollment direct through the school
reg_2027.notes: Register via joneshaywooddanceschool.com or by phone; placement class may determine level. Advanced travel intensives are by selection. Not a mass-market camp — classes are intimate and can fill.

providers.sessions_2026: All-day summer training program, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Verify current openings directly; enrollment handled by the school office
providers.extended_care: All-day format; no separate aftercare published
providers.fit_notes: A storied, nurturing place for young Black dancers (all welcome) to get serious classical training without Kennedy-Center-conservatory prices. Families wanting casual one-week dance sampling should look at studio camps instead. Pricing unverified online — call.

providers.name: Joy of Motion Dance Center Summer Camps
providers.description: DC's big everybody-dances nonprofit studio runs weekly themed camps (ballet, hip hop, musical theatre, choreography, world dance, plus specialty 'Triple Threat' and 'Little Pop Star' weeks) at its home inside the Atlas Performing Arts Center on H Street NE. Explicitly welcoming to brand-new dancers alongside experienced ones, with a joyful recreational vibe rather than a conservatory one. One of the strongest financial-aid programs of any DC dance studio.
providers.ages: 5-14, split into Lower Level (5-9) and Upper Level (10-14) camps; classes from age 2
providers.locations: Atlas Performing Arts Center, 1333 H Street NE, Washington, DC 20002 (Atlas district); check site for additional studio locations
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-4:00pm
providers.price_detail: Approximately $425/week (search-indexed 2026 pricing); varies by camp theme
providers.financial_aid: Yes — tiered need-based aid up to 100% scholarships (HUD income thresholds for the DC metro area); submit the financial-aid application before enrolling
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps late June through mid-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open with weekly camps running into mid-August — one of the better mid-summer options still bookable as of July 2026

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: Rarely a panic sign-up: individual weeks stay available later than most DC camps. Financial-aid families should apply and receive a decision before enrolling, so start that paperwork in late winter. Full camp day is 9-4 with optional before care and aftercare stretching coverage to 8am-6pm.

providers.name: Jr. Wizards & Mystics Summer Basketball Camps
providers.description: The official Wizards/Mystics youth camps: week-long coed Jr. Wizards sessions plus girls-only Mystics camps at school gyms around the DMV, including DC sites (620 Michigan Ave NE) and suburban locations. Tuition includes a reversible jersey, t-shirt, and two tickets to a Wizards/Mystics home game, which makes it a favorite for NBA/WNBA-fan kids. Instruction is fundamentals-focused and welcoming to all levels ages 6-14.
providers.locations: Multiple DMV sites incl. Washington DC (620 Michigan Ave NE, sessions July 6-24), Bowie MD, Episcopal HS Alexandria VA
providers.price_detail: $375/week standard; Black Friday rate $275/week if registered by Dec 15; $100 of tuition is a non-refundable registration fee (refundable balance through April 1)
providers.hours: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM Mon-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Mid-June through early August 2026; DC (Michigan Ave NE) weeks July 6-10, 13-17, 20-24; Bowie July 27-31 & Aug 3-7; Alexandria Aug 3-7
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-July and early-August sessions (Bowie, Episcopal HS) typically still bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: Fall (registration open by November, with Black Friday discount pricing through Dec 15)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via LeagueApps (dcfamily.com). Insider tip: register before Dec 15 and save $100/week. Refund-friendly through April 1 (minus $100 fee), so early booking is low-risk.

providers.fit_notes: Fun, brand-name basketball week for fans and rec players; girls who want an all-girls environment should pick the Mystics-branded sessions. Serious AAU-level players may find it too fundamentals-oriented.

providers.name: JTCC Future Champs Summer Camp
providers.description: JTCC in College Park is one of the top junior tennis training centers in the United States — a nonprofit campus that has produced tour pros — and its Future Champs summer camp brings that coaching to everyday kids ages 4-18, grouped tightly by age and level. Full-day and half-day options run all summer, and strong players can be spotted for JTCC's champions pathway. Included despite sitting just over the DC line in Prince George's County because it is the region's flagship tennis facility.
providers.ages: 4-18, grouped by age and skill
providers.locations: JTCC, 5200 Campus Dr, College Park MD (just outside NE DC); satellite programs incl. Chantilly VA
providers.price_detail: Full-day weekly rates typically in the $500-700 range, half-day less (verify at jtccregister.org); credits available if cancelled 2+ weeks out
providers.hours: Mon-Fri, full-day and half-day options
providers.financial_aid: JTCC is a nonprofit with need-based aid across its programs; ask about camp scholarships
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly Monday-Friday sessions all summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open (opened winter 2026); mid/late-summer weeks generally still bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: January-February (2026 registration was announced open in winter)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online; capacity is large so panic-booking isn't needed, but preferred weeks/full-day slots go by late spring. No refunds within 14 days of your camp week; account credit between May 1 and 2 weeks out.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for any kid serious about tennis, and still friendly to beginners; families wanting a cheaper neighborhood option should compare Rock Creek Tennis Center or Montgomery TennisPlex.

providers.name: KEEN Greater DC (Kids Enjoy Exercise Now)
providers.description: Completely free adaptive sports and recreation for young people with developmental or physical disabilities, built on a 1:1 pairing of each athlete with a trained volunteer. Sessions (sports, swim, music, bowling, Zumba, fitness) run year-round on weekends — including through the summer — at sites in DC, Montgomery County, and Northern Virginia. It is not a weekday camp, but it is one of very few zero-cost, no-child-turned-away recreation options for kids with significant disabilities.
providers.locations: Rotating sites in DC (e.g., Deanwood Aquatic Center), Montgomery County, and NoVA (Vienna, Fairfax)
providers.price_detail: All programs 100% free to athletes and families
providers.hours: Weekend sessions, typically 1.5-3 hours (e.g., Sat/Sun mornings and afternoons)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 weekend sessions confirmed (e.g., June 21: MD Kids Sports, VA Sports, Zumba, bowling)
providers.status_2026: Running through summer 2026; new athletes accepted on a rolling basis — most programs currently have room, some have waitlists

reg_2027.opens: rolling year-round
reg_2027.notes: Submit the New Athlete Registration Form any time; athletes are added to programs continuously and waitlisted where full. Submit early even if waitlisted. Verified via keengreaterdc.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: For kids with significant disabilities who need true 1:1 support and zero cost — a supplement to (not substitute for) weekday camp coverage, since sessions are short and on weekends. Not for families needing full-day summer childcare.

providers.name: Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens free summer camps & Junior Ranger programs
providers.org: National Park Service (with Friends of Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens)
providers.description: DC's only national park devoted to water gardens — acres of lotus and water lilies on the Anacostia in Ward 7 — runs free week-long summer (and spring break) day camps specifically for Ward 7 students in grades 2-8, exploring wetland plants and wildlife in their own backyard. Year-round free ranger walks, a Junior Ranger badge booklet, and the huge free Lotus & Water Lily Festival (July 18-19, 2026) round out one of the city's best zero-cost nature offerings.
providers.ages: Camps: 2nd-8th grade Ward 7 students; ranger programs and festival: all ages
providers.price_detail: Everything free — entry, camps, ranger programs, festival
providers.locations: Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens, 1550 Anacostia Ave NE, Washington DC
providers.hours: Camps run week-long day sessions; park open daily

reg_2027.opens: Spring announcements via nps.gov/keaq and Friends of Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens
reg_2027.notes: The free Ward 7 camps have small capacity and are announced through the park and Friends group — call (202) 692-6080 or watch kenaqgardens.org in March-April 2027. Junior Ranger and festival need no registration.

providers.sessions_2026: Week-long free camps for Ward 7 2nd-8th graders; Lotus & Water Lily Festival July 18-19, 2026, 10am-8pm
providers.status_2026: Festival upcoming next weekend (July 18-19, 2026); drop-in ranger programs running all summer
providers.fit_notes: A hidden-gem free nature camp if you live in Ward 7, and a spectacular free family outing for everyone else in mid-July when the lotuses bloom. Camp eligibility is geographically restricted; drop-in programs are not.

providers.name: Kennedy Center Dance Lab (ON HIATUS for summer 2026)
providers.description: A selective summer intensive for about 30 high school dancers (entering grades 9-12) featuring daily ballet and modern technique, choreography, dance-health, and career/leadership training at the Kennedy Center. The official application page states the program is on hiatus for summer 2026 due to a scheduling conflict, with summer 2027 information promised in fall 2026. Worth calendaring for 2027 if you have a serious pre-professional dancer.
providers.ages: Entering grades 9-12 (approx. 14-18)
providers.locations: Kennedy Center, 2700 F St NW (Foggy Bottom)
providers.price_detail: Tuition for future summers not published; could not verify past cost from official sources
providers.financial_aid: none found (unverified — check when 2027 details post)
providers.sessions_2026: None — on hiatus
providers.status_2026: Not running summer 2026 (scheduling conflict per official application page). Kennedy Center's school-year Ballet Class Series and Contemporary Dance Series master classes continue as alternatives.

reg_2027.opens: Details expected fall 2026 on the Kennedy Center site; past applications ran winter/spring via smapply portal
reg_2027.notes: Application with dance experience/essays (video component in past cycles); only ~30 spots, selective. Check kennedy-center.org pre-professional training page starting September 2026.

providers.fit_notes: For ambitious teen dancers seeking a leadership-oriented intensive rather than a technique-only summer program. Verified via search-indexed copies of official Kennedy Center/smapply pages (direct fetch blocked).

providers.name: Key Bridge Boathouse Youth Paddling Camp
providers.org: Boating in DC / Adventures Unbound (NPS concessioner)
providers.description: A kayak/canoe/paddleboard day camp launching from the Georgetown waterfront under Key Bridge, capped at 21 kids per week with an 8:1 staff ratio and themed days (river-detective paddles, balance challenges, water games). Kids build real paddling confidence on the Potomac in the middle of the city. Run by the same concessioner operating DC's public boathouses.
providers.ages: Elementary-middle school (~8-15; exact range not verified on indexed pages)
providers.locations: Key Bridge Boathouse, 3500 Water St NW, Georgetown, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: $572/week base (2026); stackable discounts to max 20% off ($457.60); 15% early-bird for registering before end of January
providers.hours: Standard camp day (June-August camp line: 703-223-2451)
providers.financial_aid: None found; early-bird and stacking discounts only
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, June 22 - August 14, 2026
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; with only 21 spots/week, check remaining late-July/August weeks directly — availability unverified.

reg_2027.opens: By early winter (registration live around New Year; 15% early-bird discount runs through January)
reg_2027.notes: First-come; the January early-bird discount is the signal to book. Tiny weekly capacity means prime mid-summer weeks go early even though the brand is low-hype.

providers.fit_notes: Urban-adventure sweet spot for water-loving kids in DC proper — walkable/bikeable from Georgetown. Swimmers only; no aftercare.

providers.name: KID Museum Summer Camp
providers.description: The region's flagship maker-learning museum runs summer camps led by professional maker educators, blending woodworking, electronics, robotics, fabrication, and craft into hands-on project weeks that end in a Friday 3:30pm family showcase. It is the definitive 'maker' camp in close-in Montgomery County, at Bethesda Metro Center and the Davis Library site.
providers.ages: approximately 6-14 (strictly grade-banded — KID Museum enrolls by grade level, not age; verify bands per week on the site)
providers.locations: 3 Bethesda Metro Center, Bethesda MD, and Davis Library, 6400 Democracy Blvd, Bethesda MD
providers.price_detail: 2026 weekly price not verified from official copy; a 10%-off promo (code SC2026) ran through July 5, 2026 (now expired)
providers.hours: Full-day weeks with Friday 3:30pm camp showcase (exact hours vary by camp)
providers.financial_aid: Camps available free or at reduced cost for families who need assistance via the 'Access for Every Maker' initiative; educator and military discounts on family programming
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed camps across summer 2026 at both Bethesda locations
providers.status_2026: Still open — registration stays open until camps sell out and the museum says it's 'never too late to sign up'; the 10% SC2026 promo ended July 5. Good bet for late availability

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration open by early in the year; runs until camps sell out)
reg_2027.notes: Registers via their iClassPro portal. Fills steadily rather than instantly — a realistic option even for spring planners, though specific popular weeks/grade bands sell out. Enrollment is by grade, and age restrictions are enforced.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for kids who like building, tinkering, and tools more than drawing — real saws, soldering, and sewing machines with trained educators. Less suited to kids wanting traditional fine art or an outdoor-heavy summer.

providers.name: Kids After Hours (KAH) Summer Camps
providers.description: A long-running MoCo before/after-school operator that converts elementary schools into summer day camps — KAH Day Camp for elementary kids, Explorers (grades 3-4, $510/session), Adventure Camp (grade 4+, $575/session with big field trips), Travelin' Teens (grades 4-9), and Skate Camp (7-12). Extended care is baked into the price, which makes it one of the more working-parent-friendly private camps in the county.
providers.ages: Elementary through 9th grade depending on program (roughly 5-15)
providers.locations: Barnsley & Farmland ES (Rockville), Flora Singer & Glen Haven ES (Silver Spring), Garrett Park ES (Kensington), William Tyler Page ES (Burtonsville)
providers.hours: Full day with before/after care included; final week (Aug 10-14) 8:30am-4:30pm
providers.price_detail: Explorers $510/session, Adventure Camp $575/session (plus trip spending money); before/after care included
providers.sessions_2026: 8 weeks, June 22-Aug 14
providers.status_2026: In session; weeks through Aug 14 remain on the calendar — call about space in remaining sessions (site blocked automated verification; details confirmed via official pages in search index)

reg_2027.opens: mid-winter (typically January-February, alongside the MoCo camp-registration season)
reg_2027.notes: Less frenzied than MoCo Rec/Parks (which open Jan 20-ish at 6:30am); KAH families often register at their school-year site. Partially search-indexed verification — kidsafterhours.com blocks fetchers.

providers.fit_notes: Practical, unfussy full-coverage camp at neighborhood schools; not a specialty or nature experience. Good default for two-working-parent households near a KAH site.

providers.name: Kids On Ice / Summer Skating Camps at Fort Dupont Ice Arena
providers.org: Friends of Fort Dupont Ice Arena (with DC DPR); DC-ICE (Inner City Excellence)
providers.description: Fort Dupont Ice Arena in Ward 7 — the rink that trained generations of Black skaters east of the river — runs the Kids On Ice program providing free or heavily subsidized figure skating, hockey, and speed skating instruction, plus summer skating camps (about four one-week sessions serving 1,100+ kids). The affiliated DC-ICE camp offers a full-day skating-based day camp for ages 6-12 with 3+ hours of ice time daily. Note: the arena is transitioning under DC DPR management with programming grants, so program names and registration may shift.
providers.ages: 5-18 (Kids On Ice); 6-12 (DC-ICE full-day camp)
providers.price_detail: Kids On Ice lessons free/subsidized for economically disadvantaged children; camp fees low with sliding scale in development
providers.locations: Fort Dupont Ice Arena, 3779 Ely Pl SE, Washington DC (Fort Dupont Park, Ward 7)
providers.hours: Camp weeks with full-day options (DC-ICE)
providers.financial_aid: Kids On Ice exists to remove cost barriers — free lessons and skate loans for low-income DC kids; ask about camperships

reg_2027.opens: Spring for summer camps; Kids On Ice sessions enroll seasonally year-round
reg_2027.notes: Register via fdia.org (or DPR's Fort Dupont page as management transitions — check dpr.dc.gov/fortdupont). The free/subsidized slots go fast; DC-ICE camp registers separately at innercityexcellence.org.

providers.sessions_2026: Roughly four one-week summer camp sessions plus summer skating school, 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer sessions running; management transition to DPR means confirm current offerings by phone
providers.extended_care: DC-ICE camp is full-day; arena camps vary
providers.fit_notes: A genuinely unique east-of-the-river institution — where else does a kid skate for free in July? Best for families near Fort Dupont/Benning. Program structure is in flux with the DPR transition, so verify details each season.

providers.name: Knock On Wood Tap Studio Summer Session
providers.description: A beloved rhythm-tap institution (est. 1994) two blocks from Takoma Metro, notable for its multigenerational, diverse community and roots in Black percussive-dance tradition. Summer is a class session rather than a day camp: kids and teens can join weekly tap classes June-August, with drop-in options and special workshops (percussive dance samplers, improv jams). Best as a supplement — an inexpensive way for a kid to try tap — not a full-day childcare solution.
providers.ages: Roughly 4 through teen (classes grouped by age/level); adult classes too
providers.locations: 6925 Willow Street NW, Suite 101, Washington, DC 20012 (Takoma, 2 blocks from Takoma Metro)
providers.hours: Class-schedule based, not camp hours; summer session June 22-Aug 9, 2026 (closed July 2-5)
providers.extended_care: Not applicable — class session, not a camp
providers.price_detail: Drop-in $23/class; class cards $22/class in 5- or 10-packs
providers.sessions_2026: Summer session June 22-August 9, 2026; workshops incl. North American Percussive Dance Sampler July 14-Aug 4 and improv jam July 19
providers.status_2026: Open — drop-in and rolling registration mid-session is normal here

reg_2027.opens: spring (summer schedule posted around May)
reg_2027.notes: No sign-up race. Kids can literally drop in with tap shoes mid-summer, which makes this a great fill-in around other camps.

providers.name: Kode With Klossy (free coding camp, DC site)
providers.description: Karlie Kloss's foundation runs FREE two-week intensive coding camps (web dev, mobile apps, machine learning, data science) for young women and gender-expansive teens 13-18, with Washington DC among its in-person host cities for 2026 alongside a virtual option. No experience needed; the cohort community and instructors get rave reviews.
providers.ages: 13-18, young women and gender-expansive youth
providers.locations: Washington DC in-person site (exact host venue announced to admitted students; varies by year); virtual camps also offered
providers.hours: Full weekdays for the two-week session
providers.price_detail: Completely free
providers.status_2026: 2026 applications closed March 31; camps running June-August 2026 in DC and other cities

reg_2027.opens: typically winter (applications open by early in the year; 2026 deadline was March 31)
reg_2027.notes: Application with two short-response questions (written or video). Free and popular, so treat the deadline seriously — set a January reminder to apply.

providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions in June, July, and August 2026
providers.fit_notes: Excellent free alternative to $1,000+/week commercial tech camps for eligible teens. Boys are not eligible; families needing all-summer coverage should note it is a single two-week session.

providers.name: Labyrinth Games & Puzzles Summer Camps
providers.description: Capitol Hill's beloved board-game shop runs screen-free Board Game Camp (ages 8+) and Game Design Camp (ages 7+): mornings-to-afternoons of curated modern board games, strategy analysis, and — in design camp — building and playtesting your own game. It's a cult favorite with tiny capacity, run by people who take games seriously as a craft.
providers.ages: 7+ (Game Design) and 8+ (Board Game Camp)
providers.locations: 645 Pennsylvania Ave SE, Washington DC
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:00pm
providers.price_detail: $450/week including $30 non-refundable deposit
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long camps plus a Summer Game Club through the summer
providers.status_2026: 2026 weeks largely sold out (registration opened Jan 10); occasional openings posted on the shop blog

reg_2027.opens: early January — 2026 registration opened Saturday Jan 10 at 12:00pm sharp
reg_2027.notes: One of the fastest sellouts on Capitol Hill — spots go within days; be online at the posted noon opening. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: After-care offered per the camps page — confirm hours
providers.fit_notes: Perfect for strategy-minded, screen-averse kids; a full week of tabletop is heaven for the right kid and torture for one who needs to run around.

providers.name: Landon Summer
providers.description: One of the largest school-run summer operations in the region: a 75-acre Bethesda campus with dozens of day, sports, specialty, and academic camps for boys and girls, plus an 8-week bus network across the area. Its mix-and-match weekly structure and sheer variety make it a one-stop shop for building an entire summer.
providers.ages: 4-16 (varies by camp)
providers.locations: Landon School, 6101 Wilson Lane, Bethesda MD 20817
providers.price_detail: Per-camp pricing in CampBrain; sessions run one to six weeks
providers.hours: Core camp day; extended day and bus options
providers.sessions_2026: 9 weeks of programs; buses run 8 weeks, June 15-Aug 7, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration stays open until noon the Wednesday before each session if space remains — real chance of finding late-July/early-August openings in the big catalog

reg_2027.opens: Mid-January (2026: early registration Jan 15 at 1pm for returning/current families, general Jan 16 at 11am)
reg_2027.notes: Two-tier opening gives returning families a one-day head start; popular sports weeks go fast. CampBrain, card/ACH only. Bus seats are separate and also sell out.

providers.fit_notes: Coed in summer despite being a boys' school; the go-to for sporty kids and families needing busing. Huge scale means less intimate than smaller camps.

providers.name: LAYC Summer Youth Programs
providers.description: The region's anchor bilingual youth-development organization runs free summer opportunities out of its Columbia Heights headquarters and Maryland centers: outdoor trips, learn-to-row programs, college-life visits, leadership development, arts, and its free DC Teen Center drop-in space. Programming is bilingual (English/Spanish) and built for immigrant and first-generation families, with wraparound services (case management, health, workforce) attached.
providers.ages: Mostly middle and high school (roughly 11-24 depending on program)
providers.price_detail: Core youth programs free to DC youth; some Maryland programs also free
providers.locations: HQ 1419 Columbia Rd NW (Columbia Heights); LAYC Maryland Multicultural Youth Centers serve Montgomery and Prince George's counties
providers.hours: Varies by program; Teen Center operates drop-in hours
providers.financial_aid: Programs are free; no aid application needed

reg_2027.opens: Rolling; summer-specific opportunities (rowing, outdoor trips, college visits) posted on the Youth Corner page in spring
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment is low-barrier — walk in, call, or sign up via the Youth Corner page. Spanish-speaking staff throughout. Capacity varies per activity, so ask in April-May for summer trips.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 slate includes outdoor exploration, learn-to-row, college immersion, and leadership programs
providers.status_2026: Teen Center and most summer programs accept youth on a rolling basis through the summer
providers.fit_notes: Best free option for Latino/immigrant middle- and high-schoolers in Columbia Heights and inner Montgomery County who want mentorship and activities without cost or paperwork barriers. Younger elementary kids are not the audience.

providers.name: LEAF Youth Program at Common Good City Farm
providers.description: A free, drop-in-friendly farm education program (Learning for the Environment, Agriculture and Food) on a working urban farm in LeDroit Park, where elementary-age kids garden, harvest, cook, and eat together on weekday afternoons. It runs in spring/summer/fall sessions rather than as an all-day camp. A rare zero-cost way for city kids to get real dirt-under-the-fingernails farm time.
providers.ages: Elementary-age focus (roughly 5-12); younger and older welcome
providers.locations: Common Good City Farm, V St NW between 2nd & 4th, LeDroit Park, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Free; parents fill out a registration form in advance or at first visit
providers.hours: Tue/Thu/Sun 4:30-6:00pm; Wed 3:30-6:30pm (not a full-day program)
providers.sessions_2026: Spring, summer, and fall sessions; summer session running now
providers.status_2026: Open now — drop-ins welcome as capacity allows; sign up for one or more days per session.

reg_2027.opens: Rolling; session signups post on the farm's site each season
reg_2027.notes: No race for spots — commit to a weekly day or drop in. Also note their paid teen youth-employment program for older kids.

providers.fit_notes: An after-camp/afternoon supplement, not childcare or a full-day camp; perfect for families near LeDroit Park/Shaw wanting free, recurring farm time.

providers.name: Learn24 / MOST-DC Free Summer Programs & OST Youth Scholarships
providers.org: DC Office of Out of School Time Grants and Youth Outcomes (with Greater Washington Community Foundation)
providers.description: DC's umbrella for free and subsidized summer programming: the MOST-DC portal (most.dc.gov) is the one-stop search-and-register hub for thousands of K-12 out-of-school-time programs, over 145 of them grant-funded to be free in 2026. Separately, the Learn24 OST Youth Scholarship (administered by the Greater Washington Community Foundation) pays $1,000-$3,000 toward one fee-based summer program per student — up to $5,000 for summer programs that solely serve youth with disabilities (year-round programs qualify for up to $5,000, or $10,000 disability-specific). This is the first place a DC family with a tight budget should look.
providers.locations: Programs citywide across all 8 wards; portal-based
providers.price_detail: Funded programs are free; scholarships $1,000-$3,000 for summer programs (up to $5,000 for programs solely serving youth with disabilities); year-round programs up to $5,000 ($10,000 disability-specific)
providers.financial_aid: This IS the aid: OST Youth Scholarship applications reviewed on a rolling basis at thecommunityfoundation.org/learn24-ost; awards based on individual need and fund availability (minimum award $1,000)
providers.hours: Varies by program
providers.sessions_2026: FY2026: ~$27M invested across 145+ organizations serving 18,000+ DC youth; DCPS free summer enrichment (e.g., CTE Robotics, Mini Medics for rising 7th-9th)
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 programs running; scholarship applications reviewed rolling while funds last — some free programs accept late enrollment

reg_2027.opens: summer scholarship application opens early March (2026: March 2); school-year/year-round application opens late October (2025: Oct 20); portal listings and Summer Strong grants announced late winter-spring
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling (varies by program; scholarship applications reviewed on a rolling basis while funds last)
reg_2027.notes: Mark the early-March opening for the summer scholarship and apply immediately before funds deplete; the disability supplement (up to $5,000 for summer, $10,000 year-round) is one of the region's least-known aid sources. DC residents only. Verified via learn24.dc.gov and Community Foundation indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: Essential starting point for any DC-resident family priced out of private camps, and for families of kids with disabilities seeking the enhanced scholarship. Quality varies program-to-program — vet individual providers on the portal.

providers.name: LearnServe International (Fellows, Incubator & Summer Abroad)
providers.description: A DC-based social-entrepreneurship pipeline: teens learn to design and pitch their own venture tackling a social problem (Fellows/Incubator), and graduates can join two-week summer service-learning trips to Zambia, Costa Rica, or Paraguay, developing a community action project with local partners. Free for DCPS and DC public charter students; everyone else pays a sliding scale from $50 to $1,250. Thrives: idealistic self-starters who want substance — and a rare genuinely affordable international summer experience.
providers.ages: Middle and high school students; Abroad trips are for high schoolers who completed the Incubator (~14-18)
providers.price_detail: Free for DCPS/DC charter students; sliding scale $50-$1,250 for others based on ability to pay; Abroad trip costs separate with substantial aid
providers.locations: Programs in-person and virtual across DC region; summer trips in Zambia, Costa Rica, Paraguay
providers.sessions_2026: Abroad: two-week trips in summer 2026; Fellows/Incubator run during the school year
providers.status_2026: 2026 Abroad cohorts were set months ago (trips underway or imminent); school-year Fellows applications for fall open in late summer.

reg_2027.opens: Fellows applications in late summer/fall 2026 — which is itself the pathway to the summer 2027 Abroad trips
reg_2027.notes: Plan a year ahead: teens generally must complete the school-year Fellows/Incubator program before a summer Abroad trip. For a newcomer family, applying in fall 2026 is the move for summer 2027.

providers.financial_aid: Core model: free for DC public/charter students, sliding scale $50-$1,250 for all others
providers.fit_notes: The most economically accessible 'summer abroad' in the region, but not an on-demand purchase — it rewards families who plan a full year out.

providers.name: Lessans Camp JCC (Inclusion & Kochavim programs)
providers.description: The flagship Jewish day camp of close-in Montgomery County, and the region's best-known fully inclusive mainstream camp: its nationally recognized Inclusion program provides individualized 1:1 support so campers with disabilities join typical bunks, while the award-winning Kochavim bunk serves teens and young adults ages 12-21 with 1:1 or 2:1 counselor support, keeping them alongside neurotypical campers while building socialization and independent-living skills (an Avodah track adds supported work experience). Activities span art, theater, cooking, STEM, music, dance, nature, swimming, and a rock wall on the Rockville campus; open to all faiths and a strong first choice for a family that wants one camp for siblings with and without disabilities.
providers.ages: 4-21 (Pre-K through entering 12th grade; Kochavim ages 12-21)
providers.locations: Bender JCC campus, 6125 Montrose Rd, Rockville MD
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates not published in verifiable indexed copies; $200 non-refundable deposit per camper, installment plan (2026 installments charged Jan 21, Feb 18, Apr 22). Member discounts apply.
providers.financial_aid: Camperships/financial assistance available through the JCC and Jewish Federation channels (One Happy Camper-style grants for Jewish overnight camps do not apply here); contact camp office for aid details — specifics not verified online.
providers.hours: Full-day camp; extended care historically offered (verify with camp)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care typically available at Bender JCC camps — confirm with camp office
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - Aug 7, 2026 in 3 sessions (Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-Aug 7) plus pre/post-camp weeks for Pre-K-entering 6th
providers.status_2026: Season underway (runs through Aug 7 plus post-camp weeks); inclusion slots are capacity-limited — contact camp office about any remaining Session 3/post-camp space

reg_2027.opens: early December (Summer 2026 applications opened to the public Wed Dec 3 at noon; expect a similar early-December date for 2027)
reg_2027.notes: Registration opens 'to the public' in early December — JCC members typically get earlier or priority access, so confirm member timing if you plan to join. Inclusion program slots are limited by support-staff capacity and fill much earlier than general enrollment — contact the inclusion coordinator in the fall to discuss your child's needs before registering. Verified via search-indexed copies of benderjccgw.org.

providers.fit_notes: Best for families who want real inclusion in a big, high-energy mainstream camp with Jewish content (all faiths welcome). Kochavim gives teens/young adults 12-21 a supported bunk of their own while still participating in camp-wide activities. Not a therapy program — kids who need clinical-level behavioral support may be better served by Camp Accomplish or public therapeutic rec camps.

providers.name: Library of Congress High School Summer Internship
providers.description: A selective ~4-week summer internship for about 12 high schoolers with the world's largest library, run mostly online with a handful of local students joining on-site on Capitol Hill several times. Interns work with the Informal Learning Office on real library projects - a serious, free resume-builder for book- and history-loving teens (the paid Junior Fellows program is college-level).
providers.ages: Current high school students (and graduating seniors) age 16+
providers.locations: Virtual nationwide; hybrid on-site visits at the Library of Congress, Capitol Hill, for selected local students
providers.price_detail: Free/unpaid internship
providers.hours: Mon-Thu, 10 am-3:30 pm ET for ~4 weeks (2025 pattern: late June to mid-July)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 session details not independently verified; program recurs annually

reg_2027.opens: Applications historically open in winter (December-March) via the LOC families blog and internships page
reg_2027.notes: Only ~12 students nationally, so treat it like a competitive application: essays/recommendations ready by early winter. Local students should flag interest in the hybrid slots.

providers.status_2026: 2026 application cycle closed; program runs midsummer. National Archives, by comparison, offers no youth summer program (its internships are college-level), so LOC is the main 'documents and libraries' teen option.
providers.fit_notes: For self-directed teens comfortable with a mostly-virtual professional setting; not childcare and not hands-on craft. Verified via LOC official blog posts (2025 cycle) - 2026 specifics unconfirmed.

providers.name: Life Pieces to Masterpieces Summer Camp
providers.description: A nationally recognized arts-based development program for Black boys and young men from Wards 7 and 8, built around collaborative painting ('Life Pieces') plus academics and character curriculum. The six-week summer camp runs 8am-4pm and, like everything at Life Pieces, is completely free for families. Boys can join as young as 3 and many stay through young adulthood.
providers.ages: Boys and young men, roughly 3-25 (summer camp is school-age focused)
providers.price_detail: Free for youth and families, including the 6-week summer camp
providers.locations: East of the river; programming has operated near Kenilworth/Deanwood (NE) serving Wards 7 & 8
providers.hours: Summer camp 8:00am-4:00pm, six weeks
providers.financial_aid: Entirely free; mission-restricted to Black boys/young men from Wards 7 & 8

reg_2027.opens: Enrollment inquiries year-round; summer intake typically arranged in spring through the organization directly
reg_2027.notes: There is no public registration portal — families contact Life Pieces to enroll ('apprentices' join the year-round community, with summer camp included). Capacity is limited; start the conversation by early spring 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Six-week camp, 8am-4pm, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 camp serving enrolled apprentices; contact directly about any remaining openings
providers.extended_care: 8-4 day already long; no separate aftercare published
providers.fit_notes: One of the best free full-day summers in Wards 7/8 for Black boys — art-centered, deeply relational, year-round community. Only for boys/young men; girls should look at Washington School for Girls or THEARC partners.

providers.name: Lions Camp Merrick
providers.description: A Lions Clubs-supported overnight camp in Nanjemoy (Charles County, ~1 hr from DC) with specialized week-long programs: a diabetes camp for ages 6-16 (with ADA support and a session for families of newly diagnosed kids, historically 'Camp Glyndon'), and a Deaf/Hard-of-Hearing/KODA program hosting ~90 youth per week. Lions fundraising keeps fees far below typical overnight-camp rates.
providers.ages: 6-16 (diabetes program); youth weeks for Deaf/HOH/KODA; some adult/PTSD programs
providers.locations: 3650 Rick Hamilton Pl, Nanjemoy MD 20662 (Southern Maryland; draws from the whole DC region)
providers.price_detail: Heavily subsidized by Lions Clubs and the ADA; fees vary by program with aid available
providers.financial_aid: Substantial — subsidy is the founding purpose; camperships available
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions across the summer: three diabetes weeks plus family session; Deaf/HOH/KODA weeks (~90 campers each)
providers.status_2026: Season underway; 'Register now' was live on the official site — late-session space possible, call the office

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration typically opens by January-February)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come with medical/audiology forms per program
reg_2027.notes: The diabetes weeks coordinate with endocrinology clinics; ask your care team for referral timing. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For T1D kids and Deaf/HOH kids (plus hearing kids of Deaf adults) who deserve a normal sleepaway week with staff who natively handle their needs; rustic riverside camp, not a resort.

providers.name: Locust Grove Nature Center Summer Camps
providers.description: The close-in Bethesda option among the county's five nature-center camps, tucked into Cabin John Regional Park — naturalist-led weeks of hikes, live animals, crafts and forest play for preschoolers through elementary kids. Its location makes it the most convenient public nature camp for Bethesda/Chevy Chase/Potomac families, and it fills accordingly.
providers.ages: preschool through elementary grades, age-banded by week
providers.locations: Locust Grove Nature Center, 7777 Democracy Blvd, Bethesda, MD 20817 (Cabin John Regional Park)
providers.hours: Typically 9am-3/3:30pm; some half-day preschool weeks
providers.extended_care: Aftercare for select camps — check listing
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on ActiveMONTGOMERY (comparable Parks nature weeks ~$350 in 2026); $25 early-bird by April 3, 2026
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (need-based, ages 3-18; 2026 fund fully awarded)

reg_2027.opens: late January (Jan 20, 2026 pattern), 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Because it is the Bethesda-side nature camp, Locust Grove weeks are typically among the first Parks camps to sell out — treat opening morning as mandatory. Early-bird discount by early April; Dream Camp scholarship opens mid-January.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; assume full with scattered cancellation seats via ActiveMONTGOMERY's openings filter (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Perfect for younger nature-lovers in the Bethesda/Potomac area; small and nurturing rather than adventure-intensive.

providers.name: Lowell Summer
providers.description: A nurturing, play-forward camp on Lowell School's 8 wooded acres bordering Rock Creek Park in NW DC, serving an unusually wide range from age 2¾ through 15 in two-week sessions. Its progressive, whole-child ethos and forest-edge campus give it a gentler feel than the big sports-driven school camps.
providers.locations: Lowell School, 1640 Kalmia Road NW, Washington DC 20012 (borders Rock Creek Park)
providers.price_detail: 2026: from $765 half-day / $1,035 full-day per two-week session (~$380-520/week); 25% non-refundable deposit
providers.hours: Half- and full-day; before/after camp care offered
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions across the summer of 2026
providers.status_2026: First-come enrollment ran from January; unfilled spots released to waitlist after May 1 payment deadline — call about remaining late sessions

reg_2027.opens: Early January (2026: Lowell families Jan 5 at 9am, public Jan 12 at 9am)
reg_2027.notes: One-week head start for school families; youngest (preschool) groups fill fastest. Two-week session structure — plan in fortnights.

providers.fit_notes: One of very few quality options for kids under 3.5, and a haven for gentle, nature-loving kids; upper Rock Creek location is easiest for 16th Street/Shepherd Park/Takoma families.

providers.name: Mad Science of Washington DC Summer Camps
providers.org: Mad Science of Washington DC (Silver Spring-based franchise)
providers.description: A DC-area institution for decades: classic wow-factor science camps (rockets, chemistry, robots, dinosaurs, secret-agent lab, the NASA-designed space academy week) at neighborhood sites across DC and close-in Maryland. The go-to affordable science option for elementary kids — more demonstrations, slime, and launches than screens.
providers.ages: Grades 1-5 per most 2026 listings (some partner sites list 1-6); roughly ages 6-11
providers.locations: Multiple sites each summer including Tenleytown (NW DC), Chevy Chase, Eastern Market/Hill Center (Capitol Hill), and Silver Spring (HQ: 12065 Tech Rd)
providers.hours: Full day 9:00 am - 4:00 pm; half day 9-12 or 1-4 at some sites
providers.price_detail: $315-$390/week (2026); $30/week multi-week discount with code multicamp2026; Red Hot Robots adds $15 materials fee
providers.extended_care: Before care 8:00-9:00 am and after care 4:00-6:00 pm available at all camps (confirmed for 2026; e.g. Hill Center listing runs 8 am - 6 pm)
providers.status_2026: Season running now (8 themed weeks: Crazy Chemworks, NASA Academy, Red Hot Robots, Eureka!, Secret Agent Lab, Dinosaurs, Flight Academy, Science in Motion). Core season June 22 - July 31 at most sites, with extra August weeks at Tenleytown (Dinosaurs Aug 10-14, NASA Aug 17-21); some mid/late-July and August weeks still bookable online.

reg_2027.opens: typically January-February
reg_2027.notes: First-come online. NASA and robots weeks at the DC sites fill first (by late spring); Silver Spring HQ site usually holds space longest. One of the better values in the sector — book popular weeks by March.

providers.sessions_2026: 8 themed weeks; June 22 - July 31 at most sites plus August weeks at Tenleytown
providers.fit_notes: Perfect for curious elementary kids who want explosions and rockets, not rigor. Ends around grade 5 — older kids should look at iD Tech, KID Museum, or Montgomery College.

providers.name: Maret Summer Programs
providers.description: Week-long enrichment camps and sports camps taught largely by Maret faculty on the school's leafy Woodley Park campus — Minecraft/tech, academics, arts, basketball, football, and more for ages 6-16. Smaller and simpler than the mega school camps, with some of the most reasonable price points among DC private-school summers.
providers.ages: 6-16 (varies by camp; e.g. Minecraft rising grades 2-7, basketball ages 8-16)
providers.locations: Maret School, 3000 Cathedral Ave NW, Washington DC (Woodley Park)
providers.price_detail: 2026 samples: full-day camps ~$450/week, half-day ~$350, boys basketball $425 — notably below the $600+ area norm
providers.hours: Typically 9:00am-3:00pm (some camps Mon-Thu full day + Fri half day)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, roughly June 15-July 31, 2026 (Juneteenth and July 4 short weeks)
providers.status_2026: Season in progress through late July; individual camps may still have space — the catalog shows per-camp availability

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January-February); not formally announced
reg_2027.notes: Straightforward first-come online signup; smaller program means less frenzy than Landon/Bullis but individual popular camps (Minecraft) still fill.

providers.fit_notes: Great value and a walkable option for Woodley Park/Cleveland Park families; smaller menu and shorter season than the big Bethesda operations.

providers.name: Mariner Sailing School Youth Camps (Belle Haven Marina)
providers.description: Half-day youth sailing camps in 14-foot Sunfish out of Belle Haven Marina, just south of Old Town Alexandria beside the Dyke Marsh wildlife preserve — on light-wind days kids paddle canoes/kayaks through the marsh or swim. Basic and advanced weeks run continuously from June into the first week of September, later than almost anyone else. Morning (10-1) and afternoon (1:30-4:30) sessions.
