providers.ages: Roughly 8-16 (youth basic and advanced tracks)
providers.locations: Belle Haven Marina, George Washington Memorial Pkwy, Alexandria, VA
providers.price_detail: Half-day week format; 2026 rates on saildc.com not directly verified
providers.hours: Morning 10:00am-1:00pm or afternoon 1:30-4:30pm
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly through the first week of September
providers.status_2026: Running weekly into early September with rolling enrollment — among the best remaining 2026 options for a late-summer week.

reg_2027.opens: Spring; rolling all season
reg_2027.notes: Low-stress rolling enrollment — this is the camp you can still book in June. Half-days pair well with a morning or afternoon at home.

providers.fit_notes: Small, old-school, and flexible; half-day only, so not a childcare solution. The Dyke Marsh setting adds real nature to the sailing.

providers.name: Mary's Center Teen Program (summer)
providers.description: The community health center that anchors DC's Latino immigrant community runs a free year-round Teen Program — a safe after-school and summer space with computer classes, career fairs, college campus trips, health education, and mentoring, integrated with Mary's Center's medical and social services. Summer programming continues the drop-in/enrollment model at its DC and Silver Spring sites.
providers.ages: Teens (roughly 12-19)
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.locations: Mary's Center sites in DC (Adams Morgan/Petworth area) and Silver Spring, MD
providers.hours: Program hours vary by site and season

reg_2027.opens: Rolling year-round
reg_2027.notes: Teens join by contacting the Teen Program through maryscenter.org or any Mary's Center site; bilingual staff, no immigration-status barriers. Not capacity-constrained the way camps are.

providers.sessions_2026: Ongoing teen programming through summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Open, rolling enrollment
providers.fit_notes: A trusted, no-cost landing place for immigrant teens — especially newcomer families already using Mary's Center for healthcare. It is enrichment and mentorship, not a structured 9-to-4 camp.

providers.name: Maryland Boys State & Maryland Girls State
providers.org: American Legion Department of Maryland / American Legion Auxiliary
providers.description: A week-long residential immersion in state government for rising seniors — participants build mock cities, run for office, draft legislation, and elect a governor, with alumni networks full of governors and senators. Boys State runs at McDaniel College (Westminster) and Girls State at Salisbury University; local American Legion posts sponsor participants, so the week is typically free to families. Standouts can be selected for Boys/Girls Nation in DC. Montgomery County students apply through the state program online.
providers.ages: Rising high school seniors (approx. 17)
providers.locations: Boys State: McDaniel College, Westminster, MD; Girls State: Salisbury University, Salisbury, MD (residential; serves students statewide incl. Montgomery County)
providers.price_detail: Sponsored by local American Legion posts — typically no cost to families
providers.sessions_2026: Boys State: June 21-27, 2026; Girls State: June 21-26, 2026
providers.status_2026: Closed — applications ran Nov 15, 2025-May 1, 2026; the 2026 sessions concluded in late June.

reg_2027.opens: Around November 15, 2026; applications due May 1, 2027
reg_2027.notes: Apply at mdboysgirlsstate.org; a local Legion post sponsors you (the program helps match). Not a lottery and rarely 'full' — but strong applications and early submission matter, and it is a genuine college-application differentiator.

providers.fit_notes: Best for civically engaged rising seniors comfortable with a structured, somewhat old-school residential week; only one summer of eligibility, so juniors must plan ahead. Verified via official mdlegion.org and mdboysgirlsstate.org (search-indexed).

providers.name: Maryland Writing Project Summer Writers Camps
providers.org: Maryland Writing Project at Towson University (National Writing Project site)
providers.description: Teacher-led creative-writing camps run by the National Writing Project's Maryland site: one-week half-day camps on the Towson University campus for kids who love to write, plus a genuinely unusual self-paced virtual/asynchronous camp that runs all summer for grades 4-12 at a reduced cost. The online option is one of the region's best-value writing programs and works from anywhere.
providers.ages: Grades 3-12 (in-person: grades 3-7 and 8-12 weeks; async: grades 4-12)
providers.locations: In-person at Towson University (Baltimore County — a drive from DC, ~1 hr); asynchronous camp is fully online
providers.price_detail: In-person: $300 for one week half-day (9am-noon). Virtual/asynchronous camp offered at reduced cost — listed at $100 for the whole summer (June 23-Aug 22, 2026); confirm current price on the MWP page.
providers.hours: 9:00am-noon in person; async is self-paced
providers.sessions_2026: Grades 3-7: July 20-24; Grades 8-12: July 27-31; Async: June 23-Aug 22
providers.status_2026: Both in-person weeks are still ahead (late July) — check the site for open seats; async camp enrolls all summer

reg_2027.opens: February 1 (per Towson University's summer-camps guide, registration opens Feb. 1)
reg_2027.notes: Online first-come registration through Towson opens around Feb 1; small program, modest marketing, so it fills quietly rather than instantly. The low-cost async camp is the sleeper pick for DC families — no commute to Towson needed.

providers.fit_notes: In-person camps are a real drive from DC — mainly worth it for families toward the Baltimore side, or pick the async camp from anywhere. Half-day only, so pair with other coverage.

providers.name: Maryland Youth Ballet Summer Programs
providers.description: One of the most respected classical ballet schools on the East Coast, in downtown Silver Spring above the Ellsworth Dr shops. Little kids (roughly 3-9) get weekly story-ballet camps built around Nutcracker/Swan Lake-style tales, while trained dancers audition for the serious 5-week Academy intensive that culminates at Wolf Trap's Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods. The children's camps are genuinely beginner-friendly; the Academy track requires about 3 years of prior training.
providers.ages: 2-9 for Early Dance/Children's story-ballet weeks; roughly 10-18 for Academy intensive (levels 3-6, min. 3 years training)
providers.locations: 926 Ellsworth Dr, Silver Spring, MD 20910 (Metro-walkable, downtown Silver Spring)
providers.hours: Children's camps are partial-day; Academy intensive runs full weekdays for 5 weeks
providers.price_detail: 2026 Academy intensive: $2,100-2,750 for 5 weeks (~$420-550/wk) depending on level and MYB-student status; children's weekly camp tuition set each spring ($40 deposit); early-bird discount if registered by April 15
providers.financial_aid: Limited summer scholarships available for the Academy intensive; inquire early
providers.sessions_2026: Academy: 5-week intensive (full-program or by-the-week option); Children's: weekly story-ballet camps through summer
providers.status_2026: Season underway as of mid-July 2026; the 5-week Academy program began in June, but by-the-week children's camps for late July/August may still have openings — call the office

reg_2027.opens: February 1 at 9:00 am
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online; audition required for Academy intensive placement (new students)
reg_2027.notes: Popular children's weeks fill through spring; early-bird pricing ends April 15. New-to-MYB dancers wanting the intensive must audition — schedule that in winter/early spring.

providers.name: Matthew Harris Ornstein Summer Debate Institute (WUDL)
providers.description: The Washington Urban Debate League's annual summer institute teaches policy debate to middle and high schoolers in leveled labs — Rookie/Novice for total beginners through Varsity for returning competitors — with a deep dive on the coming season's national topic. WUDL programming is free of charge for public school students, meals included, making this the region's most accessible serious debate training. Thrives: argumentative kids of every experience level; it doubles as the on-ramp to WUDL's school-year league.
providers.ages: Middle and high school students (~11-18), leveled labs
providers.price_detail: Free for public school students, including breakfast and lunch (private-school terms: ask WUDL)
providers.locations: Hosted at a partner university/school site in DC (venue announced each year)
providers.sessions_2026: Typically 1-2 weeks in late July/August (2026 dates on WUDL site; not verified here)
providers.status_2026: Likely still open or opening — the institute traditionally runs in late summer as the season kickoff; check the how-to-apply page now for 2026 registration.

reg_2027.opens: spring (applications posted on WUDL's camp page)
reg_2027.notes: Low-barrier signup relative to demand. Families wanting elite competitive intensives instead should look at the Georgetown Debate Seminar (residential, applications open in November, selective, costly) or Capitol Debate's DC camp (commercial, July session).

providers.fit_notes: The rare truly free academic summer program that welcomes absolute beginners. Late-summer timing conveniently fills the August dead zone after other camps end.

providers.name: Maydale Nature Classroom Summer Camps
providers.description: The smallest and least-known of the county's five nature-center camp sites, at Maydale Conservation Park in Colesville (east county, near Silver Spring) — hands-on natural and cultural history programming with live animal exhibits, native plant gardens, trails and a nature play area. Its lower profile makes it a genuine insider tip when the famous nature centers are full.
providers.ages: preschool through elementary grades, age-banded by week (verify per listing)
providers.locations: Maydale Nature Classroom, 1638 Maydale Dr, Colesville, MD 20905 (Maydale Conservation Park)
providers.hours: Typically 9am-3/3:30pm; varies by week
providers.extended_care: Aftercare for select camps — check listing
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on ActiveMONTGOMERY (comparable Parks nature weeks ~$350 in 2026)
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (need-based, ages 3-18; 2026 fund fully awarded)

reg_2027.opens: late January (Jan 20, 2026 pattern), 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Lower demand than Brookside/Locust Grove — a good fallback if you miss opening morning, and a good first choice for east-county families.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; check ActiveMONTGOMERY openings filter (availability not verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Quiet, small-group nature immersion; facilities are simpler than the flagship centers, which some families prefer.

providers.name: Mayor Marion S. Barry Summer Youth Employment Program (MBSYEP)
providers.description: DC's flagship paid summer jobs program places 14-24-year-old District residents in six-week subsidized jobs across government agencies, nonprofits, and private employers — this is the default answer to 'what does a DC 15-year-old do all summer.' Teens who pick the 'Environmental Jobs' preference join the Green Zone Environmental Program (GZEP), DOEE's conservation crew that builds rain gardens, plants trees, removes invasives, and does green-career training three field days a week. Thrives: teens who want a real paycheck, work experience, and structure; it is a job, not a camp.
providers.ages: 14-24 (tracks for 14-15, 16-19, 20-24; high schoolers dominate the younger tracks)
providers.price_detail: Free to join — participants are PAID (hourly wage by age tier). GZEP participants are paid through the same program.
providers.hours: Varies by placement; program runs weekdays during the 6-week window
providers.locations: Job sites citywide in all 8 wards; GZEP field sites across DC watersheds
providers.sessions_2026: June 29 - August 7, 2026 (single 6-week session)
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 application ran Jan 26-Mar 6 and the program is currently in session (Jun 29-Aug 7). No mid-summer entry.

reg_2027.opens: late January (2026 cycle opened at noon on Jan 26)
reg_2027.notes: Apply online at summerjobs.dc.gov by early March (2026 deadline was Mar 6 at 11:59pm, no extensions), then complete document certification (age, DC residency, work eligibility, parental consent) — in 2026 orientation ran Apr 4-May 22 with final documents due May 26; missing certification is the most common way teens lose their spot. DC residents only. Most eligible applicants who finish certification get placed; to get GZEP specifically, select Environmental Jobs as your site preference by mid-May (2026 job-site preferences were due May 15).

providers.fit_notes: Only for DC residents — Maryland families cannot use this (see Summer RISE/TeenWorks for MoCo). Placements vary widely in quality; teens with a specific interest should research host sites and rank preferences early. Ages 14-15 get more structured cohort experiences.

providers.name: MCPL Summer Reading Challenge & Teen Summer Programs (Montgomery County Public Libraries)
providers.description: The county library system's free summer layer: the all-ages Summer Reading Challenge (June 13-Aug 21, 2026; 2026 theme 'Unearth A Story' — dinosaurs, archaeology, paleontology) with prizes via the ReadSquared app, plus teen-specific offerings — a teen writing contest for middle/high schoolers, Teen Advisory Boards, and SSL-hour volunteer roles helping run summer reading at branches like Gaithersburg. Not childcare, but the best free enrichment scaffold for summer weeks between camps.
providers.ages: all ages for the reading challenge; teen programs for middle/high schoolers (SSL volunteering typically 13/14+)
providers.locations: All 21 MCPL branches countywide + online via ReadSquared
providers.hours: Branch hours; challenge runs June 13-Aug 21, 2026
providers.price_detail: Free

reg_2027.opens: challenge kickoff typically mid-June (June 13 in 2026); teen volunteer signups post in late spring via the Montgomery County Volunteer Center
reg_2027.notes: No competition for spots on the reading challenge itself — just sign up on ReadSquared anytime. Teen SSL volunteer slots for summer reading ARE limited and claimed in May-June; apply via montgomerycountymd.galaxydigital.com.

providers.status_2026: Live now — challenge runs through Aug 21, 2026; teens can still join mid-summer and some branch volunteer roles were still posted (verified via search-indexed listings, July 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Zero-cost, zero-pressure; pairs well with any camp schedule. Teens needing SSL hours who missed camp-CIT deadlines should look here.

providers.name: MCYO Summer Strings (Strathmore)
providers.org: Maryland Classic Youth Orchestras of Strathmore
providers.description: A one-week string orchestra intensive at the Music Center at Strathmore run by MCYO, the region's oldest and largest youth orchestra (founded 1946): Junior week for rising 3rd-6th graders and Senior week for rising 6th-8th graders, with ensemble rehearsals, a composition lab, and a culminating performance in a world-class venue. It doubles as a friendly on-ramp toward MCYO's auditioned year-round orchestras.
providers.ages: Rising grades 3-6 (Junior) and rising 6-8 (Senior); string players with some experience
providers.locations: Music Center at Strathmore, 5301 Tuckerman Ln, North Bethesda MD
providers.price_detail: $775 for the one-week program (most recent verified rate); free to apply
providers.hours: Full-day week-long program (exact hours vary by year)
providers.financial_aid: Strathmore offers limited scholarships across education programs; camp-specific aid not confirmed
providers.sessions_2026: Historically one week in early-mid July (e.g., July 8-12 in a prior year); 2026 dates not verifiable via indexed copies
providers.status_2026: 2026 session likely already completed (early July); status unverified - confirm with MCYO@strathmore.org

reg_2027.opens: Spring (applications via Strathmore/Acceptd)
reg_2027.notes: Free application; MCYO contacts accepted students with a tuition-payment link. For the year-round orchestras (grades 3-12), auditions run via Acceptd in late spring - the summer week is a good audition warm-up.

providers.fit_notes: For violin/viola/cello/bass kids with at least a year or two of playing who want orchestral experience in a spectacular hall. Not for total beginners or non-string players.

providers.name: Meadowbrook Stables Summer Riding Camps
providers.org: Meadowbrook Stables (nonprofit, on Montgomery Parks land)
providers.description: A storied 1934 stable tucked inside Rock Creek Park at the DC line in Chevy Chase, offering half-day Mini Camps (ages 6-9), Full Day Camps (10-14), and Show Camps (9-16) that mix daily riding instruction with real horsemanship — grooming, tacking, barn care. It is the closest full riding camp to most of NW DC and one of the hardest camp registrations in the area. With Potomac Horse Center's 2024 closure, close-in equestrian capacity is scarcer than ever.
providers.ages: 6-9 (Mini Camp, half day); 10-14 (Full Day); 9-16 (Show Camp)
providers.locations: 8200 Meadowbrook Ln, Chevy Chase MD (Rock Creek Park, just over the DC line from 16th St)
providers.price_detail: Full Day Camp and Show Camp $725/week; half-day Mini Camps less
providers.hours: Full day 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM; Mini Camps half day (AM or PM, e.g. 1:00-4:00 PM)
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions June-August 2026 (e.g., PM Mini Camp Session 7 July 27-31)
providers.status_2026: Mostly full — most weeks sold out shortly after winter registration; occasional late-session mini-camp spots (a July 27-31 PM Mini Camp was still listed) — email to check cancellations

reg_2027.opens: Early in the year (registration 'goes live in early' January-February)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online and famously fast — horse camps this close to DC are scarce, so returning families snap up weeks within days. Watch the site in December-January, have your session choices ranked, and register the day it opens.

providers.fit_notes: The dream camp for horse-obsessed kids in DC/Chevy Chase/Bethesda; expensive, sells out brutally fast, and half-day mini camps don't solve childcare. Total-beginner teens should ask about placement first.

providers.name: Meadowside Nature Center Summer Camps
providers.description: Nature camps at Rock Creek Regional Park's big nature center near Rockville/Derwood — including 'Mission Possible' (new for 2026), where kids build observation, teamwork and survival skills and put them to the test on outdoor missions, plus preschool-to-elementary weeks like Nature's Playground. Meadowside's raptor aviary and trail network make it one of the county's richest nature-camp settings.
providers.ages: preschool through elementary/middle grades, age-banded by week
providers.locations: Meadowside Nature Center, 5100 Meadowside Lane, Rockville, MD 20855 (Rock Creek Regional Park)
providers.hours: Typically 9am-3/3:30pm full-day weeks; some half-day preschool sessions
providers.extended_care: Aftercare for select camps — check listing
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on ActiveMONTGOMERY (comparable Parks nature weeks ~$350 in 2026); $25 early-bird by April 3, 2026
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (need-based, ages 3-18; 2026 applications opened Jan 13; fund fully awarded)

reg_2027.opens: late January (Jan 20, 2026 pattern), 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Same drill as all Parks nature camps: register at 6:30am on opening day, aim for the early-bird discount, and apply for the Dream Camp scholarship in mid-January if needed. Watch for cancellation openings in spring. Mission Possible is best for kids comfortable spending several hours outside and doing both cooperative and competitive activities.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; weeks largely full since spring — check ActiveMONTGOMERY openings filter for remaining seats (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: 'Mission Possible' suits adventurous elementary kids who want challenges, not just crafts. Setting is genuinely woodsy despite the Rockville address.

providers.name: Middle C Music Summer Camps
providers.description: Tenleytown's beloved neighborhood music store and school runs a summer slate of small camps: Rock Band Camp and Songwriting Camp (ages 10-16, 10am-2pm with a Friday-evening concert), Ukulele & Piano camp for ages 3-6 (ukulele included in tuition), and a Mastering Music Theory course. Weekly enrollment runs nearly all summer, making it one of upper-NW DC's most flexible music options.
providers.ages: 3-6 (Ukulele & Piano), 10-15 (Rock Band), 10-16 (Songwriting); theory camp for older students
providers.locations: Middle C Music, 4530 Wisconsin Ave NW (Tenleytown), Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Ukulele & Piano: $250 first week incl. ukulele ($200/week with own instrument); Music Theory $130/class; Rock Band/Songwriting weekly rates not published - call 202-244-7326
providers.hours: Rock Band & Songwriting 10am-2pm Mon-Fri with 6pm Friday concert; Ukulele & Piano 10-11am or 11am-12pm; Theory 12:30-2pm Mon-Thu
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions roughly June 24-August 28
providers.status_2026: Weekly sessions run through late August with rolling enrollment - real late availability for 2026; confirm specific weeks by phone.

reg_2027.opens: Spring; rolling
reg_2027.notes: Low-drama rolling enrollment by the week - a reliable late-planner option. Small store-front scale means individual weeks can still cap out; call ahead for July weeks.

providers.fit_notes: Great for NW DC families wanting a walkable, low-key music camp - true beginners welcome, and the tiny-tots ukulele camp is a rare under-6 music option. Part-day hours won't cover a full workday.

providers.name: Mikva Challenge DC Youth Councils
providers.description: Civic-leadership councils (Safety & Justice Youth Council, Citywide Student Voice Council) where DC high schoolers research real city issues, advocate directly to decision-makers, and help implement policy recommendations — with a stipend, 50+ community-service hours, and out-of-city trips. Councils run roughly eight months, so they bridge summer and the school year rather than functioning as a standalone summer camp. Thrives: opinionated, civically curious teens who want their voice to reach the Wilson Building.
providers.ages: DC high school students (~14-18)
providers.price_detail: Free; participants receive a stipend
providers.locations: Meetings in central DC; open to students citywide
providers.sessions_2026: Councils are ~8-month commitments spanning parts of summer and the school year
providers.status_2026: Rolling/periodic recruitment — application windows announced on their site; worth emailing now for the next cohort.

reg_2027.opens: cohort applications typically posted spring/summer for the coming cycle (verify — timing not firmly published)
reg_2027.notes: General program application on the national Mikva site routes to DC councils. Because this is a school-year-spanning civic fellowship rather than a June-August camp, treat it as a teen's ongoing 'anchor activity' rather than summer childcare.

providers.fit_notes: Best-in-class for policy/government kids, but it does NOT solve the summer-coverage problem — it is a part-time leadership commitment. DC students only.

providers.name: Montgomery College Youth Summer Programs
providers.description: Over 300 weekly camps for grades 1-12 on the Rockville, Germantown, and Takoma Park/Silver Spring campuses: coding, engineering/robotics (Lego, Mindstorms, VEX, circuits), science, math academies, study skills, plus arts and even circus. The area's best-value academic/STEM camp catalog, with scholarships for MCPS free/reduced-lunch families.
providers.ages: 6-17 (grades 1-12; camps grouped by grade band)
providers.locations: Montgomery College campuses: Rockville, Germantown, and Takoma Park/Silver Spring
providers.hours: Varies; many half-day sessions that can be combined into full days
providers.price_detail: Generally the most affordable structured STEM instruction in the county; exact per-camp fees in the catalog PDF — many camps historically under $250/week
providers.extended_care: Varies by campus; supervised lunch between half-day sessions at some sites
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships for students qualifying for Free or Reduced Meals in MCPS
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 registration open; many camps run through August so mid-summer signups are realistic for remaining sections

reg_2027.opens: typically February-March (catalog posts late winter)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online via the Augusoft portal; popular coding/robotics sections at Rockville fill by late spring but the sheer catalog size means alternatives usually exist. CampDoc forms required after registration.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps June-August across three campuses
providers.fit_notes: Best budget pick in this sector and one of few with real teen offerings below university-program prices. Instruction quality varies by individual instructor, as with any large catalog.

providers.name: Montgomery County 4-H Residential Camp
providers.org: University of Maryland Extension, Montgomery County 4-H
providers.description: The county 4-H program's classic five-day residential camp — cabin life, outdoor skills, crafts, campfires, and 4-H's teen-counselor leadership culture, run by UMD Extension at nonprofit prices. A small, old-fashioned, community-run alternative to commercial sleepaway camps; teen counselors and CITs grow up through the program.
providers.ages: 8-13 (as of Jan 1), with teen counselor/CIT roles for older youth
providers.locations: Montgomery County 4-H camp (residential site details via UMD Extension Montgomery County office)
providers.price_detail: 2026 fee not search-indexed; county 4-H residential camps are typically modest (a few hundred dollars all-in). Scholarships available for qualifying families.
providers.hours: Residential, 5 days

reg_2027.opens: spring (forms posted via UMD Extension; contact office late winter)
reg_2027.mechanism: application — download, complete, and upload PDF forms; not an online cart
reg_2027.notes: Paper-ish process rewards early contact with the Extension office. 4-H membership/club participation not required to attend but club families hear about it first.

providers.sessions_2026: July 12-17, 2026 (one 5-day session)
providers.status_2026: 2026 session begins today (July 12) — closed for this year
providers.fit_notes: For families wanting an inexpensive, small-scale, community-run sleepaway week rather than a polished commercial camp. Details thin online — email the Extension office. Camp site location not verifiable from indexed pages.

providers.name: Montgomery County Therapeutic Recreation Camps (TR Summer Fun Zones + Mainstreaming)
providers.description: The county's public camps for youth with disabilities: TR Summer Fun Zones 'After ESY' are afternoon camps for ages 6-21 that pick up after the morning MCPS Extended School Year session, grouped Youth (6-10), Mid (11-14), and Teen (15-21) with a ~1:4 staff ratio, plus specialty TR camps. The same office also arranges trained inclusion companions so a child with a disability can attend any regular Montgomery County Rec camp. Public pricing makes this one of the most affordable supported options in the Maryland suburbs.
providers.locations: Multiple county rec centers across Montgomery County (sites vary by year)
providers.price_detail: County camp rates (typically well under private-camp rates); exact 2026 TR fees not captured in indexed copies — see ActiveMONTGOMERY
providers.financial_aid: County fee-assistance (RecAssist-type) programs exist for income-qualified residents — not verified this cycle; ask customer service (240-777-6840)
providers.hours: TR Summer Fun Zones run afternoons (after morning ESY school); hours for other TR/specialty camps vary by site
providers.extended_care: Extended care offered at many MoCo Rec camps — verify per camp; TR Fun Zones are afternoon-only
providers.sessions_2026: Afternoon sessions during/after MCPS ESY, June-August 2026 (no camp July 3)
providers.status_2026: Season underway; TR camps are capacity-limited and generally fill in winter — call to ask about openings or companion support for late-summer regular camps

reg_2027.opens: mid-to-late January (2026 registration opened Tue Jan 20 at 6:30am for county residents)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come (online at ActiveMONTGOMERY, opens at 6:30am sharp; virtual queue if site hits capacity)
reg_2027.notes: TR camp slots are among the fastest-filling in the county system — set up your ActiveMONTGOMERY account in advance and be online at 6:30am on opening day. For mainstreaming into a regular camp, contact the TR office in advance so a trained companion can be arranged. Early-bird discount on select camps for registering by early April (2026: Apr 3). Verified via county press release and rec pages (search-indexed).

providers.fit_notes: Great for MoCo families wanting affordable, structured, small-ratio support for a child with a disability, or inclusion support in any county camp. Note the TR Summer Fun Zones are afternoon programs designed to follow morning ESY school — on their own they do not cover a full work day. Registration-day speed matters; families needing 1:1 clinical support should discuss fit with TR staff first.

providers.name: Montgomery Parks SSL & Teen Volunteer Program (nature centers, camps, park projects)
providers.description: Montgomery Parks is an approved MCPS SSL host: teens 14+ can earn Student Service Learning hours all summer as nature-center animal caretakers, naturalist aides and camp junior counselors (CITs), or at one-off stream cleanups and trail workdays. It is the standard free answer for a MoCo teen who needs SSL hours and likes being outside; under-14s can join family volunteer events with a parent.
providers.ages: 14+ independently (some roles older); under 14 with a registered parent via Family Volunteer program
providers.locations: All five nature centers (Brookside, Meadowside, Locust Grove, Black Hill, Maydale), parks and camp sites countywide
providers.hours: Varies — weekly camp-counselor commitments to one-day workdays
providers.price_detail: Free; teens earn SSL hours (only MCPS/Volunteer-Center preapproved opportunities count)

reg_2027.opens: rolling year-round; summer camp junior-counselor slots are posted in spring
reg_2027.notes: Sign up at ParksVolunteers.org. Camp CIT/junior-counselor roles at the nature centers are the plum summer assignments and are claimed by late spring — apply in March-April. One-day SSL workdays post all summer and are the easy last-minute option.

providers.status_2026: Active all summer; one-day SSL events still posting as of July 2026 (verified via volunteer portal listings in search index).
providers.fit_notes: Best structured free SSL pipeline in the county for outdoorsy teens; camp-counselor roles double as real work experience. Requires self-directed signup and reliability.

providers.name: Montgomery TennisPlex Camps (Tennis + Summer Adventure & Splash)
providers.description: The indoor/outdoor tennis hub next to the Maryland SoccerPlex in Boyds (upper Montgomery County, nearest Gaithersburg/Germantown) runs full- and half-day tennis camps for all levels, a multi-activity Adventure & Splash camp (tennis/pickleball, splash park trips, mini golf, crafts) for ages 4-14, and a unique 'Double-Plex' soccer-morning/tennis-afternoon combo with the SoccerPlex. Long camp day with extended care makes it one of the more practical upcounty options.
providers.locations: 18010 Central Park Circle, Boyds MD (adjacent to Maryland SoccerPlex; upcounty, ~15 min from Gaithersburg)
providers.price_detail: Full day 8:30-4 with half-day options; weekly rates around the mid-hundreds depending on camp (verify online)
providers.hours: Full day 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM; half-day AM 8:30-12:30 or PM 12:30-4
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open; mid/late-summer weeks generally available in July

reg_2027.opens: Winter (camp pages typically live by January-February)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come online or by phone; capacity is decent and it rarely sells out league-wide, though splash-camp weeks are the most popular.

providers.fit_notes: Good for upcounty families and kids who want variety rather than six hours of pure tennis; too far a drive for most DC-proper families.

providers.name: MOST-DC Portal note — see Learn24 entry
providers.description: REMOVE
providers.ages: n/a

reg_2027.opens: n/a
reg_2027.notes: n/a

providers.status_2026: n/a

providers.name: Movement Rockville Climbing Camps
providers.description: Indoor rock-climbing day camps at the big Movement gym on Rockville Pike: skill-building climbing sessions tailored by level, games, group challenges, and all required gear included, for ages 6-12 (some teen weeks). No experience needed — the camps are explicitly built for first-time climbers as much as gym rats, and climbing's individual-progress nature suits kids who don't love team sports.
providers.ages: 6-12 core; some regions offer 13-17
providers.locations: Movement Rockville, 725 Rockville Pike, Rockville MD
providers.price_detail: Roughly $90-100/day equivalent for full weeks; member discounts (verify current rates)
providers.hours: Weekday day-camp hours; check half/full-day formats
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions all summer 2026, plus school-break camps year-round
providers.status_2026: Registration open; later-summer weeks generally available mid-July

reg_2027.opens: Winter (summer camp pages live by January-February)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come online; steady demand but multiple weeks and a large gym mean late booking is usually possible. Members get pricing perks.

providers.fit_notes: Terrific for kids who like individual challenge, climbing-obsessed kids, and team-sport refuseniks; kids wanting outdoor rock should look at Sportrock's outdoor adventure camps.

providers.name: MPD Junior Police Academy (summer)
providers.description: A free six-week summer program where DC teens 14-17 get an insider's view of law enforcement and criminal justice: daily roll call at Police Headquarters, weekly trips to the Police Academy, sit-downs with homicide detectives and commanders, courthouse visits with judges, and tours of Harbor Patrol, K-9, and the 911 center. Often run in connection with the Marion Barry Summer Youth Employment Program, meaning participants can be paid.
providers.price_detail: Free; when run as an MBSYEP placement, teens earn a summer wage
providers.locations: MPD Headquarters, 300 Indiana Ave NW, plus field visits across the city
providers.hours: Weekday mornings or afternoons, six weeks

reg_2027.opens: Winter — MBSYEP applications open late January/February for DC residents 14-24; direct MPD inquiries year-round
reg_2027.notes: Two routes: apply to MBSYEP (summerjobs.dc.gov) and request the MPD placement, or contact the MPD youth programs office directly (Lt. contact at 202-576-6768). MBSYEP has firm early-year deadlines — mark late January 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Six-week program aligned with MBSYEP, roughly late June-early August 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 cohort selected via winter MBSYEP cycle; closed to new entrants mid-summer
providers.fit_notes: For DC-resident teens curious about law, justice, or policing — and a way to turn summer into a paid resume line. Requires the January application discipline; not a casual sign-up.

providers.name: MSI Summer Soccer Day Camps
providers.description: Montgomery County's giant community-run soccer nonprofit (the county's default rec league) runs full-day and half-day summer soccer camps at Julius West Middle School in Rockville for grades K-8, plus a High School Prep training program for teens targeting fall tryouts. Coaching is solid and unpretentious, and it's one of the more affordable soccer-camp routes in the county. Joining MSI camps is also a good on-ramp into the fall MSI rec league every newcomer family should know about.
providers.ages: Grades K-8 (camps); high schoolers for HS Prep program
providers.locations: Julius West Middle School, 651 Great Falls Rd, Rockville MD
providers.price_detail: Full-day (9-3) and half-day (9-12) weekly rates; roughly $250-400/week depending on option (verify at LeagueApps)
providers.hours: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM full day; 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM half day
providers.financial_aid: MSI has a financial-assistance tradition for its rec programs; ask about camp scholarships
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open through summer; mid/late-summer weeks generally available in July

reg_2027.opens: Early spring (camp registration typically live by March)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via LeagueApps; rarely a panic-registration situation. The HS Prep summer program is the one that draws motivated teens ahead of August tryouts.

providers.fit_notes: Best-value mainstream soccer camp in mid-county; families wanting elite club-level training should look at Bethesda SC or D.C. United RDS instead.

providers.name: Music on the Hill Summer Camps
providers.description: Capitol Hill's neighborhood music school runs small, personal week-long camps taught by working DC musicians: Jr. Rockstars for younger kids, Classical Kids Camp (group piano/violin, composer games), and Rock Band Camp where campers form a real band, name it, and play a Friday gig. Beloved hyper-local option - think community music school warmth, not a big franchise.
providers.ages: 6-15 depending on camp
providers.locations: Music on the Hill studio, Capitol Hill (SE DC)
providers.price_detail: Weekly tuition not published in indexed pages; non-refundable $100 deposit reserves a spot. Contact school for current rates.
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm, Monday-Friday
providers.sessions_2026: Jr. Rockstars June 22-26; Classical Kids July 13-17; Rock Band Camp July 27-31; teen 'Rock the Hill' sessions incl. Aug 10-14 (ages 10+)
providers.status_2026: Remaining late-July/August camps showed 'limited space' as of snapshot; earlier weeks done. Small rosters, so check directly.

reg_2027.opens: Mid-January (2026 registration opened Jan 15 at noon)
reg_2027.notes: First-come with $100 non-refundable deposit; camps are small and the popular weeks fill quickly after the January opening - set a calendar reminder for the noon opening.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for Capitol Hill families wanting a walkable, small-group music camp with real instruction. Tiny capacity - not a fallback option if you plan late; limited info published online, so call.

providers.name: My Gym Potomac Camp
providers.description: Drop-off camp at a toddler-gym franchise: fitness games, non-competitive gymnastics, crafts, and music in an indoor gym, with pay-by-the-day flexibility. My Gym serves kids from infancy (parent-and-me classes run alongside camp all summer), making it one of the few places a family with a 2-year-old and a 6-year-old can use the same building.
providers.ages: Camp roughly 3-9; classes (incl. parent-and-me) from 6 weeks
providers.locations: My Gym Potomac, Potomac MD
providers.price_detail: Pay-by-the-day options; multi-day discounts — call for current rates
providers.sessions_2026: Camp days offered throughout summer alongside the class schedule
providers.status_2026: Open — drop-in/day-booking model, space generally available including late July and August

reg_2027.opens: rolling year-round
reg_2027.notes: No registration-day scramble; also the region's main parent-and-me summer option for under-3s alongside class enrollment. Verified from official franchise page.

providers.fit_notes: Gap-filler and toddler starter camp; energetic little kids love it, older elementary kids will outgrow it fast.

providers.name: National Building Museum Summer Camp (Brick City / Invention Lab)
providers.description: Design-and-build day camps inside the Building Museum's spectacular Great Hall: 'Brick City: World Architecture Brick by Brick' (a LEGO crash course where campers build boats and zip lines, design dream playgrounds, and crack a LEGO brick code) and 'Invention Lab: Build, Create, Innovate!' (a hands-on introduction to engineering through Rube Goldberg machines, simple machines, and physics). Perfect for kids who never stop building; families often pair the two camps into a two-week run.
providers.ages: Rising 2nd-5th graders (roughly 7-11)
providers.locations: National Building Museum, 401 F St NW (Judiciary Square)
providers.price_detail: $500/week non-members, $400/week Family-level members and higher; aftercare $100/week
providers.hours: Full-day camp; aftercare available
providers.sessions_2026: One-week sessions throughout June, July, and August 2026
providers.financial_aid: None found on indexed pages; membership discount ($100/week) is the main saving

reg_2027.opens: Members early January, general public late January (2025 pattern: members Jan 8, public Jan 22)
reg_2027.notes: Family-level membership buys ~2 weeks of early access plus $100/week off - it roughly pays for itself in one week of camp. The museum also hosts a regional Summer Camp Fair in mid-February, useful for newcomer families comparing camps.

providers.status_2026: Registration opened in January and season is running June-August; check the ticketing portal for remaining late-July/August weeks (current per-week availability could not be verified - portal blocks automated checks).
providers.fit_notes: Builder kids thrive; note the narrow grade band (rising 2nd-5th only). Official site bot-blocked; verified via search-indexed copies of nbm.org and its ticketing portal.

providers.name: National Gallery of Art Teen Programs (High School Summer Institute & Teen Leadership Collective)
providers.description: The National Gallery has no drop-in summer camp, but it runs two serious free teen programs: a competitive two-week June High School Summer Institute (behind-the-scenes art-career exploration and a student-curated exhibition, open to DC/MD/VA grades 9-12) and the paid Teen Leadership Collective (up to $1,000 stipend, six DC teens ages 15-19, fall Saturdays). There is also a high-school volunteer track with a March 7 deadline for summer placements. These are resume-level museum experiences, not childcare.
providers.ages: Grades 9-12 for Summer Institute (DC/MD/VA students); 15-19 and DC-resident for Teen Leadership Collective
providers.locations: National Gallery of Art, Constitution Ave NW between 3rd and 9th Streets
providers.price_detail: All teen programs free; Teen Leadership Collective pays participants up to $1,000; lunch provided at TLC sessions
providers.financial_aid: n/a — free, and the flagship teen program pays a stipend
providers.sessions_2026: Summer Institute: two weeks in June (exact 2026 dates not confirmed); TLC: Saturdays Sept 26-Dec 12, 2026
providers.status_2026: Summer Institute application closed for 2026. OPEN NOW for the next cycle: Teen Leadership Collective applications run July 27-Aug 28, 2026 for the fall 2026 cohort. Teen Studio Series returns spring 2027.

reg_2027.opens: Summer Institute apps historically winter/early spring; TLC apps midsummer (July 27-Aug 28 in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Competitive selection, small cohorts (TLC takes only ~6 teens). High school volunteer applications due Oct 28 (school year) and March 7 (summer). dc-resident-only applies to TLC; Summer Institute accepts the whole DMV.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for art-inclined teens building college portfolios/resumes; useless for younger kids or families needing full-day coverage. VERIFICATION FLAG: nga.gov blocked direct fetch; details from search-indexed copies of official pages — confirm Summer Institute is still offered before the 2027 cycle.

providers.name: National Geographic Photo Camp (not open-enrollment in DC)
providers.description: Nat Geo's Photo Camp is a genuinely free, world-class visual-storytelling program where teens work directly with National Geographic photographers — lodging, meals, and cameras all provided. But despite the Society being headquartered in DC, it is not a bookable DC summer camp: camps are staged in partnership with community organizations in changing locations (the announced 2026 series of eight camps is in Washington State, for youth 16-22), and participants are typically recruited through partner nonprofits rather than open applications. Treat it as a watch-list item, not a plan.
providers.ages: Grades 9-12 (some series 16-22)
providers.locations: Varies by year and partner; no DC-area 2026 camp announced
providers.price_detail: Entirely free including lodging, meals, and camera equipment when selected
providers.sessions_2026: 2026 series runs in Washington State (five camps remaining through 2026); none in the DC region
providers.status_2026: Not available locally in 2026.

reg_2027.opens: No fixed cycle — camps announced rolling via natgeo.org/photocamp and @ngphotocamp on Instagram
reg_2027.notes: Recruitment usually flows through partner youth organizations; if your teen is involved with a DC youth nonprofit, ask staff to watch for partnership calls. Monitor rather than plan around it.

providers.fit_notes: Included as a 'manage expectations' entry — famous name, free, but not something a DC family can simply register for. Verified via official Society program page and press release (search-indexed).

providers.name: NCAC Cub Scout District Day & Twilight Camps
providers.org: Scouting America, National Capital Area Council
providers.description: Week-long outdoor day camps (and evening 'twilight' versions for working parents) run by each NCAC district across DC, Montgomery County, and NoVA parks: fishing, archery, BB guns, slingshots, STEM stations, scout skills, nature and crafts. For registered Cub Scouts entering grades 1-5 — joining a local pack is the entry ticket, and camps are volunteer-run and inexpensive.
providers.ages: entering grades 1-5 (must be a registered Cub Scout)
providers.locations: District-run sites region-wide, rotating yearly (2026 examples: Leesylvania State Park, Gunston Hall, Algonkian Regional Park; Montgomery County district camps listed on the 082 portal)
providers.price_detail: Exact NCAC 2026 fees not indexed; district Cub day camps regionally run well under $250/wk. Camperships (aid) applications were due Feb 1, 2026.
providers.hours: Day camps roughly 9am-4pm; twilight camps late afternoon-evening

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration pages live by Jan-Feb; campership deadline ~Feb 1)
reg_2027.notes: You must join a Cub Scout pack first (rolling, ~$85/yr national + pack dues). Parent volunteering is often requested. Pick your district's week early — each site runs once.

providers.sessions_2026: One-week district sessions June-July 2026 (e.g., June 15-19 Prince William, June 22-26 Accotink Bay, June 29-Jul 3 Difficult Run; Montgomery/DC district weeks on portal)
providers.status_2026: Most June sessions complete; some July district weeks may still accept registrations via the 082 portal
providers.fit_notes: Cheapest real-outdoors week for younger kids IF you are willing to join scouting (co-ed packs exist). Not a drop-off childcare substitute — volunteer-run, no extended care.

providers.name: NEWCOMER EXPLAINER: Montgomery County Swim League & Neighborhood Pool Culture
providers.org: Montgomery County Swim League (MCSL) / community pool associations / Montgomery County Recreation
providers.description: The defining summer institution of close-in Montgomery County isn't a camp — it's the neighborhood pool. About 90 private community pools (member-owned associations like the Bethesda Barracudas' pool) each field a summer swim team in the MCSL, a volunteer-run league of Saturday-morning A-meets, pep rallies, and decades-old rivalries for ages 5-18. Families join a pool (bond/membership fees, sometimes multi-year waitlists at popular Bethesda/Chevy Chase pools), and the swim team comes with it — practices every summer morning, meets, and a built-in social world. Montgomery County Recreation also runs its own lower-cost summer swim teams at public outdoor pools for families without a private-pool membership.
providers.ages: 5-18 (swimmers must pass basic ability checks: one 25m length with rhythmic breathing for 8&U, up to 50m free + 50m back for 13+)
providers.locations: ~90 community pools across Montgomery County; MoCo Rec teams at public outdoor pools
providers.price_detail: Private pools: one-time bond ($400-2,000+) plus annual dues ($500-1,000+), swim team fee ~$100-200 on top; MoCo Rec public-pool teams are a low-cost alternative
providers.hours: Morning practices weekdays; Saturday A-meets; season runs Memorial Day to late July
providers.extended_care: Not applicable — this is a team/community, not childcare
providers.financial_aid: Many pools quietly offer payment plans/hardship rates; MoCo Rec teams and public pools are the affordable route
providers.sessions_2026: Season ran Memorial Day through late July 2026; divisional/relay championships cap the season
providers.status_2026: Season winding down (championships late July); too late to join a 2026 team, but pools sell late-summer memberships and it's prime time to visit pools as a guest and pick one

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring for pool memberships (waitlist pools: apply ASAP, some take 1-3 years); MoCo Rec team evaluations are Tue/Fri at 5 PM the week of Memorial Day, parent meeting the Tuesday after
reg_2027.notes: Two paths: (1) join a private community pool — each has its own membership process and waitlist, so apply to nearby pools in fall/winter and ask about swim-team culture when you tour; (2) register for a Montgomery County Recreation summer team via ActiveMONTGOMERY (new swimmers attend Memorial Day-week evaluations). Insider tip: pools with long waitlists often have shorter ones a mile away — apply to 2-3.

providers.fit_notes: This is how newcomer kids make their first local friends — and how parents do too (expect to be drafted as a timer). Kids must already swim a pool length; for non-swimmers start with MoCo Rec or YMCA lessons first, then join a team the next summer.

providers.name: NEWCOMER EXPLAINER: Teen Lifeguard Certification-to-Paid-Job Pipeline (DPR, MoCo Rec, YMCA)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks & Recreation / Montgomery County Recreation / YMCA
providers.description: For DMV teens, lifeguarding is the classic first summer job, and both jurisdictions actively subsidize the pipeline. DC DPR trains anyone free of charge who intends to guard at DPR pools (Red Cross certification, normally $200-400 elsewhere), runs a free Junior Lifeguard Academy for DC residents 13-17 as a feeder, and hires teens as lifeguards, pool operators, and customer-service staff each summer. Montgomery County Recreation offers lifeguard training, CPR/AED, and pool-operator classes and hires for its aquatics centers, while the YMCA runs Red Cross lifeguard courses (ages 15+, ~30 hours) and hires at its branches — private summer pools around the county also compete hard for certified teens, often at $15-18+/hour.
providers.ages: Junior Lifeguard Academy: 13-17 (DC residents); lifeguard certification: 15+ (must pass swim prerequisites); DPR summer jobs typically 16+
providers.locations: DPR aquatic centers across DC; MoCo Rec aquatic centers (Silver Spring, Rockville, Germantown); YMCA branches (Bethesda-Chevy Chase, Silver Spring)
providers.price_detail: DPR training free for those who commit to guard for DPR; Junior Lifeguard Academy free for DC residents; MoCo Rec and YMCA courses charge course fees (roughly $200-400) unless tied to employment
providers.hours: Courses ~30 hours over several days/weekends; winter-spring cohorts feed summer hiring
providers.financial_aid: The DPR route IS the financial aid: free training plus a paid job; DC's Marion Barry Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP) can also place teens in aquatics roles
providers.sessions_2026: Training cohorts ran winter-spring 2026; summer 2026 guards already hired and working; YMCA/MoCo Rec run additional certification courses through the year
providers.status_2026: Too late for a summer 2026 job, but teens can take a late-summer/fall certification course to be ready for 2027 hiring — certifications last 2 years

reg_2027.opens: December-March: DPR posts free lifeguard training and summer-employment applications in winter; Junior Lifeguard Academy enrolls in spring/summer; MoCo Rec spring course catalog opens via ActiveMONTGOMERY; YMCA courses run rolling year-round
reg_2027.notes: The play for a 14-15 year old: do DPR's free Junior Lifeguard Academy (DC residents) or a YMCA course at 15, get certified by spring, then apply early — DPR summer hiring and private-pool management companies (which staff most MoCo neighborhood pools) start recruiting in February-March and certified 16-year-olds are in high demand. Swim prerequisites apply (continuous swim, brick retrieval, treading water).

providers.fit_notes: The best-paying, most reliably available teen summer track in the region, and a genuine leadership credential; requires solid swimming ability and teens must handle a real-responsibility job. DC residency unlocks the free/junior versions; MoCo teens usually pay for a course but recoup it in the first two weeks of pay.

providers.name: NIH HiSTEP (High School Scientific Training and Enrichment Program)
providers.org: National Institutes of Health, Office of Intramural Training & Education
providers.description: A free, PAID five-week summer program on the NIH Bethesda campus that introduces rising seniors to biomedical research and healthcare careers through lectures, lab visits, group projects, and college-application coaching — participants earn roughly a $2,300/month stipend. It is an equity program: applicants must attend a school where at least 30% of students qualify for free/reduced lunch and live within 40 miles of NIH. For eligible students this is arguably the single best free summer opportunity in Montgomery County.
providers.ages: Rising 12th graders (juniors at application time), age 17+ by mid-June
providers.locations: NIH main campus, 9000 Rockville Pike, Bethesda (Medical Center Metro)
providers.price_detail: Free to attend, plus a stipend of approximately $2,300/month
providers.sessions_2026: Approx. 5 weeks, July-early August 2026 (cohort selected from Feb 2026 applications)
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 applications closed Feb 1, 2026; program underway this summer. Note NIH has been restructuring its high-school pipeline (the general HS-SIP merged into the main Summer Internship Program), so confirm HiSTEP's exact 2027 form on training.nih.gov.

reg_2027.opens: Application portal opens mid-November 2026; deadline Feb 1, 2027
reg_2027.notes: Essays, transcript, recommendations; no research experience expected — the program targets students without lab access. Eligibility gates (30% FARMS school, 40-mile radius, citizenship/permanent residency) are strict, so verify your school qualifies before investing effort.

providers.fit_notes: Transformative for science-curious students from Title-I-type schools in MoCo, PG County, and DC; students at low-FARMS schools (e.g., most Bethesda/Potomac schools) are NOT eligible — they should look at the college-level NIH SIP later. Verified via search-indexed NIH OITE pages; recent federal program churn makes 2027 details worth re-confirming in the fall.

providers.name: Northern Virginia 4-H Educational Center Summer Camps
providers.org: Virginia Cooperative Extension / Northern Virginia 4-H Center
providers.description: The big Blue Ridge 4-H center in Front Royal, VA (~75 min from DC) where NoVA counties each run week-long residential camps: canoeing, riflery, archery, pool, crafts, and famous evening campfires, staffed heavily by trained teen counselors. Open to all kids in participating counties (not just 4-H members) at remarkable prices — meals, lodging, and t-shirt included for roughly $400.
providers.ages: roughly 9-13 for junior camps; CIT/counselor tracks for teens (varies by county)
providers.locations: Northern Virginia 4-H Educational Center, 600 4-H Center Dr, Front Royal, VA 22630; camps organized per county (Fairfax, Arlington, Loudoun/Fauquier, Prince William-Stafford, etc.)
providers.price_detail: 2026: ~$415 (Loudoun/Fauquier week), $395 (Prince William-Stafford) for 5 days/4 nights incl. meals, lodging, t-shirt, class supplies, pool snack money
providers.hours: Residential, 5 days/4 nights
providers.financial_aid: County scholarship funds typically available via Extension offices

reg_2027.opens: early March (2026 pattern: 4-H club members/counselor siblings Mar 6, general public Mar 13, 5:00pm)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come per county via Extension office registration
reg_2027.notes: You register through YOUR county's week, and DC residents should check which county weeks accept out-of-county campers. Public registration slots go quickly the opening week — calendar the March date.

providers.sessions_2026: County-specific weeks June-August 2026 at the Front Royal center
providers.status_2026: Most county weeks completed or full by mid-July; check individual county Extension pages for any late openings
providers.fit_notes: Arguably the best-value sleepaway week reachable from DC; the catch is county-based registration that newcomers in DC/MD must navigate (some weeks are county-resident-first). Rustic and screen-free.

providers.name: NSO Summer Music Institute
providers.org: National Symphony Orchestra / The Kennedy Center
providers.description: A tuition-free, three-week residential fellowship at the Kennedy Center for serious young orchestral musicians (strings, winds, brass, harp) considering music as a career. Fellows get private lessons, orchestra rehearsals, career seminars, and perform free public concerts on the Millennium Stage. This is one of the most prestigious free music opportunities in the country and draws applicants nationally, not just from DC.
providers.ages: 15-20 (must be 15 by program start; high school and college students)
providers.locations: Kennedy Center, 2700 F St NW (Foggy Bottom); supervised hotel housing within a 15-minute walk
providers.price_detail: Tuition-free scholarship covers instruction, activities, housing, and local transportation; $30 application fee (waiver available); families cover travel to DC and food
providers.hours: Full-time residential, June 7-29, 2026
providers.financial_aid: Program is itself the aid: every accepted student attends free; application-fee waivers granted on request (NSOSMI@kennedy-center.org)
providers.sessions_2026: One session: June 7-29, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season over/closed — 2026 applications closed Jan 30, 2026; the 2026 cohort completed the program in June (free SMI concerts ran mid-June at Millennium Stage). Not bookable for July 2026.

reg_2027.opens: Application portal typically opens in fall (Oct-Nov); watch kennedy-center.org/nsosmi
reg_2027.notes: Recorded video audition plus written application and recommendation; deadline has been late January (Jan 30 for 2026). Highly selective national pool — start preparing audition repertoire in the fall of junior year. $30 fee, waivable.

providers.fit_notes: For committed pre-conservatory instrumentalists only — this is career training, not an enrichment camp. Kennedy Center pages could not be fetched directly (bot-blocked); details verified via search-indexed copies of official pages.

providers.name: Olney Theatre Center Summer Camps
providers.description: One of Maryland's two state theatres runs a full ladder of summer programs in its new Trawick Education Center: Discovery Stage (rising grades 1-3), Summer Stock (grades 4-6, July 7-18), Musical Theatre Intensive (grades 7-9, July 21-Aug 1), plus Tech & Design Intensive and a Summer Audition Studio for grades 10-12. Camps are deliberately tiny (12-20 kids per session), so kids get real stage time from professional teaching artists rather than being lost in a cast of 60. It sits in Olney, upper Montgomery County — a drive from close-in suburbs, but families make it for the quality.
providers.ages: Rising grades 1-12 across five programs (approx. 6-18)
providers.locations: Trawick Education Center at Olney Theatre Center, 2001 Olney-Sandy Spring Rd, Olney, MD (upper Montgomery County, ~20-25 min from Rockville/Silver Spring — outside this guide's listed area vocabulary)
providers.price_detail: Summer Stock and Musical Theatre Intensive: $815 per two-week session (~$408/wk), 9:30am-2:30pm; payment plans available at checkout
providers.hours: 9:30am-2:30pm typical
providers.extended_care: none found on public pages — ask education@olneytheatre.org
providers.financial_aid: Payment plans at registration; families in financial need invited to email Director of Education Rebecca Dzida (rdzida@olneytheatre.org)
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions across July: Summer Stock July 7-18; Musical Theatre Intensive July 21-Aug 1, 2026; other programs per site
providers.status_2026: Likely full/limited — sessions cap at 12-20 participants and registration opened mid-January 2026; the July 21-Aug 1 Musical Theatre Intensive is the only session still ahead as of July 12 — email to check for openings.

reg_2027.opens: Mid-January 2027, online only
reg_2027.notes: No auditions for any program (even Audition Studio is instructional). With 12-20 spots per session, popular sessions fill within weeks — register in January, not spring.

providers.fit_notes: Excellent professional-theatre camp for beginners through college-bound teens; the location is the only catch for DC-proper families (no transportation offered). Verified via official olneytheatre.org pages (search-indexed).

providers.name: Oxon Cove Park & Oxon Hill Farm free kids programs
providers.description: A working historical farm run by the National Park Service just over the DC line, where kids can feed chickens, watch cow-milking and farm demonstrations, and earn a Junior Ranger badge (ages 7-13) — all completely free, no reservations needed for most drop-in programs. It fills the farm-animals gap for east-of-the-river and southern-DC families without the price tag of private farm camps.
providers.ages: All ages; Junior Ranger program for 7-13
providers.price_detail: No entry fee, no program fees
providers.locations: 6411 Oxon Hill Rd, Oxon Hill, MD (bordering SE DC, across from Alexandria)
providers.hours: Open daily; scheduled farm programs posted on the park calendar

reg_2027.opens: No registration for drop-in programs; school/group field trips book via the park education office
reg_2027.notes: Just show up — check the NPS calendar for chicken-feeding and farm-chore program times. This is not an enrolled camp; treat it as a free recurring summer activity to pair with other programs.

providers.sessions_2026: Daily free farm programs and Junior Ranger activities all summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Open now, no capacity constraints for family visits
providers.fit_notes: Not a camp — a free, unlimited-visit farm experience that newcomer families can use to fill uncovered summer weeks. Pairs well with Kenilworth for a zero-cost NPS summer east of the river.

providers.name: Pegasus Summer Camp at the German International School (with German immersion track)
providers.org: Pegasus Summer Camps, hosted by German International School Washington D.C.
providers.description: A full-service day camp on the leafy German International School campus in Potomac — sports, swimming, soccer, golf, and academics — that includes 'The Sharks,' a German-immersion track with lunch and activities conducted in German. Essentially the only German-language summer option close to DC, wrapped inside a mainstream camp so siblings without German can attend the same site.
providers.ages: Wide range ('children of all ages' — roughly preschool through middle school; confirm per program)
providers.locations: German International School Washington D.C., 8617 Chateau Dr, Potomac MD
providers.price_detail: $575 per week (2026)
providers.sessions_2026: June 29 - August 14, 2026, weekly enrollment
providers.status_2026: Season running; weekly enrollment typically bookable mid-summer for remaining July/August weeks — confirm at pegasussummercamps.com

reg_2027.opens: Winter (rolling online registration)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling online sign-up by the week; the German-immersion Sharks group has smaller capacity than the general camp, so book that track early. Licensed by the State of Maryland.

providers.fit_notes: The go-to for German-speaking families in the DMV; the general camp is a solid suburban day camp for anyone. Potomac location means driving — no Metro access.

providers.name: PhotoWorks Photography Camps at Glen Echo
providers.org: Glen Echo PhotoWorks (Glen Echo Park resident studio)
providers.description: The area's dedicated photography school, with a working wet darkroom and digital lab inside Glen Echo Park. Summer camps for tweens and teens cover film photography and darkroom printing, iPhone/smartphone photography, cyanotype, street photography (shooting in Glen Echo and Bethesda), and digital editing in Lightroom/Photoshop, ending with gallery-style presentation of work. Rare chance for a teen to learn analog film developing hands-on.
providers.ages: 10-18 (varies by camp; iPhone camp ~10-16; the teen film/darkroom camp 'The Darkroom Diaries: Introduction to Film Photography for Teens' is ages 14-17 — verify each camp's band in ProClass)
providers.locations: PhotoWorks studio, Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD
providers.price_detail: Not verified; priced per camp through the Glen Echo registration system (comparable Glen Echo weeks run ~$400-600)
providers.hours: Varies by camp; several run partial days
providers.extended_care: Via Glen Echo Park aftercare where offered
providers.sessions_2026: Multiple weeklong camps June-August 2026 (teen intro to film/darkroom, iPhone photography, cyanotype, street photography for teens)
providers.status_2026: Registration open since Feb 4 through Glen Echo's ProClass system; per-camp availability varies — check ProClass listings

reg_2027.opens: early February (with Glen Echo Park's Feb registration opening; catalog in January)
reg_2027.notes: Registers through Glen Echo Park's ProClass system, same clock as all park camps. Niche teen camps fill more slowly than the little-kid art camps but darkroom sections are small.

providers.fit_notes: Best for a tween/teen genuinely interested in photography as craft — small groups, real chemistry and equipment. Not a drop-off general day camp for young kids, and full-day childcare coverage is not the point.

providers.name: Planet Word family programs (no summer camp)
providers.description: DC's immersive museum of words and language (in the historic Franklin School downtown) does NOT run a summer camp as of 2026 - included here so newcomers stop searching. It does offer free admission, regular family programming, school-group 'Wordshops,' and free summer events like the Kids World festival in adjacent Franklin Park (July 28-30, 2026, ages 3-15).
providers.ages: All ages (family programs; Kids World targets 3-15)
providers.locations: Planet Word, 925 13th St NW (Franklin Square)
providers.price_detail: Museum admission is free (suggested donation); Kids World festival is 100% free
providers.hours: Museum hours; event-specific times for family programs
providers.sessions_2026: No camp. Kids World festival July 28-30, 2026 in Franklin Park; monthly Wordplay Wednesdays and other family events

reg_2027.opens: N/A - no camp exists; watch planetwordmuseum.org/events for family programming
reg_2027.notes: If Planet Word ever launches a camp it would likely appear on its events page; as of July 2026 none is offered, confirmed across multiple search angles.

providers.status_2026: No summer camp offered. Free family events running, including Kids World in Franklin Park July 28-30, 2026 - a genuinely bookable free option for late July.
providers.fit_notes: Listed as a 'negative result with alternatives': word-loving kids get free one-off programming here, but families needing weekly camp coverage should look elsewhere (e.g. Smithsonian writing-themed camp weeks).

providers.name: PLAY Summer Camp (Keegan PLAY-RAH-KA)
providers.description: Weekly themed theatre camps at the Keegan Theatre's intimate Dupont Circle playhouse, where elementary-age kids explore acting, design, movement, music, and visual arts under a new adventure theme each week (Storytime, Travels Through Time, Week at the Museum...). Join for one week or many — a flexible, imaginative option inside an actual working theatre.
providers.ages: Elementary (roughly 5-12; exact bands per week — verify with Keegan)
providers.locations: The Keegan Theatre, 1742 Church St NW (Dupont Circle), Washington DC; daily recess at nearby park
providers.price_detail: Weekly rate not verified on accessible pages (registration runs through Sawyer/hisawyer.com); automatic 10% discount for 2+ weeks
providers.hours: Full camp day with structured blocks (Actor's Tools, games, stories/songs, design, creative play)
providers.sessions_2026: Nine themed weeks, June 22-Aug 21 (e.g., June 22 Around the World; July 13 Travels Through Time; Aug 17 Artist Retreat)
providers.status_2026: Weekly enrollment through Aug 21; late-summer weeks typically still bookable mid-July — check the Sawyer registration page

reg_2027.opens: Winter (Sawyer listings typically post by late winter)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling weekly enrollment with multi-week discount; the most flexible schedule of DC's theatre camps — good backup when bigger camps are full.

providers.fit_notes: Best for younger kids who want playful, low-pressure creative weeks rather than a rehearsal grind; Dupont location is handy for downtown-working parents. Not a performance-intensive program.

providers.name: Play-Well TEKnologies LEGO Engineering Camps
providers.description: Engineering-concepts camps taught entirely through massive bins of LEGO — gear ratios, structures, machines — with themed weeks (Star Wars-inspired, Minecraft-inspired) hosted at rec centers and schools across Montgomery County and at DC camp hosts. A beloved, screen-free gateway STEM camp.
providers.ages: 5-14 per Play-Well (individual camps grouped in narrower bands, most commonly ~5-12 / grades K-5)
providers.locations: Rotating host sites across Montgomery County each summer (see schedule page); also appears inside DC host-camp programs (e.g. Sidwell Summer's LEGO Play-Well weeks, grades K-5, registered through the host). Often registered through MoCo Rec or host venues.
providers.hours: Half-day sessions common; full-day at some sites
providers.price_detail: Not verified; historically moderate ($ to $$ range) — confirm per site
providers.status_2026: 2026 Montgomery County camps listed on the schedule page; availability varies by site

reg_2027.opens: late winter/early spring (follows host-site registration calendars, incl. MoCo Rec openings)
reg_2027.notes: First-come; sites registered through Montgomery County Rec follow the county's spring registration opening and popular weeks go fast.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal first STEM camp for LEGO-obsessed kids. Kids over ~10 or those wanting real programming should step up to KID Museum or coding camps.

providers.name: Politics & Prose Summer Writing Classes for Kids & Teens
providers.description: DC's beloved independent bookstore runs a small slate of summer writing workshops for young people — the current lineup includes a poetry workshop for rising 3rd-6th graders, a mixed-media memoir workshop for rising 6th-9th graders, a fantasy-writing workshop for rising 8th-12th graders, plus Dungeons & Dragons and Comics Jam sessions for younger kids — in short morning blocks at the Connecticut Avenue flagship. Bookish kids get to write inside the city's most famous bookstore.
providers.ages: Roughly rising 3rd-12th grade depending on the class (poetry 3rd-6th; memoir 6th-9th; fantasy 8th-12th; D&D/comics for younger children)
providers.locations: 5015 Connecticut Ave NW, Washington DC (Chevy Chase DC flagship)
providers.price_detail: Priced per class; members get 10% off many classes. Current-year youth pricing not verifiable from indexed pages.
providers.status_2026: Summer classes are running (their 'Enjoy a Summer Class at P&P' page is live) and the youth lineup includes poetry, memoir, teen fantasy, D&D, and comics workshops — confirm exact dates and open seats at politics-prose.com/classes or (202) 364-1919

reg_2027.opens: Classes post seasonally, spring for summer
reg_2027.notes: Register online, in-store, or by phone (202-364-1919). Small sections; the youth offerings are a side dish to their adult classes, so check early and don't build summer plans around them.

providers.fit_notes: A nice supplement for a literary kid, not a camp — two-hour sessions, limited and changing lineup. Verify current offerings and prices directly with the store.

providers.name: Rochambeau French Immersion Summer Camp
providers.description: The region's premier French school runs a French-immersion day camp for ages 2.5-10 at its Bethesda campus, staffed by fully bilingual teachers — French acquisition through arts, cooking, science, music, and sports rather than lessons. Families can also mix in judo, Mousquetaires soccer, or HoopEd sports weeks on the same campus. The default choice for francophone families and serious learners.
providers.ages: 2.5-10 for French immersion camp (sports add-on camps skew older)
providers.locations: Rochambeau Maplewood campus, Bethesda MD (main school at 9600 Forest Rd, Bethesda)
providers.price_detail: Weekly pricing not verifiable from indexed pages (2025 guide PDF exists on their site); expect mid-hundreds per week — confirm via the 2026 camp guide
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 running now at Maplewood campus; popular weeks sell out — their CampBrain system uses a 20-minute cart timer on registration day, a sign of real demand

reg_2027.opens: Winter (registration day announced in advance; families told to create CampBrain accounts beforehand)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via CampBrain with a 20-minute cart timer on opening day — set up your account in advance and log in when it opens. French-speaking families snap up immersion weeks fastest.

providers.fit_notes: Strongest for kids with some French at home or in school; contact them about placement for true beginners. Toddler-friendly start age (2.5) is rare among immersion camps.

providers.name: Rock Creek Conservation Corps (RC3)
providers.description: A six-week paid summer conservation job ($2,000 stipend) for high schoolers, doing hands-on restoration in Rock Creek Park — invasive plant removal, litter cleanups, stormwater and forest-health work — alongside peers and mentors. Recruitment centers on partner high schools in DC (Roosevelt, Capital City PCS, E.L. Haynes, DC International) and Montgomery County (Einstein, Wheaton), targeting students underrepresented in conservation. Over 197 alumni since 2015.
providers.ages: High school students
providers.locations: Rock Creek Park sites in DC and Montgomery County; Conservancy office in Bethesda
providers.price_detail: No cost — pays a $2,000 stipend over 6 weeks, disbursed by attendance/hours
providers.hours: Summer work-week schedule for 6 weeks
providers.sessions_2026: One 6-week summer cohort
providers.status_2026: Applications for Summer 2026 are closed; the cohort is in the field now.

reg_2027.opens: Winter (application posts around January-February and closes by spring)
reg_2027.notes: Application-based, with recruiting focused through partner schools — students elsewhere should email the Conservancy early in the school year. Not first-come; motivation and fit matter.

providers.fit_notes: A resume-building paid job, not recreation — for teens ready for outdoor physical work in summer heat. Strongest access if you attend a partner school.

providers.name: Rock Creek Park Horse Center Summer Riding Camp
providers.description: Horseback riding camp inside Rock Creek Park itself — the only barn in the District — where kids ride park trails and learn horsemanship at the National Park Service's historic horse center on Glover Road. Sessions cover riding instruction plus horse care, and the center also runs therapeutic riding. Its in-city location and tiny capacity make it perennially oversubscribed.
providers.ages: 9-14 for riding camps
providers.locations: 5100 Glover Rd NW, Washington, DC 20015 (inside Rock Creek Park)
providers.price_detail: Not verifiable via indexed copies — pricing lives at metropolitanequestrian.org/rcphcpricing; horse camps regionally run $500-800/week
providers.hours: Day-camp hours; no verified extended care
providers.financial_aid: None found (therapeutic riding program exists separately)
providers.sessions_2026: Spring and summer riding camps (weekly)
providers.status_2026: Unverified — call 202-362-0117; capacity is very small and summer weeks typically fill by spring.

reg_2027.opens: Not verified; camp listings appear on their MainStreetSites portal in late winter/spring
reg_2027.notes: First-come through the online class portal; with one small barn serving the whole city, assume weeks vanish quickly. Taking lessons there during the year is the informal inside track.

providers.fit_notes: The only ride-in-the-park option in DC proper — magical for horse kids, but small, minimum age ~9, and pricing/dates require a phone call. Not for families needing aftercare.

providers.name: Rock Creek Park Junior Ranger Camp (NPS)
providers.org: National Park Service - Rock Creek Park Nature Center
providers.description: Short ranger-led camps at Rock Creek Park's Nature Center introducing kids to the park's nature and history: a nature hike, an astronomy show in DC's only NPS planetarium, creek exploration, a visit to historic Peirce Mill and crafts, taught by park rangers. When offered, it is free or near-free - the catch is tiny capacity and old-school phone registration.
providers.ages: roughly 6-12 (Junior Ranger badge program targets 6-8+; camp ages have varied by year)
providers.locations: Rock Creek Park Nature Center & Planetarium, 5200 Glover Rd NW, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: NPS Junior Ranger programming is free; historic camp listings showed no fee - confirm when registering
providers.hours: Historically short-format (e.g. two-day) daytime camps; hours vary by session
providers.sessions_2026: Could not verify 2026 camp dates - the NPS page was not directly reachable and indexed listings are dated; ranger-led kids programming runs all summer regardless
providers.status_2026: Unverified - call the Nature Center at (202) 895-6070 for this summer's camp/program schedule; drop-in Junior Ranger badge activities and planetarium shows are available anytime, free

reg_2027.opens: spring - announced by the Nature Center; registration is by phone/in person
reg_2027.notes: No online portal - call (202) 895-6070 in early spring to ask when summer camp registration opens. Capacity is very small and DC nature families know about it, so call the week it opens. Even without camp, the free Junior Ranger badge + planetarium make a great DIY 'camp day.'
