providers.name: The Washington Ballet Summer Programs (NW & SE campuses)
providers.description: DC's flagship professional ballet company runs summer programs at two campuses: the NW studios (Wisconsin Ave area) and the SE campus at THEARC in Ward 8, which is dramatically more affordable. Offerings run from creative-movement for ages 3-5 through fairy-tale ballet weeks (5-7), Young Dancer programs (8-12), and multi-week intensives for ages 10-18 including pointe, jazz, Horton, character, and African dance at SE. A newcomer with a ballet-curious kid can start here at any level; the SE campus is one of the best arts values in the city.
providers.ages: 3-18 (creative movement 3-5; fairy-tale/pre-ballet 5-7; Young Dancer 8-12; intensives 10-18)
providers.locations: NW campus near Wisconsin Ave NW; SE campus at THEARC, 1901 Mississippi Ave SE, Suite 202, Washington, DC 20020
providers.hours: Varies by program; SE 5-week Summer Intensive ran Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:00pm in 2026; SE ages 3-5 and 5-7 programs met Mon/Wed 4:00-7:00pm
providers.price_detail: 2026 examples: NW Young Dancer Intensive week $616; SE ages 3-5 program $240 and Pre-Ballet 5-7 $296 for four weeks; SE 5-week Summer Intensive (ages 10-18) $2,750. 5% discount for registering during the priority window (Jan 12-Feb 12 in 2026)
providers.financial_aid: Yes, but summer aid is an extension of school-year aid: TWSB awards up to $300,000/year via FACTS Grant & Aid, with school-year applications accepted May 1-June 30; families already receiving school-year aid apply for a Summer Programs Tuition Assistance Extension December-February, awarded as funding permits
providers.sessions_2026: Core window June 22-July 24, 2026 (SE ages 3-5 and Pre-Ballet 5-7 June 22-July 17; SE 5-week intensive June 22-July 24)
providers.status_2026: Programs underway and mostly ending mid-to-late July 2026; effectively closed for new 2026 enrollment

reg_2027.opens: mid-January (2026 registration opened January 11-12)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online for young-child, Young Dancer, and teen tracks; in-person audition required for NW intensives (ages 12+) and SE intensive Levels 2-6
reg_2027.notes: Register in the priority window (first month, through mid-February) for the 5% discount. SE campus (THEARC) programs are far cheaper than NW and serve ages 3-18 — a major value newcomers rarely discover. Aid seekers must plan a year out: apply for school-year aid May 1-June 30, then file the summer extension application December-February.

providers.name: The Writer's Center — Teen & Summer Writing Workshops
providers.description: Bethesda's 45-year-old literary institution runs hundreds of workshops a year, including teen creative-writing workshops and famously popular college-application-essay intensives each summer, in person on Walsh Street and online (live Zoom or asynchronous). This is a workshop, not a camp — a few hours with a professional writer, sitting alongside motivated peers.
providers.ages: Primarily 13-18 for youth offerings (rising 8th-12th; college-essay workshops for rising seniors); main catalog is adult
providers.locations: 4508 Walsh St, Bethesda, MD 20815 (downtown Bethesda) + online via Zoom/Wet Ink
providers.price_detail: Priced per workshop, not per week; typical multi-session workshops run in the low hundreds. 2026 teen-specific pricing not verifiable from indexed pages — confirm at writer.org.
providers.financial_aid: Member discounts; scholarship availability not verified
providers.status_2026: Summer catalog live at writer.org/workshops; teen and college-essay sections typically still bookable into August

reg_2027.opens: Rolling — summer workshops post in the spring catalog (~April)
reg_2027.notes: Online first-come registration; college-essay workshops are the hot ticket and fill first. Watch writer.org/workshops and filter by age/genre. No lottery.

providers.fit_notes: Best for serious teen writers and rising seniors needing college-essay help; too sparse a schedule to serve as childcare. Younger kids should look at Writopia or Day Eight instead.

providers.name: THEARC Campus Summer Programming
providers.description: THEARC — the Town Hall Education Arts Recreation Campus on Mississippi Ave SE in Ward 8 — houses a dozen-plus nonprofits under one roof, letting east-of-the-river kids access dance, music, fine arts, tutoring, theater tech internships, urban farms, recreation, and even medical/dental care at substantially reduced or no cost. In summer that means a menu of camps and programs from resident partners (Boys & Girls Club, Washington School for Girls, arts organizations, THEARC Farm) rather than one single camp.
providers.ages: Varies by partner program; collectively serves preK through teens
providers.price_detail: Substantially reduced or no cost by design; each resident partner sets its own fees
providers.locations: THEARC, 1901 Mississippi Ave SE, Washington DC (Ward 8)
providers.hours: Varies by partner
providers.financial_aid: Reduced/no-cost access for east-of-the-river families is the campus's stated purpose; aid mechanics vary by partner organization

reg_2027.opens: Varies by partner — most post summer offerings in early spring
reg_2027.notes: There is no single THEARC registration; check the Building Bridges events calendar and each resident partner (Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington THEARC branch, Washington School for Girls, Washington Ballet @ THEARC, THEARC Farm) individually starting February-March 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Assorted partner camps and events across summer 2026; events calendar active
providers.status_2026: Campus programming ongoing all summer; individual camp availability varies by partner
providers.fit_notes: The single best address to know for affordable enrichment in Ward 8 — treat it as a mall of programs, not one camp. Requires a bit of legwork to match your kid to the right resident partner.

providers.name: TIC Summer Camp (Bethesda)
providers.description: A DC-area institution running half-tech/half-sports days since the 1980s: campers pick one signature technology track — animation, filmmaking, 3D game design, Roblox, Minecraft modding, robotics, programming, or digital photography/design — for half the day and play outdoor sports the other half. The 2026 Maryland site is Sidwell Friends Lower School in Bethesda, with a long-running NoVA campus too.
providers.locations: Sidwell Friends Lower School, Bethesda MD (2026); separate Virginia campus
providers.price_detail: 2026 tuition not verified; sessions sold in two-week increments (historically premium-priced)
providers.hours: Full-day, two-week sessions
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions through summer 2026 at the Bethesda (Sidwell) campus
providers.status_2026: 2026 sessions listed via ticcamp.com and Sidwell Summer; per-session availability not verified — popular tracks fill by spring

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration live on ticcamp.com well before summer; exact date not verified)
reg_2027.notes: Two-week commitment per session and per-track capacity means favorite tech tracks (game design, filmmaking) fill first. Long-time repeat-family camp — new families should register in winter.

providers.fit_notes: The balanced pick for a kid who wants screen-creative time and running-around time in equal halves — parents like that it isn't all-day screens. Two-week sessions are a bigger commitment than weekly camps.

providers.name: Tink (Tinkering at the Cooperative School)
providers.description: The famous nature-based Takoma Park co-op preschool opens to the community each summer with 'Tink,' a five-week tinkering camp where K-5 kids build dens, whittle, use real hammers, saws, and axes, tie knots, build fires, and read maps. It's a rare genuinely risky-play, tools-in-hands program — closer to a European forest school than a typical American day camp.
providers.ages: 5-10 (must be currently enrolled in K-5th grade)
providers.locations: Takoma Park Cooperative Nursery School campus, Takoma Park MD
providers.price_detail: Per-week tuition; no co-oping required for summer — see site for current rates
providers.sessions_2026: Five week-long sessions, roughly late June through July
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; small program that typically fills by spring — check for isolated openings

reg_2027.opens: January (registration for the coming summer 'launches January' per the school)
reg_2027.notes: Very small capacity and a devoted Takoma following — register in January, not spring. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want to build and break things outdoors all day; note it serves elementary ages, NOT the preschoolers the school teaches during the year. Not for kids who dislike dirt, heat, or unstructured time.

providers.name: Tiny Chefs Cooking Camps
providers.description: The DMV's biggest kids' cooking-camp operator, running weekly themed camps (Cupcake Wars, Chopped, All-American Road Trip, Breakfast for Dinner) at sites across DC, Montgomery County, and Northern Virginia — including Union Kitchen in Eckington (DC) and Stone Ridge in Bethesda for 2026. Kids cook real recipes daily in half-day or full-day formats.
providers.ages: roughly 6-12 core (some sites/programs extend younger and to age 13)
providers.locations: Multiple DMV sites; 2026 includes Union Kitchen (Eckington, DC) and Stone Ridge School (Bethesda), plus NoVA school campuses
providers.price_detail: Example 2026 pricing: $450 full-day for the 4-day July 4 week; standard 5-day weeks priced higher; optional bus transportation $50/week at some sites
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day options depending on site
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed sessions June-August 2026 across DMV locations
providers.status_2026: Registration opened Jan 5, 2026 at 10am; popular weeks/sites fill but multi-site capacity means some mid/late-summer openings usually remain — check tinychefs.com

reg_2027.opens: early January (Jan 5 at 10:00am in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Themed weeks at convenient sites (Bethesda, DC) go fastest; the many locations mean a flexible family can usually still find a week later in spring. Book directly at tinychefs.com/summer-camps.

providers.fit_notes: Great for elementary kids who love the kitchen — hands-on, theme-driven, and beginner-safe. It's enrichment cooking, not culinary training; teens serious about cooking should look at SummerCulinary instead.

providers.name: Tudor Place Time Travelers Summer Camp
providers.description: A small history camp at a National Historic Landmark mansion and 5.5-acre garden in Georgetown, where campers try real historians' tools - object study, archaeology digs, and making time capsules - to uncover how everyone from owners to enslaved workers lived at Tudor Place. Intimate scale and a genuine historic site make it feel special.
providers.locations: Tudor Place, 1644 31st St NW, Georgetown
providers.price_detail: Price not verifiable on indexed pages; snacks provided. Registration runs through Blackbaud ticketing.
providers.hours: Day camp (exact hours not verified)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 session(s) listed via washington.org and Tudor Place's Blackbaud registration; exact dates not verified

reg_2027.opens: Typically posted in spring on tudorplace.org
reg_2027.notes: Very small camp at a small institution - if interested, call in late winter and ask when registration posts rather than waiting for marketing.

providers.status_2026: Camp offered in 2026 per event listings; dates, price, and availability could not be verified (site blocked direct fetch) - call 202-965-0400.
providers.fit_notes: Best for kids who like 'detective' history and small groups; narrow 9-11 age band and limited spots. Low confidence: verified only via search-indexed event listings.

providers.name: U.S. Chess Center Summer Chess Day Camps
providers.org: U.S. Chess Center (nonprofit; teaches chess to promote self-confidence and sportsmanship)
providers.description: The venerable DC-area chess nonprofit runs five week-long summer day camps at school sites in Silver Spring and Northern Virginia — chess instruction and play plus recreation, at notably lower prices ($230-280/week) than commercial competitors. Strong culture of sportsmanship; kids are grouped by experience from beginner to tournament level.
providers.ages: Rising grades 2-9 depending on week (Silver Spring week: rising 3-9)
providers.locations: 2026: East Silver Spring ES (Silver Spring MD, week 1); Colvin Run ES Vienna VA (weeks 2-3); Churchill Road ES McLean VA (week 4); Greenbriar West ES Fairfax VA (week 5)
providers.price_detail: $230-$280 per week depending on site; aftercare to 5pm for an added fee
providers.financial_aid: Not published on indexed pages; the org has a access-focused mission — ask admin@chessctr.org
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm, aftercare until 5:00pm for a fee
providers.sessions_2026: Jun 29-Jul 2 Silver Spring; Jul 6-10 and Jul 13-17 Vienna VA; Jul 20-24 McLean VA; Jul 27-31 Fairfax VA
providers.status_2026: Season underway; the three remaining weeks (Jul 13, Jul 20, Jul 27 — all NoVA) were open per their July posts; call to confirm space

reg_2027.opens: Late winter/early spring (2026 camps announced as open by early summer; exact opening month not published)
reg_2027.notes: Register online at register.chessctr.org; first-come, modest caps. The Silver Spring week is the only close-in MD site, so grab it early; NoVA weeks linger longer.

providers.fit_notes: Best-value chess camp in the region and welcoming to beginners; only one week is in Maryland, the rest require a NoVA drive. 9-3 day is shorter than most working parents need without aftercare.

providers.name: U.S. Senate Page Program — Summer Sessions
providers.description: The most storied civics 'summer program' in America: rising juniors and seniors (16-17, 3.0+ GPA) work on the Senate floor delivering correspondence and supporting proceedings, appointed by individual senators and paid on a real federal salary basis (annual rate around $37-38k, prorated). Summer runs as two sessions of 3-4 weeks; unlike the school year, summer pages may live with parents or relatives in the DC area, which makes the summer session unusually accessible for local families. Selection is extremely competitive and runs entirely through your home-state senators — for this guide's readers, that means Maryland's senators (e.g., Van Hollen, Alsobrooks) for MoCo teens.
providers.ages: 16-17 (rising juniors/seniors; 18-year-olds ineligible)
providers.locations: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC; summer pages may live at home with parents/relatives in the area
providers.price_detail: Paid position — stipend based on an annual salary of roughly $37,000-38,500, prorated; health insurance required (FEHB enrollment if uncovered)
providers.financial_aid: n/a — participants are paid federal employees
providers.sessions_2026: Two summer sessions of 3-4 weeks each (June-August, set by the Senate calendar)
providers.status_2026: Closed for summer 2026 — applications were due in February (e.g., Van Hollen's office deadline was Feb 20, 2026); appointees are serving now.

reg_2027.opens: Senator-by-senator, typically December-January for summer; deadlines cluster in early-to-mid February
reg_2027.notes: Apply through BOTH home-state senators to double your odds; requires letter of interest, transcript (3.0+ GPA), recommendations, physician health assessment. MD students apply via Van Hollen and Alsobrooks; DC residents have no senator, which effectively locks them out — a painful quirk worth knowing.

providers.fit_notes: For polished, politically curious 16-17-year-olds who can handle 5am wake-ups and a dress code; it is a job, not a camp. Verified via official pageprogram.senate.gov and senators' office pages (search-indexed).

providers.name: UMD Terp Young Scholars
providers.org: University of Maryland, College Park (Extended Studies)
providers.description: A three-week, 3-college-credit summer program (July 13-31, 2026) where rising 10th-12th graders take a real UMD course — options span STEM, business, public policy and more — with commuter, residential, and online formats. One of the region's best values for earning genuine transcript credit before college, with scholarships available.
providers.ages: 13+, rising 10th grade through graduating seniors, 3.0+ unweighted GPA
providers.locations: UMD College Park campus (just outside the close-in MoCo zone, Metro-accessible via College Park); online option available
providers.hours: Full weekday program during the 3-week session; residential option available
providers.price_detail: Program fee not verified; substantially cheaper per credit than private-university pre-college. Application fee + deposit required.
providers.financial_aid: Yes — dedicated scholarships (see Program Fees and Scholarships page)
providers.status_2026: Application CLOSED for 2026; program runs July 13-31, 2026. (UMD also runs many separate youth sports/STEM camps — see today.umd.edu/2026-umd-summer-camps.)

reg_2027.opens: typically winter (applications reviewed first-come until full)
reg_2027.notes: First-come admission review — apply early in the window with transcript (3.0+ unweighted required). Course sections cap individually, so early applicants get their preferred subject.

providers.sessions_2026: July 13-31, 2026 (one 3-week session)
providers.fit_notes: The best credit-per-dollar pre-college option near DC, and the public-university vibe suits self-starters. It is a real graded college course — the grade follows the student.

providers.name: University of Maryland Youth & Sports Camps (Terp Camps)
providers.description: UMD's College Park campus (20 min from Silver Spring/Takoma) runs a huge slate of summer camps: Terp Quest general day camp for ages 6-12 ($325/week with pool swim, climbing, crafts), plus varsity-run sports camps from girls' soccer day camps ($475 full day) to elite residential camps in soccer, field hockey, gymnastics, and golf, many coached by Terps staff and student-athletes. Big-campus energy and Big Ten facilities at prices below most private DC camps.
providers.ages: 6-12 (Terp Quest); grades 1-12 for sport-specific camps
providers.locations: University of Maryland campus, College Park MD (just outside NE DC / east of Takoma Park)
providers.price_detail: Terp Quest $325/wk; girls soccer day camp $475 full / $264 half; elite overnight camps $725-825
providers.hours: Terp Quest 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM; sports camps typically 9 AM - 3 PM
providers.extended_care: Check per camp; Terp Quest's long day covers most work schedules
providers.sessions_2026: Terp Quest June 22 - July 31; sports camps June-July (e.g., girls soccer June 15-18, June 22-25, July 20-23; boys soccer elite June 27-30, July 10-13)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; July sessions (girls soccer July 20-23, Terp Quest late-July weeks) may still have space — check each program

reg_2027.opens: January-March (each camp opens separately; Terp Quest typically by February)
reg_2027.notes: Decentralized: every sport's coaching staff and Terp Quest register separately — bookmark the UMD summer camps roundup page each January. First-come; Terp Quest weeks fill by late spring.

providers.fit_notes: Best value-for-facilities in the region and an easy reverse-commute from Silver Spring/Takoma; the decentralized registration is confusing at first, and DC-west families face Beltway traffic.

providers.name: Urban Evolution Parkour & Movement Camps
providers.description: The DMV's original alternative-movement gym, teaching parkour, freerunning, aerial acrobatics, and circus skills; its half-day summer camps (9am-1pm) build jumping, balancing, vaulting, and swinging skills and end the week with a Ninja Warrior-style obstacle course. As of summer 2026 operations have consolidated to a Reston, VA facility, so it's now a NoVA option — included because it fills the parkour/ninja gap for families west of the river.
providers.ages: Roughly 6-14 (kids' camps; teen/adult classes separately)
providers.locations: Reston, VA facility (fully moved in by summer 2026); formerly Alexandria/Manassas
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-1:00pm (July 4 week was 4 days in 2026)
providers.price_detail: Weekly pricing not captured in verified sources — book via urbanevo.com/camps
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly half-day camps June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Booking open online for remaining summer weeks as of mid-July 2026 — check the calendar for specific week availability

reg_2027.opens: early spring
reg_2027.notes: Half-day-only format means families usually pair it with something else. Verify the location before booking — the move to Reston is recent and search results still show old addresses.

providers.name: Vacation Bible School (VBS) week camps — newcomer explainer
providers.org: Area churches (Protestant and Catholic parishes across DC/MoCo)
providers.description: Every summer dozens of DC-area churches run Vacation Bible School: a one-week, usually morning (9am-noon) camp of songs, crafts, games, and Bible stories that is free or nearly free and typically open to non-members — the region's biggest hidden source of low-cost camp weeks for ages 3/4 through 5th grade. Verified 2026 examples: National United Methodist (NW DC, June 22-26, ages 4-5th grade, rising 6th+ as junior counselors), Bethesda UMC (ages 3-5th grade), Little Flower & St. Elizabeth Catholic parishes (Bethesda, last full week of June, 100+ kids), People's Congregational UCC (DC, Aug 3-7), and Alfred Street Baptist (Alexandria, free for the whole family).
providers.ages: Typically 3 or 4 through 5th grade; older kids volunteer as junior counselors
providers.locations: Individual churches; aggregated lists at dcmoms.com and washingtonfamily.com each spring
providers.hours: Usually 9:00am-noon for one week; a few run full-day
providers.price_detail: Free to ~$50 materials fee at most churches
providers.sessions_2026: One-week sessions scattered June-August, church by church
providers.status_2026: June sessions done; August sessions (e.g., People's Congregational Aug 3-7) often still taking registrations in July

reg_2027.opens: March-May, church by church
reg_2027.notes: Free spots at popular churches can fill within weeks of posting; expect explicit Christian religious content — this is worship-plus-crafts, not a secular camp with a church landlord. Junior-counselor slots are an easy volunteering gig for tweens (SSL hours sometimes available for MoCo students).

providers.fit_notes: Excellent for budget gap weeks and for preschoolers (many take age 3-4); only for families comfortable with religious programming. Quality and structure vary widely by congregation.

providers.name: Valley Mill Camp
providers.description: A storied family-run day camp and kayaking school on Seneca Creek in Germantown (since 1956), famous for turning out genuinely skilled whitewater kayakers alongside rock climbing, archery, pioneering, drama, and crafts. The traditional camp runs as separate boys' and girls' programs (ages 6-13) plus a coed Junior Camp for 4-5 year olds, and door-to-door bus transportation from stops across MoCo, DC, and NoVA is included in tuition. Many campers return for a decade.
providers.ages: 6-13 core single-gender programs (separate boys' and girls' camps); coed Junior Camp for preschool ages 4-5
providers.locations: 15101 Seneca Rd, Germantown, MD 20874; bus stops across Montgomery County, DC, and Northern Virginia
providers.price_detail: 2026: full season 6/22-8/21 $6,120 for 9 weeks (~$680/week, transportation included); shorter sessions available, e.g. 1st Session 6/22-7/17 $2,720; payment plans available
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm; bus pick-up/drop-off extends the day at both ends
providers.extended_care: No traditional aftercare; included bus service covers transport
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - August 21, in multi-week sessions (e.g. Session 1: 6/22-7/17) or full 9-week season
providers.status_2026: Season underway; mid-season openings unverified — call 301-948-0220 or email frank@valleymill.com.

reg_2027.opens: Rolling/early — enrollment for the following summer is typically open by fall-winter, with spring open houses (2026: March 22, March 29, April 26); exact opening date not published
reg_2027.notes: First-come; returning families roll over heavily and popular age groups waitlist. Newcomers should inquire in early fall, visit an open house, and note the camp prefers longer enrollments (multi-week sessions or full season) over single weeks.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for a kid who might fall in love with kayaking — progression is real and older kids run rivers. Note the traditional program is single-gender (separate boys' and girls' camps), and it's less ideal if you want week-by-week flexibility or a short commitment.

providers.name: VisArts Summer Camps & Teen Intensives
providers.org: VisArts (Visual Arts Center at Rockville Town Square)
providers.description: Rockville's community visual-arts center with professional studios for ceramics, glass, fiber, woodworking, painting, and mixed media. Young Artists (ages 5-6) is a one-week full-day program, Artistic Adventures (ages 7-12) runs as two-week full-day sessions where campers rotate through four studio classes daily, and teens 13-17 take the Teen Advanced Art Institute intensives. The strongest pure visual-arts camp in the Rockville area.
providers.ages: 5-17 (Young Artists 5-6; Artistic Adventures 7-12; Teen Advanced Art Institute 13-17)
providers.locations: 155 Gibbs St, Rockville Town Square, Rockville MD
providers.price_detail: Self-selected tiered pricing published for 2026: $1,150 subsidized / $1,350 true-cost / $1,550 supporter per session (Artistic Adventures sessions are two weeks, roughly $575-775/week at the tiers). Confirm which programs the tiers cover; extended care extra for ages 5-12.
providers.hours: Full-day camps for 5-12 (Artistic Adventures in two-week sessions; classes capped ~15 with instructor plus counselors); teen intensives are half-day blocks
providers.extended_care: Extended Care offered for campers ages 5-12
providers.financial_aid: Need-based scholarships plus a published pay-what-fits tier system ($1,150 subsidized rate for families who need it); scholarship fund grown by the $1,550 supporter tier
providers.sessions_2026: One-week Young Artists weeks and two-week Artistic Adventures/teen sessions across summer 2026 (11 numbered weeks; 6-year-olds folded into Artistic Adventures groupings in weeks 8-11)
providers.status_2026: Camps routinely sell out; 2026 registration opened Jan 1 and popular weeks fill by spring — check site for remaining late-summer sections

reg_2027.opens: January 1 (confirmed: 2026 registration opened Jan 1 — check visartscenter.org in December)
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment is capacity-limited and VisArts itself warns camps routinely sell out — register the week it opens for ceramics and glass weeks. Choose your pricing tier at checkout; separate need-based scholarship applications open early, apply before enrolling if needed.

providers.fit_notes: Excellent for kids who want to work in real clay/glass/fiber studios with professional teaching artists, and for teens ready for focused medium-specific work. Note the two-week session structure for ages 7-12 (a commitment, but deeper work), half-day-only teen intensives, and the need for January planning.

providers.name: Washington Conservatory of Music Summer Camps
providers.description: One-week music camps for ages 4-12 inside the storybook setting of Glen Echo Park (the conservatory's home in the old park Arcade), with some programming in Kensington. Days mix instrument discovery, ensemble play, music-theory games, and end-of-week performances - a gentle, high-quality classical-leaning introduction run by a respected community conservatory.
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park (7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD) and Kensington-area site
providers.price_detail: Most recent verified pricing (2024): $487/week tuition + $15 Glen Echo Park registration fee; 2026 rates not confirmed
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm (per most recent published schedule)
providers.financial_aid: Conservatory offers scholarships for lessons; camp-specific aid not confirmed
providers.sessions_2026: One-week camps from late June through August (2026 week-by-week schedule not fully verifiable via indexed copies)
providers.status_2026: Camps run end of June through August each year; summer 2026 camp-volunteer recruiting via county listings confirms the season is running - midsummer availability plausible; call 301-320-2770 (front desk staffed 10am-6pm Mon-Fri).

reg_2027.opens: With the Glen Echo Park spring/summer catalog, typically February-March
reg_2027.notes: First-come registration through the conservatory/Glen Echo Park systems; smaller profile than the big camps so it fills slower - a good choice for planners and latecomers alike.

providers.fit_notes: Lovely first music camp for young kids (4-12), especially those drawn to piano/strings and a calm, artsy park setting. Older kids and teens will outgrow it; not a performance-blowout rock camp.

providers.name: Washington Nationals Youth Baseball Academy (BASE Program + YBA PLAY)
providers.description: The Nats' flagship youth-development center in Fort Dupont Park (Ward 7) runs a completely free 6-week summer academy blending baseball/softball with academics, meals, field trips, and monthly Nationals Park outings for 'Scholar-Athletes' from Wards 7-8 who commit year-round. The lighter-weight YBA PLAY program offers free twice-weekly instructional clinics and summer league play for ages 5-12 at four sites in Wards 5-8, with all equipment and uniforms provided. This is a community program, not a drop-in camp — families 'pay' with commitment and attendance, and it is one of the best free summer options in the city for east-of-the-river families.
providers.ages: BASE Program: elementary through 8th grade (Wards 7-8); YBA PLAY: ages 5-12 (Wards 4-8 eligible)
providers.locations: Youth Baseball Academy, 3675 Ely Pl SE, Washington DC (Fort Dupont Park, Ward 7); YBA PLAY also at sites in Wards 5-8
providers.price_detail: Free, including uniforms, equipment, and meals; requires year-round commitment (70%+ attendance) for BASE Program
providers.hours: Summer academy weekdays; YBA PLAY clinics Mon/Wed (ages 5-8) and Tue/Thu (ages 9-12)
providers.financial_aid: Program is entirely free — no fees at all
providers.sessions_2026: 6-week summer academy (June-August) for enrolled Scholar-Athletes; YBA PLAY clinics run year-round
providers.status_2026: Summer academy underway for enrolled families; BASE enrollment is year-round cohort-based (not bookable mid-summer). YBA PLAY registration via LeagueApps is periodically open on a rolling basis.

reg_2027.opens: Spring (rolling outreach; BASE cohorts enroll before the school year/summer)
reg_2027.notes: BASE Program requires an application plus a short 20-minute parent interview and a year-round commitment through 8th grade; Ward 7-8 residents only. YBA PLAY is simple free registration on LeagueApps, open to Wards 4-8, no experience needed. Contact the Academy directly — this works like a community program, not first-come camp registration. Official site verified via search-indexed copies (nats4good.org blocked direct fetch).

providers.fit_notes: For DC families east of the river wanting a serious, free, mentorship-heavy summer program — not for families outside Wards 4-8 or those wanting casual one-week baseball camp (for that, the Nationals also license paid Headfirst-run baseball camps across the region).

providers.name: Washington Sailing Marina Summer Sailing School
providers.description: Week-long youth sailing camps on the Potomac at Daingerfield Island, just south of Reagan National along the Mount Vernon Trail — a regional fixture for learning to sail, with tracks from true beginners through racers for ages 8-15, plus big-boat options for older kids. Views of the DC monuments from the water are part of the deal. Registration runs through UltraCamp.
providers.locations: 1 Marina Dr, Alexandria, VA 22314 (Daingerfield Island, GW Parkway)
providers.price_detail: A verified July 2026 session (ages 10-15, big-boat) listed at $695; beginner dinghy weeks typically run lower — check UltraCamp for exact rates
providers.hours: 9:30am-4:00pm Mon-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August
providers.status_2026: Mid-July and August sessions were still listed/bookable on UltraCamp as of this check — a realistic late-2026 option.

reg_2027.opens: Winter (UltraCamp listings typically post by January)
reg_2027.notes: First-come on UltraCamp; fills steadily rather than instantly, and advanced/racing weeks have prerequisites. One of the safer camps to still get in spring.

providers.fit_notes: The regionally famous learn-to-sail option (included as NoVA gap-filler); driving access only, no Metro. Kids must pass a swim requirement.

providers.name: Wheaton Ice Arena Summer Skating Camps
providers.description: Wheaton Regional Park's rink runs a full-day summer skating camp where kids skate twice daily (lesson plus practice) with off-ice games and fitness in between — welcoming absolute beginners with rental skates provided — plus specialty figure-skating camps (freestyle, theater on ice, synchro) and hockey camps for progressing skaters. The east-county sibling to Cabin John with the same county pricing and registration system.
providers.ages: Roughly 6-14 (specialty camps grouped by skating level)
providers.locations: Wheaton Ice Arena, 11717 Orebaugh Ave, Wheaton MD (Wheaton Regional Park)
providers.price_detail: County rates; full-day camp includes two daily skates and rental skates if needed
providers.hours: Full-day camp; specialty camps vary
providers.extended_care: Check current season for extended-care options
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship via Montgomery Parks
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; popular weeks sold out from January registration, occasional openings on ActiveMONTGOMERY

reg_2027.opens: Mid-to-late January (6:30 AM on registration day — the rink's own social posts hype the 6:30 AM opening)
reg_2027.notes: Same ActiveMONTGOMERY 6:30 AM first-come rush as all Montgomery Parks camps, with virtual queue; early-bird discount by early April. Figure-skating specialty weeks (synchro, theater on ice) are unique in the region and fill fast.

providers.fit_notes: The place for east-county kids to fall in love with skating, and the specialty camps serve budding figure skaters well; hockey-track kids may outgrow it toward the Capitals Iceplex programs.

providers.name: Wheaton Park Stables Summer Camp
providers.description: Half-day pony and horsemanship camps at the public riding stable in Wheaton Regional Park, covering riding basics, grooming, and barn care in a low-key, small-barn setting. Camps start the first full week after MCPS lets out and serve a wide age range, with group riding from age 8. It's Montgomery County's most accessible entry point to horses.
providers.ages: Camps roughly 8-18 (riding in group lessons from 8); pony rides for 3-7 on Sundays/Thursdays outside camp
providers.locations: Wheaton Regional Park, 1101 Glenallan Ave, Silver Spring/Wheaton, MD
providers.price_detail: Half-day camps only; 2026 pricing not verifiable via indexed copies
providers.hours: Half-day sessions only
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly half-day camps from mid-June (first full week after MCPS ends)
providers.status_2026: Registration is CLOSED for 2026 — fully booked.

reg_2027.opens: Winter (exact date announced on their site/email; 2026 filled and closed well before summer)
reg_2027.notes: First-come and closes fast once full. Their own advice: if your child already rides there, email BEFORE signups open about placement. Newcomers should email in fall to ask when registration posts.

providers.fit_notes: Half-day only, so it needs pairing with other childcare; ideal affordable-ish first horse experience for MoCo kids. Assume you must plan a season ahead.

providers.name: WISummer — Washington International School Language Camps
providers.description: French and Spanish summer camps for PreK-grade 6 on WIS's storybook Tregaron campus in Cleveland Park, with separate 'immersion' groups (French, Spanish, and Chinese offered) that run entirely in the target language for kids who already speak it, alongside discovery tracks for newer learners. Grades 5-10 can do DC-area and international travel programs instead.
providers.ages: PreK-grade 6 for day camps (roughly 4-12); grades 5-10 for travel programs
providers.locations: WIS Tregaron campus, 3100 Macomb St NW, Cleveland Park, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Weekly pricing not verifiable from indexed official pages; full payment due May 22. Third-party listings suggest mid-$500s/week — confirm at wiscamps.org.
providers.sessions_2026: Roughly six weeks, mid-June through late July (2025 pattern: June 16-July 25); enroll 1-6 weeks
providers.status_2026: Season ends late July — last week or two may have space; camp closes for August

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January) via CampBrain
reg_2027.notes: First-come online; true-immersion groups (especially Chinese and French) have limited seats and fill by spring. Note the season ends in late July — plan August coverage elsewhere.

providers.fit_notes: Immersion groups genuinely require existing language comfort — they move fast and stay in-language; discovery tracks suit beginners. Gorgeous, Metro-ish (Cleveland Park) location.

providers.name: WNO Opera Institute (ON HOLD — not running 2026)
providers.org: Washington National Opera (independent of the Kennedy Center since Jan 2026)
providers.description: Historically a celebrated three-week summer vocal intensive (held at American University) for high school singers exploring opera as a career, with strong financial-assistance commitments. Washington National Opera left the Kennedy Center in January 2026 amid institutional upheaval (covered by NPR), and the official application page now states that planning for the Institute is on hold. Newcomers should not count on this for 2027 without confirmation.
providers.ages: Grades 9-12 (rising and graduating seniors welcome historically)
providers.locations: Historically American University campus, NW DC; future location unknown post-Kennedy Center split
providers.price_detail: Historic tuition not confirmed for future years; WNO explicitly worked with low- and middle-income families on affordability
providers.financial_aid: Historically strong — applicants could request financial assistance in the application
providers.sessions_2026: None — program did not run
providers.status_2026: Not running. Application page (updated Jan 2026) says planning is on hold due to institutional changes; WNO now operates independently and is rebuilding programs.

reg_2027.opens: Unknown — program on hold
reg_2027.notes: Email info@washnatopera.org asking to be notified about career-development programs for high school students. If revived, expect a recorded/live vocal audition and winter application window as in past years.

providers.fit_notes: Entry included so newcomer families stop searching: the famous Kennedy Center teen opera program is paused. Verified via search-indexed copy of the official smapply page plus NPR reporting.

providers.name: Wolf Trap (no children's camp — Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods performances instead)
providers.org: Wolf Trap Foundation for the Performing Arts / National Park Service
providers.description: Wolf Trap, the national park for the performing arts in Vienna, VA, does not run a kids' summer camp — what it offers families is Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods: $8-12 outdoor morning performances (music, puppetry, dance, author-based musicals like Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus) Tuesday-Saturday at 10:30am from late June to mid-August, ideal for ages 4+. For older students, Wolf Trap runs competitive paid summer internships and apprenticeships ($5,000 stipend, application deadline March 1) aimed at college students and recent grads living in the DMV. Regionally famous enough that newcomers assume a camp exists; it does not.
providers.ages: Performances best for 4-10 with adults; internships college-age (18+)
providers.locations: Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods, Wolf Trap National Park, 1551 Trap Rd, Vienna, VA
providers.price_detail: Tickets $8-12 per show; 'Pick 3' saves $2/ticket on 3+ shows; group discounts for 10+. Internships pay a $5,000 summer stipend.
providers.sessions_2026: Performances late June-mid August 2026, Tue-Sat 10:30am; 2026 lineup includes Jules Hoffman and Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus
providers.status_2026: OPEN and bookable right now for July 2026 — individual show tickets on sale at wolftrap.org/woods; a good late-availability outing when camps are full.

reg_2027.opens: Season lineup and tickets announced in spring; internship applications open winter, due March 1
reg_2027.notes: No lottery, no sellout panic — buy show tickets days ahead. College students wanting the internship should apply well before the hard March 1 deadline; DMV residency required during the internship.

providers.fit_notes: A 'no camp, here is what exists' entry. Perfect low-cost culture mornings for preschool/elementary kids; nothing here provides day-long coverage. Verified via official wolftrap.org pages (search-indexed).

providers.name: Writopia Lab DC Summer Writing Camps
providers.org: Writopia Lab (national nonprofit, DC Metro region)
providers.description: The DMV's flagship creative-writing camp: week-long workshops capped at eight kids, led by published writers, in genres from fiction and screenwriting to Dungeons & Dragons storytelling and college essays. Kids who love making up stories — including shy kids who hate 'school writing' — tend to thrive because instructors meet each writer where they are. Half-day workshops (3 hrs) or full-day camps (morning workshop + electives) at the Tenleytown center, with a Falls Church VA site too.
providers.ages: 4-18 (Language Play ages 4-6; age-banded workshops for elementary, middle, high school; college-essay track for teens)
providers.locations: 4000 Albemarle St NW, Suite 308, Tenleytown DC (Metro-accessible); second site in Falls Church, VA
providers.price_detail: National published rate ~$850/wk full-day; half-day weeks cost less. Honor-based sliding scale — pay what you can, no documentation required for the first tier.
providers.financial_aid: Exceptional: honor-based sliding scale plus full need-based scholarships (financialaid@writopialab.org); ~50% of students attend on partial or full scholarship; they state they have never turned a student away over cost.
providers.hours: Half-day 3-hr blocks (AM or PM) or full-day ~9am-4pm, Mon-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions all summer at Tenleytown, e.g. July 13-17 workshop confirmed on third-party listings; runs into August
providers.status_2026: Open/rolling — registering now for remaining July and August weeks; small 8-writer caps mean individual weeks close without notice

reg_2027.opens: Winter (roughly December-January) when the summer catalog posts
reg_2027.notes: Rolling online enrollment; no lottery. Because each workshop caps at 8, popular genre/age combos fill by late spring — book by March for a specific week. Email register@writopialab.org to use the sliding scale.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids who genuinely want to write or tell stories; not a general day camp. The D&D and screenwriting weeks hook reluctant writers. Not a fit for kids who need lots of outdoor/physical time in a full day.

providers.name: WTEF Summer Tennis & Learning Camp
providers.description: The charitable arm of DC tennis (connected to the city's pro tournament legacy) runs summer camps combining tennis instruction, match play, and educational enrichment at its center at 16th & Kennedy St NW, with additional DPR-partnership programming. All levels are welcome, and the mission is access: pricing is modest with generous multi-week discounts, and WTEF's core purpose is serving low-income DC youth. A standout choice for families who want tennis plus academics without country-club prices.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-17 (grouped by age/level)
providers.locations: WTEF Center for Excellence, 1600 Kennedy St NW, Washington DC; some programming at DPR sites
providers.price_detail: Modest weekly fees with multi-week discounts: 15% off 2+ weeks, 20% off 4+, 25% off 8+; contact staff for discount/aid codes
providers.hours: Weekday day camp hours (verify current schedule)
providers.financial_aid: Core mission — need-based assistance and discount codes available; contact Alex Shek or Safeeyah Iverson before registering
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions from June 17, 2026 through summer
providers.status_2026: Camp running; weekly registration via CourtReserve typically remains open for later-summer weeks

reg_2027.opens: Spring (camp pages and CourtReserve events posted by April-May)
reg_2027.notes: Create a CourtReserve account, add WTEF under 'My Organizations,' add campers under 'My Family,' then register per week under Events. Rolling first-come; rarely a sellout crunch. Ask about aid before paying full price.

providers.fit_notes: Great mission-driven, affordable tennis camp for city kids of all levels; families seeking elite training intensity should look at JTCC.

providers.name: Yellow Barn Studio Youth Painting & Drawing
providers.org: Yellow Barn Studio & Gallery (Glen Echo Park resident program)
providers.description: Glen Echo Park's celebrated painting and drawing residency, founded by legendary local art teacher Walt Bartman, known for serious instruction in watercolor, oil, acrylic, pastel, and plein air painting. Summer offerings for young people are studio classes open to beginning and intermediate students 13 and up (teens learn alongside adults), registered through the Glen Echo Park system — not weeklong kids' day camps.
providers.ages: 13-18 for teens (classes are open to students 13+ in a mixed teen/adult setting; no younger-kid camps verified)
providers.locations: Yellow Barn Studio, Glen Echo Park, Glen Echo MD
providers.price_detail: Not verified; priced per class through Glen Echo's ProClass system
providers.hours: Varies by class; summer classes typically meet on a weekly class schedule rather than camp days
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 drawing/watercolor/pastel/oil classes for students 13+ listed within Glen Echo Park's spring/summer catalog
providers.status_2026: Glen Echo registration open and ongoing since Feb 4; per-class availability not individually verified (checked via search-indexed catalog copies)

reg_2027.opens: early February (with Glen Echo Park registration; catalog in January)
reg_2027.notes: Registers through Glen Echo Park's ProClass system. Best known as the feeder studio for serious teen painters (many portfolio-bound students) — but check the catalog carefully: these are 13+ studio classes shared with adults, not drop-off camps.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for a motivated teen (13+) who wants to actually get better at drawing and painting, possibly toward a portfolio. Not a day camp and not for younger children; childcare hours are not the model.

providers.name: YMCA Anthony Bowen Summer Camp
providers.description: Day camp at the historic Anthony Bowen Y on W Street NW (the nation's first African American YMCA, founded 1853), with traditional, sports, aquatics, specialty STEM/arts, travel, and leadership camp tracks and an on-site pool. A genuine neighborhood institution serving U Street/Shaw/Columbia Heights families, walkable and Metro-accessible for car-free households.
providers.ages: roughly 5-12 for core camp; travel and leadership/CIT tracks extend to middle schoolers/teens — verify grade cutoffs by track
providers.locations: YMCA Anthony Bowen, 1325 W St NW, Washington, DC 20009
providers.price_detail: Rates in Day Camp Guide, not search-indexed; deposit per week at registration; financial assistance available
providers.hours: Full-day (verify current-year hours)

reg_2027.opens: typically midwinter (Jan-Feb)
reg_2027.notes: Aquatics weeks (on-site pool, certified instructors, beginner-intermediate levels) are the draw and fill first. Travel and leadership tracks give older kids a ladder. One of the more affordable full-day NW DC options after aid.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly traditional, sports, aquatics, specialty (STEM/arts), travel, and leadership sessions, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; remaining-week availability not verifiable from indexed pages
providers.fit_notes: Strong pick for central-DC families wanting swim-every-day without leaving the neighborhood; the travel/leadership tracks also cover older siblings. Verified via search-indexed copies; call to confirm rates/hours.

providers.name: YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase / Ayrlawn Summer Day Camp
providers.description: Classic YMCA day camp run from the Ayrlawn Program Center campus near NIH, with traditional camp (games, swim, crafts, sports, STEM) plus specialty weeks and a leadership/CIT camp track for older kids. A dependable, affordable-ish default for elementary kids whose families want weekly flexibility rather than a multi-week commitment. Thrives: kids who like variety and being outside; it is not a deep-dive specialty camp.
providers.ages: 5-12 for core camp (~K-6); leadership/CIT camp track for middle schoolers/teens — verify grade cutoffs with branch
providers.locations: YMCA Ayrlawn Program Center / YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase, 9401 Old Georgetown Rd, Bethesda, MD 20814
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates not published on search-indexed pages; per-week deposit required at registration. YMCA financial assistance available (branch-administered).
providers.hours: Full-day; typical Y camp day ~9am-4pm with extended-day options (verify with branch)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care historically offered at Y day camps; confirm hours with Ayr.Camp@ymcadc.org
providers.financial_aid: YMCA DC offers income-based financial assistance; apply through the branch

reg_2027.opens: typically midwinter (Jan-Feb); 2026 forms went live by late winter
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via YMCA 'EZ to Enroll' portal plus branch registration forms
reg_2027.notes: Week-by-week enrollment; popular mid-summer weeks fill by late spring but the Y adds capacity, so it rarely locks out as fast as boutique camps. Early enrollment recommended by the Y itself. Leadership/CIT spots are fewer than traditional-camp spots.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions across summer 2026 (mid-June through late August, typical Y calendar); traditional, sports, specialty STEM, and leadership tracks
providers.status_2026: Season underway as of July 12; weekly enrollment continues where space remains via EZ to Enroll — per-week availability not verifiable from indexed pages
providers.fit_notes: Good value-flexibility default for newcomers in Bethesda/Chevy Chase; not for kids wanting a single-focus specialty camp. ymcadc.org blocks direct fetch — details verified via search-indexed copies.

providers.name: YMCA Camp Letts
providers.description: A 100+-year-old YMCA overnight camp on a peninsula in the Rhode River near Annapolis — the closest true sleepaway camp to DC (about 45 minutes) — with sailing, kayaking, horseback riding, mountain biking, and classic waterfront camp life. Weekly Sunday-Friday sessions let families try just one week, and consecutive-week campers can stay over the weekend for $200. Its YMCA backing means real scholarship money and a less precious, more accessible culture than the private sleepaways.
providers.ages: 7-16 overnight (day camp 6-16)
providers.locations: 4003 Camp Letts Rd, Edgewater MD (~45 min from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates not captured in indexed copies; last verified pricing (2022) was ~$855-905/week overnight — expect around $1,000/wk now. Weekend stay-over $200.
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships available (limited funds, awarded by application, due March 1); YMCA 'payment assistance available'
providers.hours: Residential Sun-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions (Sun-Fri) throughout summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; weekly structure means late-July/August weeks sometimes have space — call to check

reg_2027.opens: typically fall-winter (rolling); scholarship applications due March 1
reg_2027.notes: First-come by week; one-week format makes it the easiest sleepaway trial run in the region. Apply for scholarships well before the March 1 deadline. Verified via ymcadc.org indexed pages; 2026 prices unverified.

providers.fit_notes: Best first sleepaway for a hesitant camper (single weeks, close enough for quick pickup) and for families needing aid at an overnight camp. Facilities are rustic-YMCA, not resort-grade.

providers.name: YMCA Camp Letts Day Camp
providers.description: The day-camp arm of Camp Letts: kids get the same 219-acre Chesapeake waterfront — boating, pool, ropes, farm/nature areas — and go home each night. Mostly serves families toward Annapolis/Edgewater, but it is a real option for East-of-the-Anacostia and Capitol Hill commuters wanting big-outdoors day camp rather than a rec-center program.
providers.ages: roughly 5-14 (verify current-year brackets)
providers.locations: YMCA Camp Letts, 4003 Camp Letts Rd, Edgewater, MD 21037 (~40-50 min from Capitol Hill)
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates not search-indexed; below resident-camp pricing; Y financial assistance applies
providers.hours: Full-day, weekly sessions
providers.extended_care: Extended-day historically offered; confirm with camp office
providers.financial_aid: YMCA financial assistance; camp scholarship deadline March 1 (shared with resident camp)

reg_2027.opens: midwinter with resident camp registration
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via EasyToEnroll, week by week
reg_2027.notes: Far less competitive than the overnight program; a realistic late-planner option. Confirm whether bus/transport options exist from DC in a given year — historically limited.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly day-camp sessions all summer 2026 alongside resident camp
providers.status_2026: Season underway; day camp historically has more late openings than resident weeks — call to check July/August
providers.fit_notes: Only makes sense geographically if the Edgewater drive works for you; in exchange you get waterfront acreage no in-town day camp can match. Verified via indexed copies only.

providers.name: YMCA Camp Tockwogh
providers.description: Storied co-ed overnight camp on 320+ waterfront acres of the upper Chesapeake's Eastern Shore (Worton, MD, ~1.75 hrs from DC), regionally famous for its sailing and waterski/wakeboard programs, plus horses, challenge course, and arts. Sessions run from 3-day mini-camps for the youngest through 1- and 2-week sessions and 4-week teen leadership programs — many DC families treat it as the step-up sailing sleepaway after Camp Letts.
providers.ages: grades K-11 (mini-camps for youngest; 4-week leadership for high schoolers)
providers.locations: YMCA Camp Tockwogh, 24370 Still Pond Neck Rd, Worton, MD 21678 (Kent County, Eastern Shore)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates not search-indexed; full-service private-style YMCA sleepaway, historically $1,000+ per week — verify current rates; Y financial assistance exists
providers.hours: Residential; 3-day mini through 1-2 week and 4-week sessions
providers.financial_aid: YMCA of the Chesapeake financial assistance/scholarships — inquire at 410-348-6000

reg_2027.opens: typically fall 2026 for summer 2027 (returning families first)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online registration; returning-camper priority common
reg_2027.notes: Signature sailing and waterski weeks and the teen leadership programs fill earliest — inquire by September-October. Included here as the regionally famous sailing sleepaway; verify all specifics with camp (site not directly fetchable).

providers.sessions_2026: Sessions across June 2 - Aug 23, 2026 season window, from 3-day mini-camps to 2-week and 4-week leadership sessions
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-summer session availability unverified — call the camp office
providers.fit_notes: The serious-water-sports sleepaway of this sector — best for kids who specifically want to sail or waterski, and for families ready for premium sleepaway pricing. Outside our core geography but a fixture among DC families.

providers.name: YMCA Leadership Camps & Counselor-in-Training (Silver Spring / Bethesda-Chevy Chase)
providers.description: The Y's two-phase teen leadership track: a training week covering child development, group management, and camp skills, followed by placement weeks assisting day-camp staff with 5-12-year-olds — earning about 40 MCPS SSL hours per session (10 for training, 30 for placement). Offered at YMCA Silver Spring and YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase/Ayrlawn Program Center. Thrives: teens who want a structured, supervised first work-like experience with a well-known organization.
providers.ages: Teens ~13-15 (grade requirements vary by branch — verify)
providers.price_detail: Registered as a camp program with weekly fees (rates vary by branch and membership status — not verified; do not assume)
providers.hours: Full camp day
providers.locations: YMCA Silver Spring; YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase / Ayrlawn Program Center (North Bethesda)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly/multi-week sessions through summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Season in progress; YMCA camps often retain late openings — check branch pages for remaining July/August CIT weeks.

reg_2027.opens: roughly January-February with Y summer camp registration (members typically get early access)
reg_2027.notes: Register online per session; member priority window common. CIT status requires completing the training week first, so book training early in the summer.

providers.financial_aid: YMCA offers income-based financial assistance across programs
providers.fit_notes: Less selective and more available than boutique CIT programs — a reliable fallback if ATMTC/Barrie fill. Quality varies by branch and summer staff.

providers.name: YMCA Silver Spring Summer Camp
providers.description: Large multi-track day camp at the Silver Spring Y: traditional camp plus specialty, sports/dance, tennis, aquatics, travel camp for older kids, and leadership/CIT tracks for teens. One of the few close-in camps where a family can place a 6-year-old, an 11-year-old, and a 14-year-old CIT at the same site.
providers.ages: 5-15 (traditional ~K-8; leadership/travel tracks for middle/high schoolers)
providers.locations: YMCA Silver Spring, 9800 Hastings Dr, Silver Spring, MD 20901
providers.price_detail: Rates in branch Day Camp Guide, not search-indexed. Note: all camp registrants must hold a Y community or family membership, which changes effective price.
providers.hours: Full-day with extended-day options (verify in Day Camp Guide)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care historically offered; confirm with branch

reg_2027.opens: typically midwinter (Jan-Feb)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via EZ to Enroll; membership required before camp registration
reg_2027.notes: Aquatics and travel camp weeks are the fastest to fill. Budget for the required community/family membership on top of weekly fees.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions, traditional + specialty + aquatics + travel + leadership tracks, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; week-by-week enrollment continues where space remains (not verified per week)
providers.fit_notes: Best Y option for Silver Spring/Takoma Park families and for teens needing a CIT ladder. Membership requirement is the gotcha newcomers miss. Verified via search-indexed copies only.

providers.name: Young Actors Program (Summer)
providers.description: A three-week summer conservatory for ages 10-17 at the Columbia Heights school that grew out of Studio Theatre's famed conservatory: mornings of voice, movement, and musical theatre; afternoons of ensemble acting with veteran faculty, building original ensemble pieces. Radically accessible — half of all class places are offered on full scholarship, and there is no audition.
providers.locations: Studio Acting Conservatory, Columbia Heights, Washington DC (Holmead Pl NW)
providers.price_detail: Tuition not verified on accessible pages; distinctive policy: one-half of class places offered with FULL scholarship
providers.financial_aid: One-half of places offered on full scholarship — the strongest aid ratio of any theatre camp in the region
providers.hours: Daily, mornings + afternoons for three weeks
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 term June 8-Aug 4; Young Actors summer intensive sessions include June 24-July 12 and July 15-Aug 2; no audition, no prerequisites
providers.status_2026: Summer term underway; Session II (from mid-July) was upcoming as of mid-July — call 202-232-0714 about space

reg_2027.opens: Spring (rolling enrollment once the summer schedule posts; not verified)
reg_2027.notes: No audition ever. Scholarship places (half the class) are the thing to move fast on — inquire as soon as the summer schedule appears.

providers.fit_notes: The hidden gem for families priced out elsewhere and for teens who want real acting technique (not a show-in-a-week). Smaller and less flashy than Imagination Stage or Theatre Lab; no culminating musical spectacle.

providers.name: Young Artists of America: Summer Performing Arts Intensives
providers.org: Young Artists of America at Strathmore
providers.description: The region's premier pre-professional musical-theatre training organization (performances at Strathmore) runs summer intensives for serious young musical-theatre performers, including a two-week residential Junior Conservatory for rising 7th-9th graders living 24/7 at UMD's Clarice Smith Center while mounting a full musical with room, board, and recreation included. Orchestra-backed, mentorship-driven, and audition-caliber.
providers.ages: Middle and high school (Junior Conservatory: rising grades 7-9; other intensives through high school)
providers.locations: Based in Rockville, MD; performances at Strathmore (North Bethesda); 2026 Junior Conservatory in residence at The Clarice, University of Maryland College Park
providers.price_detail: Exact tuition not verified; residential two-week program with full room and board implies premium pricing (balance due June 1, 2026)
providers.financial_aid: YAA historically offers need-based aid; not verified for summer intensives — ask info@yaa.org
providers.hours: Residential 24/7 (Junior Conservatory); day schedules for other intensives
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week Junior Conservatory residency at UMD plus other Summer Performing Arts Intensives; balance due June 1
providers.status_2026: Summer programs typically cast/enroll by late spring; 2026 enrollment effectively closed by July

reg_2027.opens: Winter (applications typically open with the season announcement; not verified)
reg_2027.notes: This is an apply-early, pre-professional pipeline — expect application materials and possible auditions/placement. The overnight Junior Conservatory is the area's only residential musical-theatre option for middle schoolers.

providers.fit_notes: For the kid who already lives for musical theatre and wants conservatory-level training with orchestras and professional venues — overkill for casual performers. Pricing and application mechanics could not be fully verified; confirm directly.

providers.name: Youth Teaching Assistants (YTA) at Smithsonian Summer Camp
providers.description: A structured teen volunteer corps that helps run Smithsonian Summer Camp: YTAs assist instructors in the classroom, lead games, and manage drop-off/pick-up, earning community-service (SSL) hours at a national institution. A one-week YTA Boot Camp trains younger teens (rising 9th graders / age 14+) in camper-wrangling and museum navigation, and confers priority in YTA selection the following summer.
providers.ages: 15+ before the first day of camp for YTA (through graduating seniors); 14+/entering grade 9 for YTA Boot Camp
providers.locations: S. Dillon Ripley Center, National Mall
providers.price_detail: YTA is an unpaid volunteer position (earns service hours). YTA Boot Camp is a separately ticketed one-week program (exact fee not verified on indexed pages)
providers.hours: Camp days ~8:30 am to end of camp day; Boot Camp runs 9:30 am-4:30 pm
providers.sessions_2026: Week-by-week assignments across the June 22-Aug 14 camp season; YTA Boot Camp week June 22-26, 2026

reg_2027.opens: Applications typically open late winter/early spring (watch the YTA page around Feb-March)
reg_2027.notes: Online application (parent/guardian must approve) with age cutoff tied to the first day of camp; selected YTAs attend an orientation. Popular with local high schoolers needing SSL hours, so apply early. Boot Camp is the entry path for 14-year-olds/rising 9th graders and gives priority for next summer's YTA selection.

providers.status_2026: Applications for summer 2026 are closed; the 2026 YTA Boot Camp (June 22-26) sold out.
providers.fit_notes: For teens who want real responsibility with younger kids and museum-world exposure; requires reliability for full camp-day shifts. Verified via search-indexed copies of official YTA and Boot Camp pages.
